## Phase-2 · Contracts + Traceability — screens 358–368 [module M22 + PRD §16.3 — REAL migrated tables + one M18 concept screen, schema gaps noted, designs lead on 6 items]

> **Trace canon (Meera Ben's palak batch — exact 397 reuse verified, do not contradict):** Public no-auth QR scan resolves trace code **KV-TRC-2026-18834**, crop **Palak (પાલક)**, farmer **Meera Ben** (Vanthali, Junagadh, 14 km from scan location). Journey timestamp trail from `trace_events`. Honesty chips: "Pesticide-free / Organic — self-declared" and "FPO member — verified" are explicitly distinguished (declared vs verified, never blended into one unqualified claim). Farmer-pay note: "₹74 of every ₹100 paid goes to farmers" is labeled a general platform figure, not a per-scan receipt. `blockchain_anchor` is modeled in `trace_lots` but unpopulated in Phase 1 — screen renders an honest `anchored=false` state ("Blockchain verification — coming Phase 2"), never overclaimed. EN/हिं/ગુ 3-way language toggle (this screen only — public, no account/device locale to infer from).
>
> **Second trace lot (Ramesh's okra, cross-referenced by 360):** **KV-TRC-2026-19102**, Okra/Bhindi, picked 6 Jul 2026, 12 scans-to-date; batch-link manager also shows past batches Tomatoes (KV-TRC-2026-17960, 27 scans) and Carrots (KV-TRC-2026-17421, 19 scans).
>
> **Contract canon (Ramesh Patel × Mehta Oil Industries, Manavadar — website-canon buyer, do not contradict):** Contract **KV-CON-2026-0231**, `contract_kind=forward`, `price_model=fixed`, crop **GG-20 groundnut (in-shell/pod) મગફળી**, committed quantity **10 qtl**, agreed rate **₹6,200/qtl**, total **₹62,000** (10 × ₹6,200). Negotiation history (illustrative, see schema gap below): opened ₹6,000/qtl → countered ₹6,400/qtl → settled ₹6,150/qtl + oil-content premium → buyer accepted **₹6,200/qtl** (base + **₹100/qtl oil premium** for oil content >48%, per `price_terms` jsonb threshold) — a **₹2,000 gain on the full 10 qtl** vs the opening counter. **MSP 2026 context shown explicitly: ₹6,783/qtl** (contract rate is below MSP — shown honestly, not hidden, alongside mandi range ₹5,800–6,900/qtl for the farmer to judge). Delivery window **October 2026**. E-sign envelope **KV-ESN-88123**, signed 6 Jul 2026, 4:12 PM (IT Act 2000 + Model Contract Farming Act 2018 legal basis cited). Quality spec: moisture max 8%, foreign matter max 1%. Milestones: **sowing_confirm ✓ 18 Jun 2026** (geo-tagged 21.05°N/70.34°E) → **midseason ✓ 5 Jul 2026** (sub-label "crop establishment," jsonb) → **midseason active, due Aug** (sub-label "flowering" — same `midseason` vocab value used twice with different jsonb sub-labels, an honest demonstration of the free-varchar milestone-type design) → preharvest_estimate pending Sep → delivery pending Oct → payment pending (auto-trigger on delivery).
>
> **Past contract (Ramesh, fulfilled — QA-fixed, do not regress the fix):** Contract **KV-CON-2025-0987**, Lokwan wheat, `status=fulfilled`, delivered **12.0 qtl** (quality passed ✓, moisture 7.2% vs ≤8% limit), rate **₹2,450/qtl**, gross **12 × ₹2,450 = ₹29,400**, seed advance recovered **−₹2,400**, **net paid ₹27,000**, delivered 28 Mar 2026, paid 8 Apr 2026, UTR WHTDBT426198035512. Contract beat mandi average (₹2,390/qtl) by ₹60/qtl. **QA FIX APPLIED:** screen 361's past-contract summary line was relabeled to read **"₹27,000 net paid · gross ₹29,400"** to match 368's full breakdown exactly (previously showed only the net figure, which read as inconsistent with 368's itemization) — verified textually identical framing (net-then-gross order) now present on both screens. Note for future batches: 361's card still shows the past contract by crop/date ("Lokwan wheat · Apr 2026 · pre-sowing contract") without inlining the KV-CON-2025-0987 ID — acceptable for a summary card, but a candidate polish if a future batch touches 361 again.
>
> **Modular-farming concept (367, M18, no schema — clearly labeled as such):** Oyster mushroom production unit — ₹8,500 input-advance package, buyback rate ₹120/kg, 90 kg/cycle output, gross value per cycle = 90 × ₹120 = **₹10,800**, ~3 cycles/season. On-screen disclaimer: "New concept — backend in progress. Numbers below are an illustrative plan, not a live offer yet" and "This is not a guaranteed income figure" — never presented as a live, bookable offer.
>
> **Enums referenced:** `farming_contracts.status` (9): draft · proposed · negotiating · signed · active · fulfilled · breached · terminated · disputed. `farming_contracts.contract_kind` (3): pre_sowing · forward · tripartite. `farming_contracts.price_model` (3): fixed · floor_ceiling · formula. `farming_contracts.milestone_type`-equivalent vocab (free varchar, 5 values used, NOT an enum): sowing_confirm · midseason (used twice with distinct jsonb sub-labels) · preharvest_estimate · delivery · payment.
>
> **Design principles carried across all 11 screens:** (1) **radical trace honesty** — declared vs verified claims are always visually and textually distinguished (358's chip row), blockchain anchoring is labeled "coming Phase 2" rather than implied as live (358); (2) **farmer-empowerment negotiation** — 364's copy and framing treat countering an offer as the farmer's right, not a confrontation, and shows the MSP/mandi context so the farmer can judge a fixed-price offer with real information, never in the dark; (3) **transparent settlement math** — 368 shows every line of gross → deduction → net arithmetic inline, never a single opaque number; (4) **gross/net labeling discipline** — enforced consistently after the 361 QA fix, now matching 368 exactly.
>
> ⚠ **SCHEMA GAPS (designs-lead area — 6 items, prominent, backend pending):**
> 1. **No `farm_stories` table.** Screen 359 (farm story editor) is fully mocked UI with no backing schema — needs a new table (farmer bio, practices jsonb, plot/experience fields) before this screen can persist anything.
> 2. **No negotiation-message log.** Screen 364's back-and-forth thread (opening offer → counters → acceptance) has no backing table — `farming_contracts` stores only the final agreed terms; the negotiation history shown is illustrative UI-only state, not derived from real rows. Needs a `contract_negotiation_messages` (or similar) table before this thread can be real.
> 3. **No contract settlement table.** Screen 368 is a reconciliation VIEW computed at render time from 4 existing tables (contract + delivery + quality-check + payment records) — "net paid = gross − recovered advance" is computed here, not stored as a single settlement row anywhere. A dedicated settlement/reconciliation table or materialized view is recommended so this math isn't re-derived ad hoc by every client.
> 4. **No M18 modular-farming schema at all.** Screen 367 is a pure product-concept screen (oyster mushroom package) with zero backing tables — flagged inline on-screen as "backend in progress," not a real bookable flow yet.
> 5. **Trace enrichment fields are app-layer only, not stored in `trace_lots`/`trace_events`.** Farmer display name/photo/village, cold-chain °C readings, km-traveled, and the "₹74 of ₹100" farmer-pay note are all composed at the application layer for screen 358 — `trace_lots`/`trace_events` do not carry these fields directly. Needs either enrichment columns or a documented join contract before this becomes a stable API surface.
> 6. **No FK between `trace_lots` and box orders.** Screen 360's batch-link manager associates a trace lot with a consumer box-order at the application layer only — there is no direct `trace_lots → order` foreign key today. A polymorphic or direct FK is needed before this linkage can be queried structurally rather than assembled per-request.
>
> **SYSTEMIC NOTE flagged for D16 (platform-wide, not specific to this batch):** `.screen-back` is defined at **40px** in `system/screen.css` — below the 44px minimum rural tap-target standard (`--tap-rural`) used everywhere else in the design system. This is a pre-existing platform-wide issue observed while auditing D9's screens (which all use the standard back-header pattern), not a D9-specific defect. Flagged here as a candidate fix for the Sprint D16 final platform audit rather than patched locally in this batch, to avoid a one-off divergent fix ahead of the systemic sweep.
>
> **Backward-canon verification:** Traceability reuses and exactly matches the previously-approved screen 397 (Consumer batch D12) trace-code presentation for KV-TRC-2026-18834/Meera Ben — verified as an exact reuse with zero deviations (see `batch_pack/06-qa-report.md` trace-consistency table). Contract canon (Mehta Oil Industries, Manavadar) matches the buyer identity already established as website-canon. No contradictions found against any prior batch (D1–D8/D12).
>
> **New canon introduced in D9 (downstream batches must not contradict):** Contract KV-CON-2026-0231's complete terms and negotiation arc (₹6,000→6,400→6,150+premium→₹6,200/qtl, ₹62,000 total, MSP ₹6,783 context, e-sign KV-ESN-88123, milestone timeline through Oct 2026 delivery). Past contract KV-CON-2025-0987's fulfilled settlement math (₹29,400 gross − ₹2,400 advance = ₹27,000 net, now consistently labeled on both 361 and 368). Ramesh's second trace lot KV-TRC-2026-19102 (okra) and its batch-link history. The modular-farming mushroom concept package (₹8,500 / ₹120 per kg / 90 kg per cycle). The six schema gaps above and the `.screen-back` 40px systemic note for D16.
>
> Source: PRD §16.3 (traceability — `trace_lots`, `trace_events`, `trace_scan()` RPC) and PRD §25 (contract farming M22 — `farming_contracts` suite), both **REAL migrated tables** including migration 0028, confirmed implemented and used as designed (not schema-scaffolded-only). One exception: **367 is M18 modular-farming, a concept screen with NO backend schema at all**, clearly labeled as such and excluded from the "real tables" claim. ⚠ **Explicitly NOT implemented / pending:** `farm_stories` (gap 1), negotiation-message log (gap 2), settlement table (gap 3, currently a view-over-4-tables), M18 modular-farming schema entirely (gap 4), trace enrichment fields (gap 5, app-layer today), `trace_lots`↔order FK (gap 6).

### 358-public-qr-trace-view
*358 · Public QR Trace View — Krishi-Verse*

- **PUBLIC, no-auth** page (no back-header, no bottom nav — accessible directly via QR scan). `trace_lots` (qr_token, listing_id, farmer_user_id, parcel_id, crop_season_id, declared_inputs jsonb, certificate_ids jsonb, `blockchain_anchor` nullable) + `trace_events` (event_code, meta jsonb) resolve trace code **KV-TRC-2026-18834** — Palak (પાલક), farmer **Meera Ben**, Vanthali, Junagadh, 14 km from scan location. Journey timeline built from `trace_events` rows
- EN/हिं/ગુ 3-way language toggle (exact generalization of approved screen 397). Chips distinguish **declared vs verified**: "Pesticide-free / Organic — self-declared" vs "FPO member — verified." `blockchain_anchor` honestly shown as `anchored=false` ("Blockchain verification — coming Phase 2," never overclaimed). Farmer-pay note "₹74 of every ₹100 paid goes to farmers" labeled a general platform figure, not a per-scan receipt. ⚠ Farmer name/photo/village, cold-chain °C, km-traveled and the ₹74/₹100 note are APP-LAYER ENRICHMENT, not stored directly in trace_lots/trace_events (schema gap 5)
- API: GET /v1/public/trace/:qr_token (calls `trace_scan()` RPC — public security surface, no auth required, rate-limited)

### 359-farmer-farm-story-editor
*359 · Farm Story Editor — Krishi-Verse*

- ⚠ BACKEND PENDING — no `farm_stories` table exists (schema gap 1); screen is fully UI-mocked. Farmer profile: Ramesh Patel, Vanthali Junagadh, 4.7★, 6-bigha plot, 18 years farming, crop okra/bhindi. Practice checkboxes: Drip irrigation ✓, No chemical pesticide ✓, Organic manure unchecked
- Honesty callout: "Only claim what's true — buyers can report a mismatch, and it affects your trust score" (radical trace honesty principle applied to self-reported practices, not just verified certs)
- API: proposed POST /v1/me/trace/farm-story (NOT implemented — needs new `farm_stories` table)

### 360-farmer-batch-link-manager
*360 · Batch Link Manager — Krishi-Verse*

- QR generation is REAL via `trace_lots` (qr_token). Active batch: Okra/Bhindi, picked 6 Jul 2026, code **KV-TRC-2026-19102**, 12 scans to date. Past batches: Tomatoes (KV-TRC-2026-17960, 27 scans), Carrots (KV-TRC-2026-17421, 19 scans)
- ⚠ Box-order linkage is APP-LAYER ONLY — no direct `trace_lots`→order foreign key exists (schema gap 6). Honesty callout: "What you declare shows to every scanner — be exact"
- API: POST /v1/me/trace/lots (creates trace_lots row, generates qr_token) · GET /v1/me/trace/lots/:id/scans — ⚠ order association has no FK, assembled at application layer

### 361-farmer-contracts-home
*361 · Contracts Home — Krishi-Verse*

- Module hub (back-header, bottom-nav deferred to D16 audit). Active contract card: Mehta Oil Industries, Manavadar, "Forward contract," **KV-CON-2026-0231**, badge Active, GG-20 groundnut (in-shell) મગફળી, 10 qtl @ ₹6,200/qtl, total **₹62,000**, "Next milestone: Mid-season check — Aug." Offers teaser: "2 new offers near you"
- Past contract card: Lokwan wheat, fulfilled, Apr 2026 pre-sowing contract — **"₹27,000 net paid · gross ₹29,400"** (QA-FIXED to match 368's breakdown exactly; card omits the KV-CON-2025-0987 ID inline, a minor future-polish candidate, not a QA blocker). Callout: "Price locked before harvest — no mandi-day stress"
- API: GET /v1/me/contracts/home-summary (active contract + past contracts + nearby-offers count)

### 362-farmer-contract-offer-browse
*362 · Browse Contract Offers — Krishi-Verse*

- `farming_contracts` browse, `status=proposed` offers open to this farmer. 3 offers: Mehta Oil Industries (Manavadar, forward, verified buyer, GG-20 groundnut, floor_ceiling ₹6,100–6,400/qtl, delivery Oct 2026, suggested 8–12 qtl); Saurashtra Castor Traders (Rajkot, pre_sowing, castor seed, fixed ₹5,850/qtl, delivery Feb 2027); Gir Oilseeds Pvt Ltd (Junagadh, tripartite, bank-finance included, sesame, floor_ceiling ₹13,200–13,900/qtl, delivery Nov 2026)
- Each card shows its true `contract_kind` and `price_model` enum label — never invented values
- API: GET /v1/me/contracts/offers?crop=&contract_kind=&price_model=

### 363-farmer-contract-detail-terms
*363 · Contract Terms — Krishi-Verse*

- `status=negotiating`, KV-CON-2026-0231. Committed quantity 10 qtl, price_model=fixed ₹6,200/qtl, total contract value ₹62,000 (10 × ₹6,200/qtl). `price_terms` jsonb includes oil-premium threshold (oil content >48% → +₹100/qtl); `quality_spec` jsonb: moisture max 8%, foreign matter max 1%. Legal basis cited: Model Contract Farming Act 2018 template
- Callout: "no penalty for shortfall due to yield" (farmer-protective term surfaced plainly)
- API: GET /v1/me/contracts/:id (full terms + price_terms + quality_spec jsonb)

### 364-farmer-contract-negotiation
*364 · Contract Negotiation — Krishi-Verse*

- ⚠ BACKEND PENDING — no dedicated negotiation-message table (schema gap 2); thread is UI-only illustrative state, not derived from real rows. MSP 2026 context shown explicitly: **₹6,783/qtl**; mandi range ₹5,800–6,900/qtl. Thread arc: opened ₹6,000/qtl → countered ₹6,400/qtl → settled ₹6,150/qtl + oil-content premium → "Mehta Oil accepted ₹6,200/qtl + oil-content premium · 5 Jul, 2:30 PM"
- Agreed total 10 × ₹6,200 = ₹62,000, "a gain of ₹2,000 on the full 10 qtl" vs the opening counter. Farmer-empowerment copy: countering is framed as the farmer's right, not confrontation
- API: proposed POST /v1/me/contracts/:id/negotiate (NOT implemented — needs new negotiation-message log table; current UI reads only the final `farming_contracts` row)

### 365-farmer-contract-esign
*365 · Contract e-Sign — Krishi-Verse*

- `esign_envelope_ref` is REAL: **KV-ESN-88123**. Legal basis: IT Act 2000 + Model Contract Farming Act 2018. Contract ₹62,000, KV-CON-2026-0231, phone ending 4521 for OTP-gated signature. "Signed on 6 Jul 2026, 4:12 PM"
- API: POST /v1/me/contracts/:id/esign (creates esign envelope, stores esign_envelope_ref on farming_contracts, status→signed)

### 366-farmer-contract-milestones-tracker
*366 · Contract Milestones Tracker — Krishi-Verse*

- `milestone_type` is free VARCHAR, not an enum — honest vocab demo: **midseason used twice** with distinct jsonb sub-labels. Timeline: sowing_confirm ✓ 18 Jun 2026 (geo 21.05°N/70.34°E) → midseason ✓ 5 Jul 2026 (sub-label "crop establishment") → midseason **active**, due Aug (sub-label "flowering") → preharvest_estimate pending Sep → delivery pending Oct → payment pending (auto-trigger on delivery)
- API: GET /v1/me/contracts/:id/milestones · POST /v1/me/contracts/:id/milestones/:milestone_id/evidence (photo/geo evidence upload — media asset linkage)

### 367-farmer-modular-farming-plan
*367 · Modular Farming Plan — Krishi-Verse*

- ⚠ BACKEND PENDING — M18 "modular farming" has NO dedicated schema at all (schema gap 4); pure product-concept screen. Oyster mushroom production unit: ₹8,500 input advance, buyback ₹120/kg, 90 kg/cycle, gross value per cycle = 90 × ₹120 = **₹10,800**, ~3 cycles/season
- On-screen disclaimers: "New concept — backend in progress. Numbers below are an illustrative plan, not a live offer yet" and "This is not a guaranteed income figure" — never presented as a live, bookable offer
- API: NONE — concept screen only, no backend exists for M18 today

### 368-farmer-contract-settlement
*368 · Contract Settlement — Krishi-Verse*

- ⚠ NO settlement table exists (schema gap 3) — screen is a reconciliation VIEW computed at render time from 4 tables (contract + delivery + quality-check + payment records); "net paid = gross − recovered advance" computed here, not stored as a single row. KV-CON-2025-0987, Lokwan wheat, status=fulfilled, delivered 12.0 qtl ✓, quality passed ✓ (moisture 7.2%, limit ≤8%), rate ₹2,450/qtl
- Gross **12 × ₹2,450 = ₹29,400**; seed advance recovered **−₹2,400**; **net paid ₹27,000**; delivered 28 Mar 2026, paid 8 Apr 2026, UTR WHTDBT426198035512; "Contract won by ₹60/qtl ✓" vs mandi avg ₹2,390/qtl. This screen's exact figures are the source-of-truth that 361's summary line was QA-fixed to match
- API: GET /v1/me/contracts/:id/settlement (computed view: JOIN farming_contracts + delivery_records + quality_checks + payments — ⚠ no dedicated settlement table, recommend materialized view or new table)

## Phase-2 · Memberships SHG/JLG (M24) — screens 369–381 [module M24 — PRD-numbering note below — REAL core tables (finance_groups/finance_group_members/group_ledger_entries), 4 SCHEMA GAPS + 1 scope decision, designs lead]

> **PRD-numbering note:** the PRD does not use the label "M24" — it files this functionality under **PRD §22.10–22.11** (SHG/JLG group mechanics) and **PRD §29** (group savings/lending), with acceptance criteria at **§40.13** referencing module **M26**. "M24" is an **internal renumbering** used by this design program's module sequence to slot the Groups batch between Contracts+Traceability (M22) and Services marketplace (M25) — it is not a PRD-native module code. Do not search the PRD for a literal "M24" heading; cross-reference §22.10–22.11/§29/§40.13(M26) instead.
>
> **Canon (Vanthali Mahila Bachat Mandal SHG — do not contradict):** Formed **Mar 2024**, Vanthali, Junagadh district, Gujarat. NRLM code **GJ-JND-SHG-04412**. **12 members**, **₹200/member/month = ₹2,400/month group total**. **28 months** of contributions (Mar 2024–Jun 2026) = 28 × ₹2,400 = **₹67,200** contributions + **₹4,800** interest earned = **₹72,000 corpus**, split **₹67,600 in bank + ₹4,400 out on loan** (reconciles: 67,600 + 4,400 = 72,000). `grade` = **tier1** (free varchar, not an enum). Meeting cadence: **monthly, 2nd Sunday** (moved from fortnightly in 2025 as members took up dairy work — their own choice, no negative framing); **12 Jul 2026 verified as a Sunday** (2nd Sunday of July 2026). Officers elected **Feb 2026**, unanimous 12/12 vote, 2-year term (next election Feb 2028): **President Meera Ben Patel** · **Secretary Hansa Ben Chauhan** · **Treasurer Ranjan Ben Vaghela**. **Kanta Ben Rathod's internal loan:** ₹5,000 principal (disbursed 12 Apr 2026, buffalo feed) + ₹500 interest (1%/month flat × 10 months) = **₹5,500 total repayable**, monthly installment **₹550**; 2 installments paid (10 May, 14 Jun) = ₹1,100 paid; **₹5,500 − ₹1,100 = ₹4,400 remaining** (3rd installment due at the 12 Jul 2026 meeting). **Jashoda Ben Parmar's loan request:** ₹8,000 for a tailoring machine, same terms (1%/month flat × 10 months) → ₹800 interest → **₹8,800 total repayable**, **₹880/month**; **approved 11-0-1** (Yes-No-Absent show of hands) at the 12 Jul 2026 meeting, co-signed by President Meera Ben Patel and Treasurer Ranjan Ben Vaghela; first installment due 9 Aug 2026. **Federation: Junagadh Mahila Federation** (shg_federation tenant type) — **34 member groups, 412 members, ₹21,40,000 combined corpus**; Vanthali Mahila Bachat Mandal is a member group since formation.
>
> ⚠ **DISAMBIGUATION (critical — do not conflate these three "Meera Ben"/"Kanta Ben" identities):**
> 1. **Meera Ben Patel** (this SHG's President, wife of farmer **Ramesh Patel** — she fills the previously-unnamed spouse slot referenced on screen **285**) is a **different person** from:
> 2. **"Meera Ben"** — the surname-less palak/spinach trace-canon farmer used on screens **355, 358, 397** (public QR trace, Vanthali, KV-TRC-2026-18834) — common first name only, no relation, no shared surname.
> 3. **Kanta Ben Rathod** (this SHG's member with the active internal loan) is a **different person** from **"Kanta Ben"**, the D12 consumer-app subscriber persona on screen **229** — common first name only, no relation.
> Future batches referencing any "Meera Ben" or "Kanta Ben" must specify the full disambiguating identity (surname + role/context) to avoid canon collision.
>
> **Vocab lists:** `finance_groups.group_kind` CHECK constraint (2, exact): **shg | jlg** — no other values allowed. `finance_group_members.role` (free varchar, comment-vocab, 4 values used): **member | president | secretary | treasurer** — not a DB enum type. `group_ledger_entries.entry_kind` (free varchar, EXACT vocab, 5 values): **saving_deposit | internal_loan | internal_repayment | bank_credit | expense**. `finance_groups.meeting_cadence` — free varchar, default `'weekly'`, this group overrides to `'monthly'`; designs-lead common values offered at creation (370): weekly | fortnightly | monthly, not a DB enum list. `finance_groups.grade` — free varchar bank-linkage rating, NOT an enum; **tier1/tier2/provisional** values shown across 379/380 are designs-lead labels, not a fixed DB list. External JLG group-crop-loan applications ride the existing **D2 loans module** (`loan_applications.group_id` + loan kinds `shg`/`jlg` + collateral `group_guarantee`) — a **deliberate scope decision**, not duplicated in this M24 hub (369's scope-note strip).
>
> ⚠ **SCHEMA GAPS (designs-lead area — 4 items, prominent, backend pending):**
> 1. **No balance/corpus column anywhere in `finance_groups`.** The ₹72,000 corpus (and every "in bank"/"on loan" split) is COMPUTED at render time by summing `group_ledger_entries` rows — never a stored total. Per-member savings-to-date (e.g. Kanta Ben Rathod's ₹5,600 shown on 372) is likewise computed by summing that member's `saving_deposit` rows, not read from a per-member balance column.
> 2. **No loan-request/approval workflow columns.** `group_ledger_entries` has no status, `approved_by`, or `approved_at` column — the "pending group decision" state (375), the 11-0-1 show-of-hands tally (376), and "paid/pending" contribution chips (374) are all DESIGNS-LEAD CONVENTIONS layered over the ledger's free-text `note` field. No table stores a request or a vote tally before the resulting `internal_loan`/`saving_deposit` ledger row is written.
> 3. **NO meeting-minutes table at all.** Screen 377 (group meeting minutes) is **fully designs-lead** — `finance_groups` only stores `meeting_cadence`; `group_ledger_entries` has an optional `meeting_date` but no agenda/attendance/narrative fields. Proposed future migration: a `group_meetings` table (id, group_id, tenant_id, meeting_date, place, attendance_count, agenda jsonb/text, recorded_by_user_id, photo_url, created_at) that `group_ledger_entries.meeting_date` could reference.
> 4. **No election/vote audit table.** `finance_group_members.role` stores only role + joined_at/left_at — no term-start/term-end, no ballot record, no vote log (378). `coop_resolutions` DOES exist in the schema but is scoped to dairy cooperative societies (`coop_id`) and is **NOT foreign-keyed to `finance_groups`** — future wiring should either extend `coop_resolutions` to be polymorphic (society_type + society_id) or add a dedicated `group_resolutions`/election-audit table keyed to `finance_groups`.
> 5. **No group↔federation junction table.** "Federation" (380) = a `tenants` row of `type = shg_federation`; a group's relationship to its federation is implied only by `finance_groups.tenant_id` pointing at the federation's tenant row — conceptual, no dedicated link table, no federation-level aggregate metadata stored (every number on 379/380 is a proposed rollup). NABARD's ₹1,50,000 cash-credit ceiling for tier1 SHGs after 6+ months good standing (379) is **domain copy shown for context, not a database field**.
>
> **Design principles carried across all 13 screens:** (1) **women-led dignity** — savings framed as strength ("Asking your own group is strength" on 375), no-shame catch-up copy for late contributions ("Life happens — catch-up is always OK" on 374), leadership rotation framed positively ("Leadership rotation grows everyone" on 378); (2) **democratic decisions** — the group decides, not one person (376's 11-0-1 show-of-hands, co-signed by President + Treasurer, never a unilateral approval); (3) **radical ledger transparency** — "every rupee visible to every member," the running-corpus strip and every ledger row are shown with full entry_kind/date/note detail (373), and honesty notes explicitly state figures are computed live from the ledger, never a hidden or opaque balance; (4) **flat-interest SHG convention** — Kanta's and Jashoda's loans both use 1%/month flat interest over a fixed term (not a bank-style reducing-balance EMI), consistent with real-world SHG lending practice.
>
> **QA-fix note:** 3 post-audit fixes applied. **372** — member-savings arithmetic corrected: Kanta Ben Rathod's rendered figure on the member list is her **savings-to-date (₹5,600)**, not her loan balance; an earlier draft's incorrect adjacent figure was corrected from **₹4,200 → ₹4,400** to match the authoritative loan-remaining-balance arithmetic on 373/379/371 (₹5,000 principal + ₹500 interest − ₹1,100 paid = ₹4,400) — the two figures (₹5,600 savings vs ₹4,400 loan-remaining) are now unambiguously distinct and consistently cross-referenced, not conflated. **379 and 380** — a hardcoded hex-white literal was replaced with the CSS keyword `white`/`rgba(255,255,255,…)` on decorative gradient/chip backgrounds (`.ga2-bank-section`/`.ga2-grade-chip` on 379; `.tfl-hero`/`.tfl-hero::before`/`.tfl-hero-type` on 380) for token-convention consistency; the underlying brand-gradient hex stops (`#1e6f3f`/`#0e4a28`, `#eef7f1`/`#e2f0e7`) remain as intentional decorative gradients, consistent with the same pattern used elsewhere in the design system (e.g. 369's `.gh2-savings-strip`, 373's `.gsl-strip`).
>
> Source: PRD §22.10–22.11 (SHG/JLG group mechanics) + §29 (group savings/lending) + acceptance §40.13/M26, cross-referenced against `Database_Architecture/10_fintech_schemes.sql` (`finance_groups`, `finance_group_members`, `group_ledger_entries` — PARTITIONED — all **REAL migrated tables**, confirmed implemented and used as designed for the core savings/membership surface). ⚠ **Explicitly NOT implemented / pending:** balance/corpus column (gap 1), loan-request/approval workflow columns (gap 2), `group_meetings` table (gap 3, screen 377 fully designs-lead), election/vote audit table (gap 4, `coop_resolutions` not FK'd to `finance_groups`), group↔federation junction (gap 5, conceptual via `shg_federation` tenant type only). External JLG group-loan applications intentionally reuse the existing D2 loans module (`loan_applications.group_id`, loan kinds `shg`/`jlg`, collateral `group_guarantee`) — a scope decision, not a gap.

### 369-farmer-groups-home
*369 · Groups Home — Krishi-Verse*

- Module hub (FARMER, Meera Ben Patel, President view). `finance_groups` (group_kind, nrlm_code, bank_account_id, meeting_cadence, grade) + `finance_group_members` (role, joined_at/left_at) + `group_ledger_entries` (PARTITIONED; entry_kind). Vanthali Mahila Bachat Mandal, NRLM GJ-JND-SHG-04412, 12 members, formed Mar 2024, tier1 grade. Corpus **₹72,000** (⚠ computed from ledger, no balance column — schema gap 1). Viewer's own savings "₹5,600" (28 × ₹200)
- Next meeting "Sun, 12 Jul 2026 — 5 days away" (verified Sunday). Agenda teasers: "Jashoda Ben's loan request," "Monthly contributions." Scope-note strip: JLG group-crop-loan applications intentionally live in the existing D2 loans module, not duplicated here — a deliberate scope decision
- API: proposed GET /v1/me/groups/home-summary (group summary + own savings + next-meeting agenda)

### 370-farmer-create-group
*370 · Create Group — Krishi-Verse*

- Single scrollable creation form (FARMER). `finance_groups.group_kind` CHECK constraint allows ONLY 'shg' or 'jlg' — the two radio chips map 1:1, nothing else valid. `nrlm_code` optional text (no DB format enum — state-scheme convention only). `meeting_cadence` free varchar, default 'weekly'; form offers weekly/fortnightly/monthly as designs-lead common values, not a DB enum list. `bank_account_id` nullable FK — linking a bank account modeled as a follow-up step after creation (no screen number reserved for it in this batch)
- `finance_group_members` rows are created only once each invited phone number accepts — this screen only captures phone numbers to invite. PRD-sourced membership-size guidance (design copy only): SHG typically 10–20 members, JLG typically 4–10 members
- API: proposed POST /v1/me/groups (creates finance_groups row) · invited members create finance_group_members rows only on accept — NOT a single-transaction bulk-invite endpoint

### 371-farmer-group-detail
*371 · Group Detail — Krishi-Verse*

- Group profile (FARMER, Meera Ben Patel, President view). NRLM GJ-JND-SHG-04412, formed Mar 2024, 12 members, bank account linked. Money position: "In bank ₹67,600" + "On loan ₹4,400" = **₹72,000** (reconciles exactly with corpus). ⚠ Schema gap 1 (no balance column, computed from ledger) and schema gap 2-equivalent (no loan-status column — money-position figures derived by summing ledger entries, not read from a status field)
- Officers: President Meera Ben Patel (elected Feb 2026, 2-year term — designs-lead convention, no election/term column exists), Secretary Hansa Ben Chauhan, Treasurer Ranjan Ben Vaghela. Meeting "Monthly, 2nd Sunday" — moved from fortnightly in 2025 (members' own choice, no negative framing)
- API: proposed GET /v1/me/groups/:id (full profile: finance_groups row + computed money-position rollup + officers via finance_group_members)

### 372-farmer-member-management
*372 · Member Management — Krishi-Verse*

- Full 12-member roster + 1 past member (FARMER, Meera Ben Patel, President view). `finance_group_members.role` free varchar (member|president|secretary|treasurer, not a DB enum); per-member savings-to-date COMPUTED by summing that member's saving_deposit rows — no per-member balance column exists. "KYC ✓" and consent chips are UI-only conventions, no dedicated columns
- Officers + Kanta Ben Rathod ("KYC ✓," "loan active" chips, **₹5,600 saved** — her savings-to-date, distinct from her separately-tracked ₹4,400 loan-remaining-balance shown on 371/373/379) + Jashoda Ben Parmar ("request pending") + 6 more Mar-2024 founding members at ₹5,600 each + 2 later joiners (Vina Ben Gohil ₹4,400 saved since Sep 2024; Nita Ben Bavarva ₹3,600 saved since Jan 2025) at proportionally lower savings. Past member Radha Ben Prajapati — "left Mar 2026 — savings returned ✓" (left_at populated, kept visible, never hard-deleted). **QA FIX APPLIED:** an adjacent member-savings figure was corrected ₹4,200→₹4,400 to remove ambiguity with the authoritative loan-remaining-balance arithmetic (₹5,000+₹500−₹1,100=₹4,400) documented on 373/379/371 — Kanta's own rendered figure on this screen is her ₹5,600 savings-to-date, never conflated with her loan balance
- API: proposed GET /v1/me/groups/:id/members (roster incl. left members) · POST /v1/me/groups/:id/members (invite by phone)

### 373-farmer-group-savings-ledger
*373 · Group Savings Ledger — Krishi-Verse*

- Full ledger view (FARMER, Meera Ben Patel, President view). `group_ledger_entries` PARTITIONED flat ledger, entry_kind EXACT vocab (5 values), columns amount_minor/meeting_date/note, NO status/approval columns. Running corpus strip "₹72,000" (⚠ computed, no balance column — schema gap 1)
- Authoritative loan-math source: **Kanta Ben Rathod's loan — ₹5,000 principal (12 Apr 2026 internal_loan, "buffalo feed") + ₹500 interest (1%/month flat × 10 months) = ₹5,500 total; 2 × ₹550 internal_repayment (10 May, 14 Jun) = ₹1,100 paid; ₹5,500 − ₹1,100 = ₹4,400 remaining.** Monthly reconciliation shown: Jun/May/Apr 2026 each show 12 × ₹200 = ₹2,400 saving_deposit rows plus Kanta's repayment/disbursal rows
- API: proposed GET /v1/me/groups/:id/ledger?month= (paginated group_ledger_entries feed, filterable by entry_kind)

### 374-farmer-contribution-entry
*374 · Contribution Entry — Krishi-Verse*

- Treasurer/President action screen, dated to the 14 Jun 2026 meeting (FARMER, Meera Ben Patel). "Save" writes one `group_ledger_entries` row per paying member (entry_kind='saving_deposit', amount_minor=20000, meeting_date='2026-06-14') — no separate "contribution batch" table exists, each member's ₹200 is its own row. ⚠ No pending/paid status column — a member with no saving_deposit row for this meeting_date is simply absent from the ledger; "will pay next meeting" is a UI-only convention
- 11 of 12 members "Paid ✓" at ₹200 each; Nita Ben Bavarva "Will pay next meeting." Totals: Collected ₹2,200 + Pending ₹200 = Expected ₹2,400 (verified). No-shame copy: "Life happens — catch-up is always OK"
- API: proposed POST /v1/me/groups/:id/ledger/contributions (bulk-writes one saving_deposit row per paying member for a given meeting_date)

### 375-farmer-internal-lending-request
*375 · Internal Lending Request — Krishi-Verse*

- Submission screen (FARMER, Jashoda Ben Parmar, member). ⚠ NO status column anywhere in `group_ledger_entries`/`finance_groups` (no pending/approved/declined enum, no approved_by/approved_at) — the "pending group decision" state is a DESIGNS-LEAD CONVENTION layered on the ledger's free-text `note` field; the request exists only as a UI state until the meeting, when (if approved) a single internal_loan row is written
- Requests ₹8,000 for a tailoring machine. Terms preview: 1%/month flat × 10 months → interest ₹800 → total repayable ₹8,800 → monthly installment ₹880. Her savings-to-date ₹5,600 (28 × ₹200, same tenure as founding members). Context: "₹8,000 of ₹67,600 in bank." Dignity copy: "Asking your own group is strength"
- API: proposed POST /v1/me/groups/:id/loan-requests (⚠ NOT backed by a dedicated table — proposal only, becomes a real row only as a future internal_loan ledger entry if approved)

### 376-farmer-internal-loan-approval
*376 · Internal Loan Approval — Krishi-Verse*

- Meeting screen, 12 Jul 2026 (FARMER, group member view). ⚠ NO approval-workflow columns on `group_ledger_entries` (no status/approved_by/approved_at) and NO election/vote-audit table — "approval" is a DESIGN CONVENTION: a show-of-hands count recorded at the meeting, followed by an authorized internal_loan ledger entry; no DB table stores the tally itself
- Jashoda Ben Parmar's ₹8,000 request: ₹880 × 10 months, ₹800 interest, ₹8,800 total. **Approved 11-0-1** (Yes-No-Absent), co-signed by President Meera Ben Patel + Treasurer Ranjan Ben Vaghela. Comfort strip: "Group corpus is ₹72,000 — after Jashoda's ₹8,000, ₹64,000 stays with the group." Disbursed ₹8,000; first installment ₹880 due 9 Aug 2026. Democratic-decision copy: group vote, never a unilateral approval
- API: proposed POST /v1/me/groups/:id/loan-requests/:id/approve (writes internal_loan ledger row with note recording the vote tally; ⚠ vote tally itself not separately persisted)

### 377-farmer-group-meeting-minutes
*377 · Group Meeting Minutes — Krishi-Verse*

- ⚠ FULLY DESIGNS-LEAD SCREEN — NO meeting-minutes table exists in the current schema (schema gap 3). `finance_groups` only stores meeting_cadence; `group_ledger_entries` has an optional meeting_date but no agenda/attendance/narrative fields. Attendance, agenda, discussion notes, photo, and secretary sign-off are all DESIGN CONVENTION. **PROPOSED FUTURE MIGRATION: `group_meetings` table** (id, group_id, tenant_id, meeting_date, place, attendance_count, agenda jsonb/text, recorded_by_user_id, photo_url, created_at) that `group_ledger_entries.meeting_date` could reference
- 14 Jun 2026 meeting, 11/12 attendance, Meera Ben's courtyard. Money figures shown DO map to real ledger rows (11 × ₹200 = ₹2,200 contributions; Kanta's ₹550 installment 2 of 10) — only the meeting "wrapper" is speculative. Recorded by Secretary Hansa Ben Chauhan. Next agenda: Jashoda Ben's ₹8,000 request
- API: NONE — proposed POST /v1/me/groups/:id/meetings (NOT implemented, needs new group_meetings table)

### 378-farmer-group-elections-roles
*378 · Group Elections & Roles — Krishi-Verse*

- ⚠ NO election audit table (schema gap 4) — no vote log, no term-start/term-end columns, no ballot records anywhere in the schema; every term date, vote tally, and "since" chip is DESIGNS-LEAD. `coop_resolutions` DOES exist but is scoped to dairy cooperative societies (coop_id) and is NOT foreign-keyed to `finance_groups`
- Officers: President Meera Ben Patel (since Feb 2026), Secretary Hansa Ben Chauhan, Treasurer Ranjan Ben Vaghela. "Last election: February 2026 — unanimous," 12/12 voted, 12/12 for Meera Ben Patel. Next election Feb 2028. Footer note: updates `finance_group_members.role` — no separate ballot record exists yet, the meeting itself is the record. Dignity copy: "Leadership rotation grows everyone"
- API: proposed PATCH /v1/me/groups/:id/members/:member_id/role (updates finance_group_members.role only; ⚠ no audit trail persisted)

### 379-farmer-group-analytics
*379 · Group Health Analytics — Krishi-Verse*

- Corpus-growth chart + health indicators (FARMER). `group_ledger_entries` is the source for all figures — corpus growth is a running sum, no separate analytics/snapshot table. Chart: Year 1 end (Dec 2024) ₹24,000 → Year 2 end (Dec 2025) ₹57,600 → Now (Jul 2026) **₹72,000** (of which ₹67,600 bank + ₹4,400 on Kanta Ben's active loan). "100% repayment rate — 0 missed in 28 months." Savings per member ₹5,600 (₹200 × 28 months, same for all 12 members)
- NABARD ₹1,50,000 cash-credit ceiling for tier1 SHGs after 6+ months good standing — explicitly labeled **domain guidance, not a database field**. Federation row: "Junagadh Mahila Federation — member group since formation" (⚠ no junction table, schema gap 5). **QA FIX APPLIED:** hardcoded hex-white literal replaced with CSS keyword `white` on `.ga2-grade-chip` (tier1 chip text) for token-convention consistency; `.ga2-bank-section`'s decorative pale-green hex gradient (`#eef7f1`/`#e2f0e7`) retained intentionally
- API: proposed GET /v1/me/groups/:id/analytics (computed rollup: corpus-growth series + repayment rate + per-member savings, no stored snapshot)

### 380-tenant-federation-link
*380 · Federation Link — Krishi-Verse [TENANT — English-only]*

- Federation console (TENANT admin, Junagadh Mahila Federation; English-only per tenant convention, no bilingual spans). CONCEPTUAL DISCLOSURE: "Federation" = a `tenants` row with `type = shg_federation`. NO junction table links member SHGs to a federation tenant — relationship implied only by `finance_groups.tenant_id` pointing at the federation's tenant row (schema gap 5). No federation-level metadata (member count, aggregate corpus) is stored or computed — every number shown is a rollup this screen proposes, not a stored fact
- Junagadh Mahila Federation: **34 groups, 412 members, ₹21,40,000 combined corpus**. Member rows incl. Vanthali Mahila Bachat Mandal (12 members, tier1, ₹72,000, 100% repayment), Keshod Pragati Mahila Mandal (14 members, tier1, ₹94,500, 98% repayment), Mangrol Sakhi Mandal (10 members, tier2, ₹41,200, 100% repayment), Bantva New Beginnings SHG (8 members, provisional, ₹18,600, no loans yet). NRLM verification checks nrlm_code against state SHG records — no verification-status column exists, so "verified" badges are a design convention. **QA FIX APPLIED:** hardcoded hex-white literal replaced with CSS keyword `white`/`rgba(255,255,255,…)` on `.tfl-hero`/`.tfl-hero::before`/`.tfl-hero-type` (hero banner + tenant-type chip) for token-convention consistency; the brand hex gradient (`#1e6f3f`/`#0e4a28`) on `.tfl-hero` retained intentionally
- API: proposed GET /v1/tenant/federation/:id/groups (rollup of member finance_groups rows — ⚠ no junction table, assembled via tenant_id match only)

### 381-farmer-group-alerts
*381 · Group Alerts — Krishi-Verse*

- Notification feed (FARMER, Vanthali Mahila Bachat Mandal member view). Summarizes real `group_ledger_entries` plus designs-lead conventions for meeting reminders (no group_meetings table — see 377), grade renewal (finance_groups.grade free varchar), and federation invitations (no junction table — see 380). No dedicated notifications table assumed beyond the shared platform convention; representative design state, not a literal DB dump
- Today: meeting reminder (Sun 12 Jul, 5 PM, Meera Ben's courtyard) + Jashoda Ben's ₹8,000 agenda item. Earlier: Kanta Ben's ₹550 installment-due reminder (3 of 10), contribution-recorded confirmation (₹200 ✓, running total ₹800 this year), grade-renewed notice ("still tier1!"), Junagadh Mahila Federation dairy-training invite (18 Jul)
- API: proposed GET /v1/me/notifications?module=groups (reuses shared platform notification convention, no module-specific table)

## Phase-2 · Services marketplace — screens 382–391 [module label "M25 services marketplace" — PRD-numbering caveat below — REAL core tables (service_offerings/service_bookings), 1 proposed dispatch-grade status extension + several proposed columns, designs lead]

> **PRD-numbering caveat:** this design program's internal module label **"M25 services marketplace"** is **not** the PRD's own module M25 — the PRD's own **M25 is drones** (§28, DGCA-regulated aerial spraying), an entirely different, explicitly **excluded** module (see scope lines below). This batch's actual functionality grounds in **PRD §23.5** (repair services) plus the PRD's generic service-marketplace tables and acceptance language. Do not search the PRD for "M25" expecting this batch's content — cross-reference §23.5 instead.
>
> **Canon (booking KV-SVC-2026-0871 — do not contradict):** **7 Jul 2026.** Ramesh Patel's (Vanthali, Junagadh) borewell pump fails mid-Kharif groundnut irrigation. He books **Dinesh Solanki**, pump & motor mechanic in **Keshod** (★4.7, 214 jobs, KYC-verified, emergency-available, visit fee ₹300/visit). Urgency marked **URGENT** (crop needs water) — the farmer, not the provider, judges the emergency-vs-urgent threshold. **Dispatch timeline (proposed 8-state, exact times): requested 8:10 AM → accepted 8:16 AM → en_route (arriving ~9:40 AM) → quoted → in_progress → completed 11:20 AM.** **Quote math (exact, verified): Capacitor (part) ₹450 + Labour ₹300 = ₹750 total.** The visit fee ₹300 is **waived and adjusted against the final bill** — shown transparently as a struck-through **"₹0, was ₹300"** line, never silently dropped. **The farmer pays ₹750 total, never ₹1,050** (which would double-charge the visit fee). Paid via UPI, **UTR 402719558124**. Both sides rated each other **5★** — Dinesh's note on Ramesh: "clear problem description, prompt payment."
>
> **Dinesh Solanki's provider side:** Keshod, 12 km service radius covering Vanthali/Junagadh outskirts. Services: pump repair ₹300/visit (waived on repair), motor rewinding ₹1,200 fixed, borewell check ₹500/visit. **July 2026 earnings: 11 jobs, ₹4,850** — 6 visit-only × ₹300 = ₹1,800, 3 small repairs ≈₹500 = ₹1,500, 1 medium job = ₹800, plus today's ₹750 = ₹4,850. **YTD ₹28,400** (illustrative season total). Job queue at snapshot: 1 completed today (Ramesh Patel, ₹750, 5★ received), 2 pending — **Meena Ben** (Keshod, borewell check, URGENT, requested 12:40 PM — ⚠ a deliberately distinct spelling/identity from "Meera Ben Patel" (D10 SHG President) and the surname-less trace-canon "Meera Ben" (screens 355/358/397); no conflation) and **Suresh Bhai** (Vanthali, motor rewinding, tomorrow AM, already accepted — common name, no cross-batch identity link).
>
> **Arjun Rathod becomes a provider:** existing **D7 canon character exactly** (worker, ★4.3, 6 jobs, Junagadh) adds a new service offering "Knapsack spraying," **₹150/acre**, `pricing_model='per_unit'` unit='acre', 8 km radius. Because `skills.code='spraying'` has `is_hazardous=true` (real column, `07_labour.sql`), the listing flow requires a **PPE declaration** (mask/gloves/boots checklist + safe-handling pledge) specifically for this listing. His **existing worker KYC is reused** — no re-upload, per the platform rule that KYC is identity-level, not listing-level.
>
> **Vocab lists:** `service_offerings.pricing_model` CHECK constraint (6, exact): **per_hour | per_day | per_unit | per_person | per_visit | fixed**. `service_offerings.status` CHECK constraint (4, exact): **draft | published | paused | archived**. `service_bookings.status` (REAL varchar CHECK, 6, exact): **requested | confirmed | in_progress | completed | cancelled | disputed**. ⚠ **PROPOSED dispatch-grade extension (8, not yet in schema, mirrors `vet_bookings`): requested | accepted | en_route | in_progress | quoted | completed | cancelled | no_show.**
>
> ⚠ **SCHEMA NOTES (designs-lead area — 1 proposed extension + several proposed columns, prominent, backend pending):**
> 1. **Dispatch-grade status enum layered over the REAL narrower enum.** `service_bookings.status` in the database today only supports `requested|confirmed|in_progress|completed|cancelled|disputed`. The live-tracking timeline (386) and job-queue dispatch states (389) use a **proposed 8-value extension** (`accepted`, `en_route`, `quoted`, `no_show` are NOT real values) mirroring the existing `vet_bookings` dispatch pattern (screens 206/207) — flagged for backend review on every screen that uses it.
> 2. **Proposed columns on `service_bookings`:** `urgency` (`emergency`|`urgent`|`routine`), `problem_description` text, `problem_photo_media_id`, `quoted_items` jsonb (the itemized quote, e.g. `[{item:"Capacitor",amount_minor:45000},{item:"Labour",amount_minor:30000}]`). None of these are real columns yet.
> 3. **Proposed provider-profile columns:** `rating_avg`, jobs-completed count, emergency-available flag, work gallery — none are real columns on `service_offerings` yet, though the underlying reviews are real (shared `reviews` table, used identically to worker ratings on screen 342).
> 4. **Service category rows PENDING SEED.** The "services" branch of the shared categories tree needs seeding — category names shown throughout (Pump & motor mechanic, Equipment mechanic, Electrician, Plumber, Spraying, Soil testing, Vet) are design-lead names, not yet backed by seeded `category_id` rows.
> 5. **No notification/event table for `service_bookings` status transitions.** Screen 391 proposes the trigger set (booking accepted, en_route, quote ready, work completed, rating received, new booking request) — none of these are wired to a live backend event bus yet.
>
> **Scope lines (explicit exclusions, not gaps):** (1) **Drone spraying is EXCLUDED** — the PRD's own module M25 (§28) is DGCA-regulated aerial spraying, a separately-regulated module; this batch's "Spraying" category tile is explicitly knapsack/manual only. (2) **Soil-testing tile deep-links to a future screen (410, batch D13)** — its booking flow is NOT built in this batch. (3) **Vet tile deep-links to the existing screen 205** (D4 livestock/vet module) — no duplicate vet-booking flow is built here.
>
> **Design principles carried across all 10 screens:** (1) **help-comes-to-the-farm calm urgency** — "Something broken? Help comes to your farm" (382), "Dinesh is on his way — help is on the way" (386), urgency acknowledged without panic, farmer (not the app) judges the emergency-vs-urgent threshold; (2) **quote-before-work consent** — "Work starts only after you say yes" (386), a non-negotiable UX gate, never assumed from a UI click alone; (3) **visit-fee-waived transparency** — every screen states explicitly, never implies, that the visit fee is charged only if no repair happens and is waived/credited against the final bill if repair proceeds, always shown as a struck-through "₹0, was ₹300" line, never silently dropped or double-charged; (4) **providers-as-professionals dignity** — clear earnings breakdowns, tax-time-friendly settlement records, rating-driven browse priority, professional-livelihood framing ("Your labour record speaks for you," 388; "Repairs earn more — quote fairly, earn steadily," 390); (5) **new-provider fair chance** — "New provider — give a fair chance" (383), "everyone gets fair visibility while they earn their first ratings" (388), visibility not solely rating-gated.
>
> **QA-fix note:** 1 post-audit fix applied. **386** — the live quote card's visit-fee line initially read "waived on repair: ₹0" without the "was ₹300" comparison already present on 387's receipt; corrected to explicitly show the struck-through comparison **"₹0, was ₹300"**, matching 387 exactly, so the waiver is transparent at the moment of quote approval, not only after the fact. The ₹750 total itself was correct throughout, both before and after this fix — this was a transparency/consistency fix, not an arithmetic-error fix.
>
> **Deferred nits (flagged for D16, not fixed in this batch):** (1) `kv-btn-sm` renders at 36px, below the platform's 44px minimum tap target — a pre-existing systemic issue across multiple prior batches, used on secondary CTAs throughout this batch (e.g. 383's Profile/Book buttons); (2) bilingual wrapper-class inconsistency on the outer `.screen` div across screens 387–391 — content translations are complete and correct, only the `bilingual` CSS class marker on the wrapper is inconsistently applied.
>
> Source: PRD §23.5 (repair services) + generic service-marketplace tables/acceptance language, cross-referenced against `Database_Architecture/14_audit_additions.sql` (migration 0015 — `service_offerings`, `service_bookings` — confirmed **REAL migrated tables**, used as designed for the core listing/booking surface). ⚠ **Explicitly NOT implemented / pending:** dispatch-grade status enum + supporting columns (urgency, problem_description, problem_photo_media_id, quoted_items jsonb), provider rating_avg + gallery columns, service category seed rows, notification/event table for service_bookings status transitions. Drone spraying — a **different, excluded** module — PRD §28 (the PRD's own "M25").

### 382-farmer-services-home
*382 · Services Home — Krishi-Verse*

- Module hub (FARMER, Ramesh Patel view). `service_offerings` (category_id — pending seed, pricing_model, status) + `service_bookings` (booking_no, starts_at, total_minor, status). Active-booking strip shows KV-SVC-2026-0871 at `en_route` (⚠ proposed status value) — Dinesh Solanki, Keshod
- 7 category tiles: Pump & motor mechanic, Equipment mechanic, Electrician, Plumber, Spraying (knapsack — drone spraying explicitly excluded, DGCA module §28), Soil testing ("Coming soon" — deep-links to future screen 410/D13, booking flow not built here), Vet ("Opens vet directory" — deep-links to existing screen 205, no duplicate flow). "Have a skill? Earn with it" teaser links to 388. Recent services: Dinesh Solanki (en_route), Ismail Bhai (completed), Ravi Parmar (completed)
- API: proposed GET /v1/me/services/home-summary (category tiles + active booking + recent bookings, single round-trip)

### 383-farmer-service-category-browse
*383 · Pump & Motor Mechanics — Krishi-Verse*

- Category browse list (FARMER, near Vanthali). 3 providers, `service_offerings` REAL rows (pricing_model='per_visit', status='published'): **Dinesh Solanki** (Keshod, 6 km, ⭐4.7/214 jobs, KYC-verified, emergency-available, ₹300/visit, waive-chip) — shown first, later booked. Ismail Bhai (Junagadh, 11 km, ⭐4.5/156 jobs, ₹250/visit). Ravi Parmar (Vanthali, 1 km, ⭐4.2/38 jobs, "New provider — give a fair chance" chip, ₹250/visit)
- ⚠ rating_avg, jobs-completed count, emergency-available flag are PROPOSED provider-profile columns — not yet real; reviews aggregate from the shared (real) reviews table once seeded. Filter chips: Distance (active), Emergency now, Rating 4+. Every card shows the visit-fee-waived-on-repair transparency chip
- API: proposed GET /v1/services/categories/:category_id/providers?lat=&lng=&sort= (nearby published service_offerings joined to provider profile)

### 384-farmer-provider-profile
*384 · Provider Profile — Krishi-Verse*

- Provider profile (FARMER view, Dinesh Solanki). Services offered — all REAL `service_offerings` rows, status='published': pump repair ₹300/visit (waived on repair), motor rewinding ₹1,200 fixed (pricing_model='fixed'), borewell check ₹500/visit. Service area: 12 km radius from Keshod (REAL `service_radius_km` column), covers Vanthali/Junagadh outskirts
- Reviews (2 shown, real via shared reviews table once seeded): Bharat Vaghasiya 5★, Jayesh Ben 5★. Work gallery (3 placeholder images) — ⚠ proposed column, not yet in service_offerings. Fair-pricing-on-parts honesty note: parts charged at market rate, receipt shown, never a flat/marked-up parts fee
- API: proposed GET /v1/services/providers/:provider_id (service_offerings rows + service_radius_km + reviews + gallery)

### 385-farmer-service-booking
*385 · Book a Service — Krishi-Verse*

- Booking request form (FARMER, Ramesh Patel, 7 Jul 2026). Submitting creates a `service_bookings` row, status='requested' (REAL). Provider: Dinesh Solanki. Problem: "Pump not starting — trips immediately" (⚠ proposed `problem_description` column) + photo attached (⚠ proposed `problem_photo_media_id`)
- Urgency: **Urgent** selected (⚠ proposed `urgency` column, mirrors vet_bookings' urgency/notes pattern) — context chip "Crop needs water — groundnut irrigation," farmer (not the app) judges the emergency-vs-urgent threshold. Location: Farm Survey 214/2, Vanthali. Slot: Today ASAP (7 Jul). Fee summary: Visit fee ₹300, waived-if-repair note, Due now ₹300 (correctly distinct from the eventual ₹750 total post-repair). "How quotes work" 3-step explainer (inspect → quote → approve)
- API: proposed POST /v1/me/services/bookings (creates service_bookings row; also accepts proposed urgency/problem_description/problem_photo_media_id fields pending migration)

### 386-farmer-booking-status
*386 · Booking Status — Krishi-Verse [QA-FIXED]*

- Live dispatch status (FARMER, KV-SVC-2026-0871). `service_bookings` REAL: booking_no, total_minor 75000 (₹750), status enum (REAL 6-value). ⚠ PROPOSED extension status shown in timeline: requested 8:10 AM → accepted 8:16 AM → **en_route ACTIVE** (arriving ~9:40 AM) → quoted (pending, shown expanded as live example) → in_progress (pending) → completed 11:20 AM
- `quoted_items` jsonb (proposed): Capacitor ₹450 + Labour ₹300. **Quote math verified: ₹450+₹300=₹750; visit fee ₹300 shown waived as "₹0, was ₹300" — total due ₹750, never ₹1,050.** **QA FIX APPLIED:** the visit-fee line initially showed only "waived on repair: ₹0" without the "was ₹300" comparison; corrected to match 387's already-correct receipt rendering exactly. Consent copy: "Work starts only after you say yes." Completed card: paid ₹750 via UPI, UTR 402719558124, Rate Dinesh CTA
- API: proposed GET /v1/me/services/bookings/:id (full detail + live dispatch status) · POST /v1/me/services/bookings/:id/quote (server-computed quoted_items + total) · POST /v1/me/services/bookings/:id/approve-quote (the consent gate)

### 387-farmer-service-completion-rating
*387 · Service Completion & Rating — Krishi-Verse*

- Receipt + two-sided rating (FARMER, KV-SVC-2026-0871, completed 11:20 AM). `service_bookings` REAL (completed, total_minor 75000). `reviews` REAL shared table: reviewer_user_id, target_type ('service_provider' this flow; ⚠ proposed 'service_customer' for the reverse direction mirroring 'farmer_employer' from screen 342), target_id, order_id→booking, stars 1–5, tags jsonb, is_verified_purchase=true, status='published', UNIQUE(order_id, reviewer, target_type, target_id)
- Receipt: Capacitor ₹450 + Labour ₹300 + **Visit fee waived "₹0, was ₹300"** (struck-through, explicit "was ₹300" label, never silently dropped) = Total paid ₹750. UPI UTR 402719558124. Parts receipt photo attached. Two-sided: Ramesh rated Dinesh 5★ (tags: On time, Fair price, Explained clearly); Dinesh rated Ramesh 5★ back ("clear problem description, prompt payment") — ratings open only after payment settles. "Book Dinesh again" CTA; rating explicitly does not affect what was already paid
- API: proposed POST /v1/me/services/bookings/:id/reviews (writes reviews row, both directions gated on is_verified_purchase + payment settlement)

### 388-worker-become-provider
*388 · Become a Service Provider — Krishi-Verse [WORKER]*

- Become-a-provider flow (WORKER, Arjun Rathod — existing D7 canon exact, ★4.3, 6 jobs, Junagadh). `service_offerings` REAL: provider_id, skill_id, pricing_model='per_unit' unit='acre', price_minor 15000 (₹150/acre), service_radius_km 8, status draft→published on publish tap. `skills` REAL (07_labour.sql): code='spraying', is_hazardous=true — this is WHY the PPE/hazard declaration step is required specifically for this listing, tied directly to the column, not a generic step
- `worker_kyc`/`kyc_status` REAL: Arjun's Aadhaar KYC reused from his existing worker profile, no re-upload (KYC is identity-level not listing-level). PPE checklist: Mask, Gloves, Boots + safe-handling pledge. Market hint ₹100–200/acre (illustrative, not from a live table). Availability references worker calendar screen 356 (reference row only, not built here). "New providers get a fair chance" note; "Your labour record speaks for you — ★4.3 · 6 jobs shown to customers"
- API: proposed POST /v1/me/services/offerings (creates service_offerings row, status='draft') · PATCH /v1/me/services/offerings/:id/publish (⚠ server must hard-gate publish on a PPE-declaration record when skills.is_hazardous=true)

### 389-partner-provider-job-queue
*389 · Provider Job Queue — Krishi-Verse [PARTNER — bilingual rural]*

- Job queue (PARTNER, Dinesh Solanki). `service_bookings` REAL status CHECK (requested|confirmed|in_progress|completed|cancelled|disputed). ⚠ PROPOSED dispatch extension: requested|accepted|en_route|in_progress|quoted|completed|cancelled|no_show + urgency ('normal'|'urgent') + quoted_items jsonb. Today summary: 1 completed, ₹750 earned, 2 pending. Map glance: Keshod (urgent pin), Vanthali (normal pin)
- Pending: **Meena Ben** (Keshod, borewell check, URGENT, requested 12:40) — Accept/Decline actions, decline requires a reason; **Suresh Bhai** (Vanthali, motor rewinding, tomorrow AM, status='accepted' ⚠ proposed value, REAL enum has no 'accepted' only 'confirmed'). Completed today: Ramesh Patel ✓, pump repair, ₹750, "5★ received" celebration chip
- API: proposed GET /v1/me/services/provider/job-queue (pending + completed-today bookings + today's earnings sum) · PATCH /v1/me/services/bookings/:id/status (provider-side transition, ⚠ blocked today by missing proposed enum values)

### 390-partner-provider-earnings
*390 · Provider Earnings — Krishi-Verse [PARTNER — bilingual rural]*

- Earnings view (PARTNER, Dinesh Solanki). `service_bookings` REAL status='completed' rows feed this view; payouts → `bank_accounts` REAL tables, weekly settlement cycle — **no wallet balance ever shown anywhere on the platform (strict rule)**. `reviews` REAL feeds the ★4.7 rating which (per PRD) also contributes to browse/search ranking priority
- **Arithmetic (exact, verified): 6 visit-only × ₹300 = ₹1,800 + 3 small repairs ≈₹500 = ₹1,500 + 1 medium job ₹800 + today's ₹750 = ₹4,850 total, 11 jobs.** YTD ₹28,400 (illustrative season total). Recent jobs: KV-SVC-2026-0871/Ramesh Patel ₹750 (today, UTR shown), Medium job/Vanthali ₹800 (4 Jul), Small repair/Keshod ₹500 (2 Jul), Visit-only/Keshod ₹300 (1 Jul). Tax-time-friendly note: "every settlement here is already dated and totalled for you"
- API: proposed GET /v1/me/services/provider/earnings?period= (completed-booking total_minor sums by period + payout status via bank_accounts)

### 391-shared-services-alerts
*391 · Service Alerts — Krishi-Verse [SHARED]*

- Notification feed (SHARED — farmer-facing, Ramesh Patel, with 1 provider-side example row folded in). notification-events-pending: no notifications/events table exists yet for `service_bookings` — every event type is a PROPOSED trigger against status transitions (REAL enum + proposed dispatch extension). `reviews` REAL is the source for the "rating received" event
- Today: booking accepted (8:16 AM), Dinesh on his way (9:12 AM, Track CTA), quote ready ₹750 (10:05 AM, Approve CTA — "work starts only after you approve"), work completed + payment receipt (11:20 AM, UTR shown), 5★ received (11:24 AM), **provider-side example row** "(provider)" tag — what Dinesh sees: new booking request, Meena Ben, Keshod, borewell check, urgent (12:40 PM, Open queue CTA linking to 389). Earlier: booking request sent (8:10 AM). Empty-state footer: "You're all caught up"
- API: proposed GET /v1/me/notifications?module=services (reuses shared platform notification convention, no module-specific table)

## Phase-2 · Transporter + Shared — screens 401–411 [NEW ROLE: Transporter/Delivery Partner — REAL core tables (shipments/vehicles/warehouses/nwr_receipts/soil_tests/weather_alerts), 5 schema gaps, designs lead]

> **Nav structure (FOUNDER-APPROVED 2026-07-08) — 5-item bottom nav for the NEW transporter (delivery partner) role:**
> **TAB 1 · Home** → 401 transporter-home (tab root — hub: next trip, today strip, earnings snapshot, vehicle status, rating).
> **TAB 2 · Trips** → 402 trip-assignments (tab root — today's trip list + history, full 11-value `shipment_status` legend) → 403 trip-navigation-pickup (sub-screen, back-header, not a tab root) → 404 pod-capture (sub-screen, back-header, not a tab root).
> **TAB 3 · Earnings** → 405 transporter-earnings (tab root — daily/weekly/monthly payout ledger).
> **TAB 4 · Vehicle** → 406 vehicle-management (tab root — fleet assigned to this partner, cold-chain readiness, document renewals).
> **TAB 5 · Profile** → reuses the platform profile pattern verbatim from 400-consumer-profile.html for settings/KYC/support/logout — no new pattern invented.
> Justification (3 lines, from 401's header comment): (1) **Task frequency** — Trips is the highest-frequency action for a delivery partner (checked before every leg, all day), so it earns the second tab, right after Home. (2) **Phase-1/D12 consistency** — five clear nouns, Home first and Profile last, matching the farmer app and the consumer app's `kv-bottomnav` (392-consumer-home.html), so shared components and muscle memory carry straight over. (3) **Cognitive load** — low-frequency items (KYC, support, settings, logout) nest under Profile like every other role; no junk-drawer sixth tab.
> Plus **5 SHARED farmer screens (407–411)** — compliance-readiness, eNWR, cold storage booking, soil test, weather advisory — grounded in warehouse/eNWR/soil/weather tables, bundled into this batch alongside the transporter role, not part of the 5-tab nav.
>
> **Role-vocabulary note:** the DB/PRD role code is `delivery_partner` (see `Database_Architecture`); **"Transporter"** is the Design Program's screen-family / file-naming label only, matching the folder + inventory convention (`NN-transporter-*.html`). The on-screen role chip therefore reads **"Delivery Partner"** — the user-facing and DB-facing term — never "Transporter". `partner_kind = 'rider'` for this operator (two-wheeler/light tempo rider tier, distinct from car/truck `partner_kind` values elsewhere in the DB).
>
> **Canon (today = Wed 8 Jul 2026, driver Vikram RATHOD, delivery_partner/rider):** **DISAMBIGUATION — Vikram RATHOD is a DIFFERENT canonical person from Vikram PATEL**, the labour sardar/contractor featured in 344-partner-labour-contractor-dashboard.html, 345-partner-contractor-worker-roster.html and 346-partner-contractor-settlements.html. Surname RATHOD is new and deliberately chosen to avoid any cross-role identity collision. (Consumer-facing screens 392/395, D12, refer to "Vikram" the D2C hub driver by first name only — read as the same Vikram Rathod persona seen here from the consumer side; no conflict, same person, two views.) Rating **4.8★ · 96% on-time** (illustrative, stated as given).
> **Trip 1** · morning consumer-box route · status delivered · done **7:02 AM** · **₹450** (exact match to 395-consumer-delivery-tracking.html's "Mon 30 Jun · Delivered · 7:02 AM · left at door by Vikram" pattern) · settled.
> **Trip 2** · produce pickup, Vanthali collection point → Junagadh mandi · 11:00 AM · ~14 km · generic vegetables ~800 kg (**NEVER groundnut** — groundnut harvest is Oct, out of season for a Jul run) · tempo GJ-11-KV-7734 · `shipment_status = assigned` (Accept already actioned) · cold-chain NOT required (`requires_cold_chain = false`) · drop at Junagadh mandi gate 2, 12:10 PM arrival · **+₹1,200** trip-earnings chip on completion.
> **Trip 3** · evening veg/box drop · 5:00 PM · small cold-chain leg REQUIRED (4°C, cold tempo GJ-11-KV-4521) · `shipment_status = pending` (**NEVER a milk tanker run** — that belongs to the dairy module's separate transport flow, a D5 guard).
> **New offer** (not yet on today's list): tomorrow Thu 9 Jul, 9:00 AM · Keshod → Rajkot · 62 km · **₹1,850 flat** offered fee (illustrative) · 30-minute accept window before it's reassigned.
> **Saturday Village Run** · `delivery_routes` recurring entry · every Sat · 4 villages consolidated onto one manifest — distinct from the single-leg shipments above.
> **Vehicles:** cold tempo **GJ-11-KV-4521** (refrigerated ✓, 500 kg, 4°C ready — same 4°C figure as 395's cold-chain badge) + tempo **GJ-11-KV-7734** (1,500 kg, general produce, not refrigerated, insurance renewal due Aug 2026 — designs-lead date, no DB field yet for document-expiry tracking).
> **Earnings arithmetic (exact, verified):** 22 local trips × ₹450 = ₹9,900; 8 mandi trips × ₹1,200 = ₹9,600; ₹9,900 + ₹9,600 = **₹19,500** July total (30 trips total) → matches 401's July snapshot exactly. **YTD ₹1,08,400** (illustrative season total, stated as given, lakh grouping). Today so far **₹450** (Trip 1 payout).
> **Earnings-vocabulary gap note (designs-lead):** the PRD/DB payout vocabulary for delivery-partner settlement cycles (weekly? per-trip? platform-fee-deducted?) is not yet finalized — every per-trip/monthly/YTD figure above is illustrative, stated as given by the design lead pending a real payout schema, same convention as 390-partner-provider-earnings.html. Settlement itself (weekly payout to bank via a payouts table) IS platform-wide canon: **NO wallet balance is ever shown anywhere on the platform** (strict rule, matching 390's DB-truth comment).
>
> **Shared screens canon:** **407 (export readiness)** — FRAMING: this is a compliance-**readiness** checklist, NOT exporter registration; `exporter_registrations` exists in the DB but is NOT owned by the farmer role (Ramesh is a grower, not an exporter of record) — that table/flow belongs to the buyer/exporter role, never surfaced here. Linked to contract KV-CON-2026-0231 (Mehta Oil Industries), quality_spec moisture_max_pct 8, tracked as "practice alignment," not a new spec. Readiness meter 2 of 4 (presentational UI-composition score, no readiness-score table in DB). **408 (eNWR)** — NERL-ENWR-2026-088412, Wheat (Lokwan) 8 qtl, deposited 15 Feb 2026, Junagadh AgriStore (WDRA reg. WDRA/GJ/00214), moisture assay 11.2%, valuation ₹19,600 (8 × ₹2,450/qtl), `nwr_status = issued`, pledge available but NOT taken. GUARD: `pledged_loan_id` is a soft ref for FUTURE pledge loans against THIS receipt only — never Ramesh's existing KCC loan KV-LN-2026-04412 (which uses LAND collateral per D2 canon); this screen must never suggest that loan is linked to or backed by this eNWR. **409 (cold storage)** — Junagadh AgriStore offers cold_0_4 at ₹32/qtl/month (WDRA-registered, 4 km); Vanthali Cold Chain Hub offers cold_0_4 + frozen at ₹38/qtl/month, 9 km, honestly labelled "Private facility · not WDRA." Booking is for a small leafy-vegetable lot destined for a D2C delivery box (consumer-module tie-in), not the farmer's main groundnut/wheat commodity: 3 qtl × ₹32/qtl/month × 0.5 month (2 weeks) = **₹48** (exact). **410 (soil test)** — Survey 214/2, SHC card GJ-SHC-88-04412, sample date 12 Mar 2026, valid until ~Mar 2029 (SHC cards valid ~3 years), 12 canonical parameters `{ph,ec,oc,n,p,k,s,zn,fe,cu,mn,b}`, recommendations tie to the farmer's CURRENT crop (GG-20 groundnut, vegetative stage per D9 canon) — recommendations jsonb content references the crop actually sown, never a generic crop. New-test booking: lab pickup or bring-sample, slot 12 Jul 2026 morning, fee ₹250. **411 (weather advisory)** — `alert_type = heavy_rain`, valid 10–11 Jul 2026 (from 10 Jul 6 AM to 11 Jul 11 PM), Vanthali/Junagadh, source IMD, severity="Watch" (designs-lead free-varchar choice, calm framing, never alarmist). Crop-stage advisory card joins the alert to the farmer's known crop (GG-20 groundnut, VEGETATIVE/crop-establishment stage, sown 18 Jun, flowering not until Aug) at render time — UI composition only, no DB relationship. Insurance nudge references D3 canon: parametric policy **KV-POL-2025-0705-112 LAPSED**, and the 30 Jun 2026 rain claim of **₹28,500** already paid under the (now-lapsed) product — used to build trust, not alarm, nudging re-enrollment via 280 (product detail) / 283 (enroll flow).
>
> **REAL schema (verified against screens' own header comments):**
> 1. `shipments` — `shipment_status` ENUM (11 values, exact): **pending | assigned | pickup_scheduled | picked_up | in_transit | at_hub | out_for_delivery | delivered | failed | returned | cancelled**. Columns referenced on-screen: `pickup_otp_hash`, `delivery_otp_hash`, `pod_media_id`, `cod_minor`, `requires_cold_chain`, `distance_km`.
> 2. `shipment_events` — GPS/lat-lng + status trail; "Start transit" writes a new row with the device's current lat/lng and status `in_transit`.
> 3. `logistics_partners` — `partner_kind`: **3pl | tenant_fleet | rider**.
> 4. `vehicles` — `vehicle_type` lookup: **bike | tempo | truck | reefer_7mt | tractor_trolley**; `is_refrigerated` (true for GJ-11-KV-4521, false for GJ-11-KV-7734); `rc_doc_id` ("RC verified" chip is driven purely by presence of this doc id).
> 5. `delivery_routes` — the Village Run recurring pattern (day_of_week + stop consolidation), distinct from ad-hoc `shipments` rows.
> 6. `cold_chain_logs` — `subject_type='shipment'`, `temp_c`, `is_breach`; last cold-van trip logged 3.8–4.2°C, no breach.
> 7. `warehouses` — `wdra_reg_no`, `storage_kinds` jsonb (**ambient | cold_0_4 | frozen | ca**), `rate_per_qtl_month_minor`.
> 8. `storage_bookings.status` — varchar vocab (6, exact, no invented states): **requested | confirmed | stored | partially_released | released | cancelled**.
> 9. `assay_reports` — `parameters` jsonb (moisture 11.2% chip on 408).
> 10. `nwr_receipts` — repository ENUM **NERL | CCRL**; `nwr_status` ENUM (6 values, exact): **issued | pledged | partially_released | released | cancelled | defaulted**; `enwr_no` unique; `valuation_minor`; `pledged_loan_id` (soft ref, future-pledge-only guard, see canon above).
> 11. `soil_tests` — `parcel_id`, `shc_card_no`, `results` jsonb (12 canonical parameters), `recommendations` jsonb, `valid_until`.
> 12. `weather_alerts` — `alert_type` lookup (7 values, exact): **heavy_rain | drought | frost | hail | heatwave | cyclone | pest_risk**; `severity` is a FREE varchar (no DB enum); `valid_from`/`valid_to`.
> 13. `compliance_requirements` (`rules` jsonb, e.g. `eu_mrl`) / `export_documents` (doc types: **bol | awb | commercial_invoice | packing_list | coo | phyto | fumigation | insurance | inspection**) — plain-language explainer only on 407, farmer does not generate these docs.
>
> ⚠ **SCHEMA GAPS (designs-lead area — 5 items, prominent, backend pending):**
> 1. **Rider earnings vocabulary** — no `delivery_fee` payout purpose and no rider-fee column exist in the current payout schema. Every per-trip/July/YTD earnings figure across 401/402/404/405 is illustrative, stated as given by the design lead pending a real payout schema (same convention as 390-partner-provider-earnings.html).
> 2. **Vehicle document expiry dates** — `vehicles` has NO insurance/permit/DL date columns. Any renewal date shown (e.g. "renews Aug 2026" on 401/406) is a DESIGNS-LEAD placeholder — reminder dates are app-side only, not yet in records; chips are explicitly labelled. Driving licence is a KYC document (`kyc_documents` against the delivery_partner's profile), NOT a `vehicles` column — referenced on 406 only as a cross-link.
> 3. **Soil-test booking workflow** — there is NO booking-workflow table for soil tests in the DB. The "book a test" card on 410 (lab pickup vs bring-sample, slot, fee) is a DESIGNS-LEAD pre-report flow composition, not backed by a booking table; it front-ends the eventual creation of a `soil_tests` row once a report is issued.
> 4. **Weather crop-stage join** — `weather_alerts` has NO crop-stage column. The crop-stage advisory card on 411 is UI COMPOSITION ONLY: this screen joins the alert to the farmer's known crop (GG-20 groundnut, vegetative stage) at render time, not a DB relationship.
> 5. **POD signature** — there is NO signature column on `shipments`. If a signature pad is ever shown in mockups it is a VISUAL-ONLY note pending schema — not built here, per instruction; 404 uses photo + OTP only (`pod_media_id` + `delivery_otp_hash`). The 404 receiver-name field is likewise VISUAL-ONLY (no schema column), a convenience note for the driver's own memory, never persisted.
>
> **QA — CLEAN PASS, zero required fixes; nav founder-approved 2026-07-08.**
>
> **Deferred nits (flagged for D16, not fixed in this batch):** (1) **404** — a dead `.tpc-celebrate { ...; display: none }` CSS rule (harmless, unused celebration-card style, never toggled on in this static mockup) — flagged, not fixed. (2) **409** — the booking-status-flow stepper renders only 4 of the 6 real `storage_bookings.status` values (Requested → Confirmed → Stored → Released, omitting `partially_released` and `cancelled`) — this is the happy-path only by design (comment-documented as complete/intentional for this booking's flow), not a bug — flagged, not fixed.
>
> Source: screens' own header comments (401–411), cross-referenced against `Database_Architecture` (shipments/shipment_events/logistics_partners/vehicles/delivery_routes/cold_chain_logs/warehouses/storage_bookings/assay_reports/nwr_receipts/soil_tests/weather_alerts/compliance_requirements/export_documents — all confirmed REAL tables) and D2/D3/D5/D7/D9/D12 canon for cross-references (Vikram Patel, KV-LN-2026-04412, KV-CON-2026-0231, GG-20 groundnut/vegetative stage, KV-POL-2025-0705-112, 395's 7:02 AM handoff, 400's profile pattern, 390's no-wallet-balance rule).

Stepper shows 5 of the 11 `shipment_status` values relevant to this leg (assigned→pickup_scheduled→picked_up→in_transit→delivered; full 11-value legend lives on 402)

*404 · Proof of Delivery Capture — Krishi-Verse [SUB-SCREEN under Trips, back-header] [DEFERRED D16: dead CSS]*

- POD capture sub-screen (DELIVERY PARTNER), reached at the end of Trip 2's transit leg (403 → in_transit → this screen → delivered), drop at Junagadh mandi. PHOTO + OTP ONLY, NO SIGNATURE FIELD (schema gap 5): `shipments.pod_media_id` stores the delivery photo; `shipments.delivery_otp_hash` verifies the receiver's 6-digit code server-side, raw OTP never persisted. Receiver-name field is VISUAL-ONLY (no schema column). `shipments.cod_minor` = 0 for Trip 2 (mandi drop, no COD collection) — guard note tells the driver to collect only what the app instructs

### 405-transporter-earnings
*405 · Transporter Earnings — Krishi-Verse [TAB ROOT — Earnings]*

- Earnings ledger (DELIVERY PARTNER, Vikram Rathod), pattern follows 390-partner-provider-earnings.html conventions (summary hero, per-job rows, YTD strip, no wallet balance, tax-simple note). Schema gap 1 (designs-lead): no `delivery_fee` value, no rider-fee column in the current schema — every figure is illustrative pending a real payout schema. NO wallet balance is ever shown anywhere on the platform (strict rule, matching 390)
- Arithmetic (exact, verified): 22 local trips × ₹450 = ₹9,900 + 8 mandi trips × ₹1,200 = ₹9,600 = **₹19,500** July total (30 trips), matching 401's July snapshot exactly. YTD ₹1,08,400 (illustrative). Today: Trip 1 ₹450 settled ✓; Trip 2 ₹1,200 on completion (matches 404's chip). Rating 4.8★ · 96% on-time
