## P0 Additions · Platform, Auth & Access — screens 428–434 [D16 REMEDIATION] [batch #1 of the D16-audit P0 remediation plan; 7 screens, 2 roles (shared ×6, worker ×1); closes the auth/session/access gaps flagged by the final platform audit — forgot-PIN, OTP/PIN lockout, session expiry, account suspension, device/session management, a v2 9-role picker superseding 04-role.html, and worker registration]

> **Canon law (validation-locked, carried verbatim from Phase-1 03-otp.html / 75-settings.html — re-verified, not re-invented):**
> 1. OTP = 6-digit, 7-minute expiry, 3 attempts ("2 of 3 attempts remaining"), resend cooldown **0:42**, mono-digit countdown treatment.
> 2. Masked phone format **"+91 98765 ●●●●●"** — used identically on 428 (forgot-PIN) and 430 (session-expired re-auth).
> 3. Lockout law (429, microcopy-library verbatim, required copy — do not paraphrase): *"🔒 Account temporarily locked / Too many incorrect OTP attempts. Please try again in 30 minutes, or contact support."* Trigger: 3 wrong OTP attempts → 30-minute cool-down, live countdown. A sibling PIN-lockout copy variant ("Too many incorrect PIN attempts") ships on the same screen for the 428 forgot-PIN flow.
> 4. Lockout-bypass guard (428→429): before Stage 2 (send/verify OTP) the backend MUST check the Redis lockout key for the phone number first; if locked, hard-redirect to 429 — a user must never retry OTP send from 428 while a lockout is active.
>
> **DB TRUTH — REAL schema this batch reads/writes today:**
> 1. `users.status` ENUM EXACT `active\|pending_verification\|suspended\|restricted\|soft_deleted` — 431 reads/displays `suspended`.
> 2. `sessions.revoked_at` / `sessions.expires_at` — the actual invalidation mechanism behind both 430 (idle-timeout re-auth) and 432's per-device "Log out" action (writes `revoked_at = now()`, device row stays in history, only the session token is killed).
> 3. `devices` table — `fingerprint`/`platform`/`model`/`os_version`/`app_version`/`last_seen_at`/`push_token` drive 432's device list. `push_token` is an internal delivery credential and is **never surfaced in any UI**. No raw IP or precise location is shown for any device (privacy) — only "Junagadh, Gujarat (approx.)"-style copy is proposed pending a future geo-lookup service; until then the UI omits location rather than show a raw IP.
> 4. `login_events` — source of 432's new-device alert banner (`role="alert"`, announced immediately on load), derived from first sight of an unrecognised device fingerprint.
> 5. `data_subject_requests` — backs 431's "request copy or deletion" row; its existing 90-day DPDP cooling-off period is unaffected by a suspension (suspension does not pause or affect these rights).
> 6. `worker_profiles.age_verified_18` — HARD server-side rule on 434, enforced from DOB at submission, not merely UI copy; under-18 registrations are blocked before KYC.
> 7. `worker_profiles.stay_away_ok` ENUM EXACT `same_day\|overnight\|weekly\|monthly` — 434's migrant-worker willingness ladder.
> 8. `roles.requires_kyc` / `roles.requires_approval` — columns EXIST on the `roles` table today, but the table is **UNSEEDED** for the 5 new roles 433 introduces (worker/vyapari/consumer/transporter/service already existed; the gap is seed data, not schema).
>
> **CONCEPT / BACKEND-PENDING schema (no backing tables — design proposals only, flagged for the backend team, not yet in Database_Architecture):**
> 1. **`users.pin_hash` / `users.pin_set_at`** — NO PIN-hash column exists on `users` today. Shipping 428's reset flow requires adding `users.pin_hash` (hashed, never plaintext) + `users.pin_set_at` (timestamptz). Until then, 428 is UI-only / design-lead-approved mock. OTP attempt-count and lockout TTL themselves are Redis-only (counter + TTL key per phone/session) — there are NO lockout fields on `users` or `sessions` in Postgres; `sessions.revoked_at` is only the mechanism to force re-login once a lockout clears, never the lockout timer itself.
> 2. **`suspension_reason_code` + appeals queue** — no `suspension_reason` enum/column exists in Database_Architecture. The 4 reason classes on 431 (Policy violation / Suspicious activity review / Unresolved dispute / Tenant-initiated) are a design-lead PROPOSAL for de-facto display codes only, pending backend ratification of the final taxonomy as `suspension_reason_code`. 431's "respond within 3 business days" appeal SLA is a COPY COMMITMENT only — no appeals-queue table exists yet; a backend ticket is pending for an appeals/disputes queue to operationally back that promise.
> 3. **`roles.requires_approval` seeds** — the column exists but has no seeded rows for the pending roles (vet, warehouse operator, sardar) 433's invite footnote names; those roles route through invitation/tenant assignment, not self-serve pick, until seeded.
> 4. **Missed-call IVR confirmation channel** — 429's "Verify via missed call instead" chip is a DESIGNER PROPOSAL; no IVR/missed-call channel exists in the current OTP delivery stack (SMS-only today). Shown as a disabled/pending chip pending backend + telco confirmation, never a working alternate flow.
>
> **Canon-guard summary:**
> 1. 428/429/430 OTP/lockout copy, countdown format and masked-phone format are byte-for-byte the same law used on Phase-1's 03-otp.html and 75-settings.html — no drift, no re-invention.
> 2. 430 deliberately DEVIATES from the full-screen takeover convention used by 428/429/431: it renders as a bottom-sheet (`kv-backdrop` + `kv-sheet`, existing approved `components.css` primitives) over a dimmed, frozen mock of the screen the user was on — documented, not an oversight. Rationale: session timeout is routine/low-stakes, so the UI stays "quiet," visibly preserving the user's place in the app ("nothing was lost" / "return where you left off").
> 3. 431 structure deliberately mirrors 177-account-delete.html (red gradient hero, warning box, alternative-path callout) but is scoped to a USER-level `users.status='suspended'`, never a tenant-level suspension — `tenant_status` is a separate enum on the `tenants` table, out of scope here and explicitly DEFERRED to batch D21 per the D16 pending-work note; the "Tenant-initiated" reason class is the individual user's echo of a tenant decision, never the tenant's own suspended-tenant admin view.
> 4. 432 privacy guard: no raw IP ever rendered; `push_token` never surfaced; revoke is non-destructive (`sessions.revoked_at`), never a `devices` row delete — history is preserved.
> 5. 433 explicitly SUPERSEDES 04-role.html as the live 9-role picker (04 retained in the screens folder for design-history reference only, not deleted) — extends 04's verbatim hero copy and reuses its 5 original gradients, adding 4 new role gradients (worker teal `#2a9d8f→#1a6b60`, consumer rose `#e76f8a→#b03a56`, transporter slate `#5a7d9a→#33506b`, service copper `#c07a3a→#8a4f1d`). Consumer/transporter/service-provider routes correctly show an `is-pending` "onboarding coming shortly" chip pointing at D23 backlog targets (screens 501, 503, and a third TBD-numbered flow) rather than a dead link. Invite footnote names Vet, Warehouse Operator and Sardar as invitation/tenant-assigned roles, consistent with the `roles.requires_approval` unseeded-gap note above.
> 6. 434 fair-wage helper text ("Local market range: ₹350–₹500 · You're in fair range ✓") is verbatim-consistent with the Phase-1 fair-range helper pattern (screen 136); the migrant `stay_away_ok` ladder, e-Shram number field and emergency-contact fields are optional/encouraged, never gating; the spraying hazard chip carries a protective (farmer-must-provide-safety-gear) note, never framed as a warning to the worker; KYC entry reuses the exact 72-kyc-aadhaar-start.html worker-framed pattern ("Required to receive wages — Needed before farmers can pay you").
>
> QA — batch-wide bilingual-wrapper regression caught on independent QA and fixed identically on all 7 screens (single systemic root cause, single-point fix); 3 screens' `<meta name="description">` tags normalized to the catalog's "Role App — Screen Title" convention; `04_SCREEN_GENERATION_SPEC.md` hardened afterward to prevent recurrence of both patterns in future batches.
>
> Source: screens' own header comments (428–434), cross-referenced against `Database_Architecture` (users/sessions/devices/login_events/data_subject_requests/worker_profiles/roles tables) and Phase-1 canon (03-otp, 75-settings, 177-account-delete, 04-role, 72-kyc-aadhaar-start, 136 fair-range helper) for verbatim/format fidelity.

### 428-shared-forgot-pin-reset

*428 · Forgot PIN Reset — Krishi-Verse [SHARED] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- 3-stage flow (phone entry → OTP verify → new PIN set), lockout-aware: before Stage 2 the client must check the Redis lockout key for this phone first and hard-redirect to 429 if locked — a user can never retry OTP send from here mid-lockout. Canon OTP law verbatim (6-digit/7-min/3-attempts/0:42) and masked phone "+91 98765 ●●●●●" (matches 75-settings.html).
- ⚠ BACKEND-PENDING: `users` has no `pin_hash` column today. Shipping this flow requires `users.pin_hash` (hashed, never plaintext) + `users.pin_set_at` (timestamptz) — flagged for backend, not yet in Database_Architecture; screen is UI-only / design-lead-approved mock until then. Attempt-count/lockout state itself is Redis TTL + counter only, never a Postgres column.
- API: proposed POST /v1/auth/forgot-pin/start · POST /v1/auth/forgot-pin/verify-otp · POST /v1/auth/forgot-pin/set-pin (writes pin_hash/pin_set_at once schema lands)

### 429-shared-otp-lockout

*429 · OTP / PIN Lockout — Krishi-Verse [SHARED] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Verbatim required copy (microcopy-library law, do not paraphrase): "🔒 Account temporarily locked / Too many incorrect OTP attempts. Please try again in 30 minutes, or contact support." Live mono-digit 30-minute countdown, same visual language as the 0:42 OTP-resend timer. Sibling PIN-lockout copy variant included in the same shell for 428's forgot-PIN flow ("Too many incorrect PIN attempts").
- Lockout state is Redis-only (attempt counter + TTL key per phone/session) — no lockout fields exist on `users`/`sessions` in Postgres; `sessions.revoked_at` only forces re-login once a lockout resolves, never stores the countdown. SIM-swap safety banner is protective disclosure, calm tone, never accusatory. "Verify via missed call instead" is a DESIGNER PROPOSAL chip shown disabled/pending — no IVR/missed-call channel exists in the current SMS-only OTP stack.
- API: proposed GET /v1/auth/lockout-status (reads Redis TTL/counter) · POST /v1/support/contact (lockout escalation)

### 430-shared-session-expired

*430 · Session Expired — Krishi-Verse [SHARED] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- DOCUMENTED DEVIATION from the full-screen takeover convention used by 428/429/431: renders as a bottom-sheet (`kv-backdrop` + `kv-sheet`, existing approved components.css primitives) over a dimmed, frozen mock of the screen the user was on, not a full-screen replace — intentional, because a session timeout is routine/low-stakes and the UI should stay "quiet," visibly preserving the user's place ("return where you left off"). No back-header — nothing to go back to except the sheet's own re-auth action.
- Session invalidation is `sessions.revoked_at` being set under idle-timeout policy — no separate "session_expired" status exists; the client simply finds its access token rejected and shows this sheet. Masked phone "+91 98765 ●●●●●" matches 75-settings.html. The frozen "Draft listing" backdrop is illustrative — the real return target is whatever route/draft state the client had in memory at expiry.
- API: proposed POST /v1/auth/reauth (re-validates OTP/PIN against the still-valid user, clears the expired session's block) · client resumes cached route/draft state on success

### 431-shared-account-suspended

*431 · Account Suspended — Krishi-Verse [SHARED] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Structure mirrors 177-account-delete.html (red gradient hero, warning box, alternative-path callout) but scoped to a USER-level suspension. `users.status` ENUM EXACT `active\|pending_verification\|suspended\|restricted\|soft_deleted` — this screen reads `suspended`. ⚠ No `suspension_reason` enum/column exists in Database_Architecture; the 4 reason classes shown (Policy violation / Suspicious activity review / Unresolved dispute / Tenant-initiated) are a design-lead PROPOSAL for de-facto display codes — pending backend ratification as `suspension_reason_code`, not yet a ratified DB enum.
- GUARD: user-level suspension only — a SEPARATE tenant-level suspension exists (`tenant_status`, its own enum on the `tenants` table, independent of `users.status`); that tenant-suspended admin view is out of scope here and explicitly DEFERRED to batch D21. Appeal SLA ("respond within 3 business days") is a COPY COMMITMENT only — no appeals-queue table exists yet, backend ticket pending. Wallet balance stays fully visible/withdrawable (wallet-safe) throughout suspension. DPDP: `data_subject_requests` backs the "request copy or deletion" row with its existing 90-day cooling-off period, unaffected by suspension.
- API: proposed GET /v1/me/suspension-status (reads users.status + proposed suspension_reason_code) · POST /v1/me/suspension/appeal (writes to proposed appeals queue, 3-business-day SLA copy commitment) · existing GET/POST /v1/me/data-subject-requests (DPDP, unaffected)

### 432-shared-manage-devices-sessions

*432 · Manage Devices & Sessions — Krishi-Verse [SHARED] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Lists `devices` rows (`fingerprint`/`platform`/`model`/`os_version`/`app_version`/`last_seen_at`) joined against `sessions`. Per-device "Log out" writes `sessions.revoked_at = now()` for that session only — the `devices` row itself is never deleted, only revoked, preserving device history. Bulk "Log out of all other devices" uses the same spell-out confirmation pattern used elsewhere for destructive-adjacent actions.
- PRIVACY GUARDS: no raw IP or precise location shown for any device — current-device card and others intentionally omit `sessions.ip` (not user-facing); a future "Junagadh, Gujarat (approx.)"-style geo-lookup is proposed but not built, so the screen omits location rather than expose a raw IP today. `devices.push_token` is an internal delivery credential and is never surfaced in any UI, anywhere. NEW-DEVICE ALERT banner is sourced from `login_events` (first sight of an unrecognised fingerprint), `role="alert"` so screen readers announce it immediately on load.
- API: proposed GET /v1/me/devices (joins devices+sessions) · POST /v1/me/devices/:id/revoke (sets sessions.revoked_at) · POST /v1/me/devices/revoke-all-others (bulk, spell-out-confirmed) · GET /v1/me/login-events (new-device banner source)

### 433-shared-role-picker-v2

*433 · Choose Role (v2) — Krishi-Verse [SHARED] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- SUPERSEDES 04-role.html as the live 9-role picker (04 retained in the screens folder for design-history reference, not deleted). Extends 04's verbatim hero copy and its 5 original role gradients, adding 4 new gradients: worker teal `#2a9d8f→#1a6b60`, consumer rose `#e76f8a→#b03a56`, transporter slate `#5a7d9a→#33506b`, service copper `#c07a3a→#8a4f1d`. 9 role cards total.
- `roles.requires_kyc` / `roles.requires_approval` columns EXIST on the `roles` table but are UNSEEDED for the 5 newer roles — flagged gap, not a schema gap. Consumer/transporter/service-provider routes correctly show an `is-pending` "onboarding coming shortly" chip targeting D23 backlog screens 501/503 (and a third TBD-numbered flow) instead of a dead link. Invite footnote names Vet, Warehouse Operator and Sardar as staff roles added by invitation or FPO/tenant assignment, not self-serve pick.
- API: proposed GET /v1/roles (reads roles table incl. requires_kyc/requires_approval once seeded) · POST /v1/me/roles (select/add role) — same endpoint shape as 04, extended for the 4 new role codes

### 434-worker-registration-onboarding

*434 · Worker Registration — Krishi-Verse [WORKER] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Skills-tiers registration form. `worker_profiles.age_verified_18` is a HARD server-side rule enforced from DOB at submission (not just UI copy) — under-18 registrations are blocked before KYC. Fair-wage helper text ("Local market range: ₹350 – ₹500 · You're in fair range ✓") verbatim-consistent with the Phase-1 fair-range helper pattern (screen 136). `worker_profiles.stay_away_ok` ENUM EXACT `same_day\|overnight\|weekly\|monthly` powers the migrant-willingness ladder, framed as widening job matches, never a requirement.
- e-Shram number and emergency-contact fields are optional/encouraged, never gating. Spraying hazard chip surfaces a protective note ("farmers must provide protective gear and safety training before you start") — framed at the farmer's obligation, never as a warning to the worker. KYC entry reuses the exact 72-kyc-aadhaar-start.html worker-framed pattern ("Required to receive wages — Needed before farmers can pay you").
- API: proposed POST /v1/workers/register (writes worker_profiles incl. age_verified_18 server check, stay_away_ok, skills, wage_expectation) · existing KYC endpoints per 72's pattern

---

## P0 Additions · Commerce Trust — screens 435–449 [D16 REMEDIATION] [batch #2 of the D16-audit P0 remediation plan; 15 screens, 4 roles (tenant ×2, farmer ×6, buyer ×5, shared ×2); closes the marketplace-trust gaps flagged by the final platform audit — listing moderation, farmer resubmit, offer negotiation round 2, buyer/farmer/shared dispute lifecycle, payment failure, order cancel, delivery failure, returns (buyer + farmer sides), logistics bounce intake, and partial fulfilment (buyer + farmer sides)]

> **Canon-continuation table (which Phase-1/prior-batch screens each story extends — verified exact, not re-invented):**

| Screen(s) | Extends | Canon anchor — exact figures |
|---|---|---|
| 435, 436 | 12-my-listings.html (AI-grade badge source) · 79-tenant-listings.html (tenant listings surface) · 99-buyer-make-offer.html (`mo-fair` fair-band calculation, reused for the fair-band check) | Ramesh Patel's Okra listing, `listings.status='pending_approval'` |
| 437 | 435/436 (same listing, rejected → resubmit) | Same Okra listing, `reject_reason` free text carried verbatim |
| 438 | 99-buyer-make-offer.html — EXACT continuation, same offer thread | Buyer offer ₹2,650/qtl × 2 qtl = ₹5,300 (round 1, `open`) → farmer counters ₹2,780/qtl × 2 = ₹5,560 (round 2, `countered`); new 10% earnest ₹556 − buyer's existing ₹530 lock = ₹26 top-up owed |
| 439, 440, 441 | 156-tenant-dispute-detail.html — EXACT continuation, same case | #DSP-2026-0247 · escrow_holds ₹14,400 (5 qtl × ₹2,880) · resolution split ₹2,200 buyer / ₹12,200 seller = ₹14,400 exact |
| 442 | 15-checkout.html — EXACT continuation, same order | ₹5,760 (Premium Wheat Lokwan 2 qtl) + ₹150 delivery + ₹296 GST − ₹100 discount = ₹6,106 exact |
| 443 | 56-farmer-orders.html — EXACT continuation | #KV-2026-0215 · Rajesh Trader (buyer) · Yellow Maize 5 qtl · ₹9,550 · pre-accept ("⏱ 2 hours left") |
| 444 | 131-buyer-order-track.html — extends the timeline-chrome pattern; COMMENT GUARD: a DIFFERENT order from 131's own case | #KV-2026-0251 (Ramesh Patel's wheat, 5 qtl, driver Hareshbhai · GJ-23-AB-7821) — never 131's #KV-2026-0247, which succeeded and is untouched here |
| 445, 446 | 56-farmer-orders.html — EXACT continuation | #KV-2026-0198 · Vadodara Mills (buyer) · Yellow Maize 8 qtl · ₹15,280 (₹1,910/qtl) · Delivered ✓; seller = Ramesh Patel |
| 447 | 444 (same order, logistics continuation after 2nd failed attempt) | #KV-2026-0251, `shipments.status` `failed` (444) → `returned` (447) |
| 448, 449 | 56-farmer-orders.html — EXACT continuation | #KV-2026-0201 · Anand FPO (buyer, bulk) · Wheat-Sharbati 10 qtl · ₹31,500 · shortfall 2 qtl × ₹3,150 = ₹6,300 → revised ₹31,500 − ₹6,300 = ₹25,200 exact |

> **DB TRUTH — REAL schema this batch reads/writes today:**
> 1. `listings.status` ENUM EXACT (9 values) `draft | pending_approval | published | paused | sold_out | expired | rejected | hidden | archived` — 435's queue lists rows WHERE `status='pending_approval'` only; 436 sets `rejected`; 437 moves `rejected` → `pending_approval` on resubmit (same row, not a new listing).
> 2. `listing_offers` table (migration 0021) — `status` ENUM `open | countered | accepted | rejected | expired | converted`, plus `round` and `counter_price_minor` columns tracking the negotiation ladder used by 438.
> 3. `disputes.status` ENUM EXACT (7 values) `open | seller_responded | under_review | escalated | resolved | rejected | withdrawn` — 439 shows `under_review`, 440 captures the compose moment while still `open` (submitting transitions it to `seller_responded`), 441 moves `resolved` (accept) or `escalated` (appeal — a valid enum value, so the appeal path is enum-correct, not a UX-only branch).
> 4. `escrow_holds.amount` — real column backing 439/440/441's ₹14,400 lock and its scheduled +24h release on acceptance.
> 5. `order_status` ENUM includes `cancelled` (443's action sets it) and `partially_fulfilled` (449's confirm action sets it, mirrored read-side on 448).
> 6. `shipments.status` ENUM includes `failed` (444's delivery attempt) and `returned` (447's logistics bounce-back after a 2nd failed attempt); `order_status` itself is unaffected by a single failed attempt — it stays `in_transit` until redelivery or a return decision.
> 7. `payments.payment_status` ENUM includes `failed` plus refund states (442) — full refund-state list not yet ratified, see backend-pending below.
> 8. ANTI-IDOR: 439/440's dispute thread/compose screens are only servable to users where `disputes.against_user` (or the complainant/respondent pair) resolves server-side to the requesting user's own ID — a party must never fetch another party's dispute by guessing dispute IDs; enforced at the API/query layer, never by client-side role checks alone. This is the same `dispute_eligibility` guard pattern referenced across 439/440/441.

> **CONCEPT / BACKEND-PENDING schema (no backing tables/ratified types — design proposals only, flagged for the backend team, not yet in Database_Architecture):**
> 1. **`listings.reject_reason`** is FREE TEXT — there is no reason-code lookup table. The reason "chips" on 436 are a DESIGN-LEAD UX AFFORDANCE ONLY: tapping one pre-fills the free-text field with a standard phrase; the DB still stores plain text, never an enum key. 437 displays whatever free text was stored, verbatim.
> 2. **436's "Request changes" branch** is a DESIGN-LEAD PROPOSED UX-ONLY BRANCH — it does NOT change `listings.status` (the listing remains `pending_approval`) and only attaches an internal note; there is no separate `changes_requested` DB status today. Flagged as a candidate future enum value, not yet ratified.
> 3. **`cancel_reason`** is a LOOKUP TYPE THAT EXISTS in schema but has ZERO SEEDED VALUES — 443's 4 reason chips are a DESIGNS-LEAD PROPOSED LIST pending backend/ops confirmation, not yet a ratified enum with real rows.
> 4. **`return_reason`** — no separate type exists in the schema. Returns (445/446) REUSE the `dispute_reason` vocabulary (e.g. `poor_quality`) as a designs-lead reuse decision — backend must either ratify this reuse formally or introduce a dedicated `return_reason` type; until decided, this is a documented assumption, not DB truth.
> 5. **`returns.status`** — currently a comment-vocab VARCHAR (`requested | approved | in_transit | received | refunded | rejected`), NOT yet a ratified Postgres ENUM type — flagged for backend to formalize into a real enum.
> 6. **`payments.failure_code`** — FREE VARCHAR, gateway-style (e.g. `UPI_TIMEOUT`, `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS`, `BANK_DECLINED`); no fixed enum/vocabulary exists yet. 442 shows `UPI_TIMEOUT` as a designs-lead placeholder code, not a ratified value.
> 7. **Second-failure redelivery fee policy** — 447's buyer-charge note reflects a per-policy fee for a second failed delivery attempt; this is DESIGNS-LEAD, not yet a ratified fee-schedule column anywhere in Database_Architecture.
> 8. **Max negotiation rounds (3)** on 438 is a DESIGN-LEAD ASSUMPTION for the offer-ladder UI — not a stored cap on `listing_offers` — backend should confirm before treating it as a hard limit.
> 9. **441's `resolution_type`** (`refund_full | refund_partial | no_action | replacement`) is a DESIGN-LEAD FIELD NAME proposal pending backend ratification as a column on `disputes`/`dispute_resolutions`; "one appeal only" is a COPY COMMITMENT / product rule, not yet a stored appeal-count column.

> **Canon-guard summary:**
> 1. 435/436 are TENANT-role, English-only (no bilingual wrapper) — consistent with the program's back-office convention (147/12 precedent); 437 onward (farmer/buyer/shared) are bilingual per the hardened spec rule (mandatory wrapper whenever `.hi` spans are present).
> 2. 438 is an EXACT continuation of 99-buyer-make-offer.html's own offer thread, not a new negotiation — the round/earnest/top-up math ties back to 99's stored figures precisely.
> 3. 439/440/441 all resolve to the SAME case (#DSP-2026-0247) already fully specified on 156-tenant-dispute-detail.html — no new dispute was invented, only the buyer/farmer/shared party-view screens around the existing canon case.
> 4. 442 reuses 15-checkout.html's order figures byte-for-byte (₹6,106) — tone is money-safety-first: auto-refund language appears before any retry CTA, zero-blame framing (a failed UPI debit is common and never assumed to be the buyer's fault).
> 5. 443 is retention-gentle: "Keep My Order" is given equal visual weight to cancelling, never buried beneath it; pre-accept cancellation is 100% refund with no fee.
> 6. 444 carries an explicit COMMENT GUARD distinguishing its order (#KV-2026-0251) from 131-buyer-order-track.html's own successful order (#KV-2026-0247) — the two must never be conflated despite the coincidental digit overlap with the unrelated #DSP-2026-0247 dispute ID (different ID namespace entirely).
> 7. 445/446 are a matched buyer/farmer pair on the same return case (#KV-2026-0198); reasons reuse `dispute_reason` vocabulary; farmer-side "Contest" routes into the SAME dispute pattern as 441 (`disputes.status` lifecycle), not a separate flow.
> 8. 447 is explicitly DISTINCT from 446: it is a LOGISTICS bounce-back (goods physically returned after 2 failed delivery attempts on #KV-2026-0251) — never a buyer-quality return like #KV-2026-0198's 445/446 flow. No-shame/no-blame framing throughout.
> 9. 448/449 are a matched buyer/farmer pair on the same partial-fulfilment case (#KV-2026-0201); 449 explicitly rewards declaring the shortfall BEFORE pickup over a buyer discovering it post-delivery — "declare-early" trust framing is the design intent, not merely a status label.
> 10. 437's resubmit flow applies no penalty/strike on rejection — there is no strikes/penalty column on `listings` or `farmer_profiles` today; tone is no-shame throughout (okra-story framing).

> QA — 14/15 screens clean on first pass; 1 canon-identity fix applied post-audit: **443's farmer name corrected to Ramesh Patel** (the platform's established recurring farmer-canon identity, consistent with 56/57/61 and every other batch's use of the same character) — buyer remains Rajesh Trader per 56's own canon. All Phase-1/prior-batch story continuations (99, 156, 15, 56, 131) re-verified exact against their source screens' stored figures; no drift found in any of the 5 continuation threads.
>
> Source: screens' own header comments (435–449), cross-referenced against `Database_Architecture` (listings/listing_offers/disputes/escrow_holds/order_status/shipments/payments/returns tables) and prior canon screens (99-buyer-make-offer, 156-tenant-dispute-detail, 15-checkout, 56-farmer-orders, 131-buyer-order-track, 12-my-listings, 79-tenant-listings, 147-tenant-farmer-approvals) for verbatim/format fidelity.

### 435-tenant-listing-moderation-queue

*435 · Listing Approvals — Krishi-Verse [TENANT] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Tenant-role, English-only (back-office convention, no bilingual wrapper). Lists rows WHERE `listings.status='pending_approval'` only. "SLA >24h" age is COMPUTED at query time from `listings.submitted_at` (`NOW() - submitted_at`) — there is no stored `sla_breached` flag. AI-grade confidence % reads from the existing AI quality-grading pipeline (same source as `kv-badge-ai` on 12-my-listings.html).
- Fair-band check compares `listings.price_per_unit` against the mandi fair-price range for that crop/mandi — same computation as `mo-fair` on 99-buyer-make-offer.html, also computed, not stored.
- API: proposed `GET /v1/tenant/listings/moderation-queue?status=pending_approval` · `GET /v1/listings/:id/ai-grade` (existing pipeline) · `GET /v1/mandi/fair-price-range` (reused calc)

### 436-tenant-listing-reject-detail

*436 · Review Listing — Krishi-Verse [TENANT] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Tenant-role, English-only. `listings.reject_reason` is FREE TEXT — no reason-code lookup table exists. The reason chips are a DESIGN-LEAD UX AFFORDANCE ONLY: tapping one pre-fills the free-text field; the DB still stores plain text, not an enum key.
- "Request changes" is a DESIGN-LEAD PROPOSED UX-ONLY BRANCH — it does not change `listings.status` (stays `pending_approval`) and only attaches an internal note; no separate DB status exists for it today. Case shown: Ramesh Patel's Okra listing (see 435 queue).
- API: proposed `POST /v1/tenant/listings/:id/reject` (writes free-text `reject_reason`, sets `status='rejected'`) · `POST /v1/tenant/listings/:id/request-changes` (UX-only note, no status change — BACKEND-PENDING as a real state)

### 437-farmer-listing-rejected-resubmit

*437 · Resubmit Listing — Krishi-Verse [FARMER] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Bilingual (mandatory wrapper — `.hi` spans present). `listings.status='rejected'`; `reject_reason` free text displayed verbatim (no reason-code table — see 436 for its tenant-side origin). Resubmitting moves status back to `pending_approval` on the SAME row, not a new listing.
- No penalty/strike is applied on rejection + resubmit — there is no strikes/penalty column on `listings` or `farmer_profiles` today. Tone: no-shame, encouraging (okra-story framing) — this is a routine correction, not a failure.
- API: proposed `POST /v1/farmer/listings/:id/resubmit` (writes updated fields, sets `status='pending_approval'`)

### 438-farmer-respond-to-offer

*438 · Respond to Offer — Krishi-Verse [FARMER] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- EXACT continuation of 99-buyer-make-offer.html (same offer, farmer side). Bilingual (mandatory wrapper). `listing_offers.status` ENUM `open | countered | accepted | rejected | expired | converted`; `round` + `counter_price_minor` track negotiation.
- Buyer's original offer ₹2,650/qtl × 2 qtl = ₹5,300, earnest lock ₹530 (10%), round=1 (`open`). Farmer counters at ₹2,780/qtl × 2 = ₹5,560 → round=2, `status='countered'`. New 10% earnest ₹556; buyer already locked ₹530, so top-up owed = ₹556 − ₹530 = ₹26. Max rounds (3) is a DESIGN-LEAD ASSUMPTION — not a stored cap, backend should confirm.
- API: proposed `GET /v1/listing-offers/:id` · `POST /v1/listing-offers/:id/counter` (writes `counter_price_minor`, increments `round`, sets `status='countered'`) · `POST /v1/listing-offers/:id/accept` / `/reject`

### 439-buyer-dispute-status-thread

*439 · Dispute #DSP-2026-0247 — Krishi-Verse [BUYER] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Buyer (Mehta Trading Co.) POV of the SAME dispute as 156-tenant-dispute-detail.html. Bilingual wrapper mandatory (escrow-safety strip carries a `.hi` span). `disputes.status` ENUM shows `under_review` here (seller has responded; admin has not yet proposed a resolution — that's sibling screen 441). `escrow_holds.amount` = ₹14,400 (5 qtl × ₹2,880) remains locked. `dispute_reason='poor_quality'`.
- ANTI-IDOR: servable only where `disputes.against_user`/complainant-respondent pair resolves server-side to the requesting user's own ID — enforced at the API/query layer, never by client-side role checks.
- API: proposed `GET /v1/disputes/:id/thread` (anti-IDOR server-side ownership check) · `GET /v1/escrow-holds/:id`

### 440-farmer-dispute-respond

*440 · Respond to Dispute — Krishi-Verse [FARMER] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Ramesh Patel's POV of #DSP-2026-0247 at the compose moment — before submission (`disputes.status` still `open`, `seller_respond_by` countdown running). Bilingual wrapper mandatory. Submitting transitions `open` → `seller_responded` (the thread state shown in 439/156).
- `seller_respond_by` is the SLA deadline column; if it lapses with no response, the case proceeds to admin review using only the buyer's evidence (fair-warning copy shown). Same server-side `against_user` ANTI-IDOR resolution as 439 — Ramesh can only load disputes where he is the resolved respondent, never by guessing IDs.
- API: proposed `POST /v1/disputes/:id/respond` (writes response + evidence, sets `status='seller_responded'`)

### 441-shared-dispute-resolution-accept-appeal

*441 · Resolution Proposed — Krishi-Verse [SHARED] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Same-pattern screen used by BOTH parties of #DSP-2026-0247 (buyer view shown; labels flip "to you"/"to seller" for the respondent's render). Bilingual wrapper mandatory. Accepting moves `disputes.status` → `resolved` (escrow release scheduled +24h). Appealing moves status → `escalated` — a VALID enum value, so the appeal path is enum-correct, not a UX-only branch.
- `resolution_type='refund_partial'` is a DESIGN-LEAD FIELD NAME (proposed values `refund_full | refund_partial | no_action | replacement`) pending backend ratification. Split: ₹2,200 to buyer + ₹12,200 to seller = ₹14,400 (full escrow, exact). "One appeal only" is a COPY COMMITMENT, not yet a stored appeal-count column.
- API: proposed `POST /v1/disputes/:id/accept-resolution` (sets `resolved`, schedules escrow release +24h) · `POST /v1/disputes/:id/appeal` (sets `escalated`)

### 442-shared-payment-failed-retry

*442 · Payment Failed — Krishi-Verse [SHARED] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Reuses 15-checkout.html's order EXACT (Premium Wheat Lokwan 2 qtl ₹5,760 + delivery ₹150 + GST ₹296 − discount ₹100 = ₹6,106). `payments.payment_status` ENUM includes `failed` plus refund states (full list pending backend ratification). `payments.failure_code` is FREE VARCHAR, gateway-style (`UPI_TIMEOUT`, `INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS`, `BANK_DECLINED`) — no fixed enum exists; shown as a designs-lead placeholder code.
- Tone: zero-blame, money-safety reassurance FIRST — a failed UPI debit is common and never assumed to be the buyer's fault; auto-refund language appears before any retry CTA.
- API: proposed `GET /v1/payments/:id/status` (reads `payment_status`/`failure_code`) · `POST /v1/payments/:id/retry`

### 443-buyer-order-cancel

*443 · Cancel Order — Krishi-Verse [BUYER] [P0 — D16 remediation] [canon fix applied: farmer identity → Ramesh Patel]*

- Order #KV-2026-0215 canon (56-farmer-orders.html: Rajesh Trader (buyer) · Yellow Maize 5 qtl · ₹9,550 · "⏱ 2 hours left" = farmer hasn't accepted yet). `order_status` ENUM includes `cancelled` (this action sets it). `cancel_reason` is a LOOKUP TYPE THAT EXISTS in schema but has ZERO SEEDED VALUES — the 4 reason chips are a DESIGNS-LEAD PROPOSED LIST pending backend/ops confirmation.
- Pre-accept cancellation = 100% refund, no fee. Tone: retention-gentle — "Keep My Order" is offered with equal visual weight to cancelling, never buried. Post-audit fix: farmer name corrected to Ramesh Patel (established canon identity) — buyer remains Rajesh Trader per 56's own canon.
- API: proposed `POST /v1/orders/:id/cancel` (writes `cancel_reason` from unseeded lookup, sets `order_status='cancelled'`, triggers 100% pre-accept refund)

### 444-buyer-delivery-failed-redeliver

*444 · Delivery Attempt Failed — Krishi-Verse [BUYER] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Extends 131-buyer-order-track.html's timeline chrome with an appended failed-attempt step. COMMENT GUARD: this is order #KV-2026-0251 (Ramesh Patel's wheat, 5 qtl, driver Hareshbhai · GJ-23-AB-7821) — a DIFFERENT order from 131's own #KV-2026-0247, which succeeded and is untouched by this screen.
- `shipments.status` ENUM includes `failed` (this attempt) — a subsequent farmer-side bounce-back to hub sets `shipments.status='returned'` (see 447). `order_status` is unaffected by a single failed attempt; it stays `in_transit` until redelivery or a return decision.
- API: proposed `GET /v1/shipments/:id/timeline` (extends 131's pattern with failed-attempt step) · `POST /v1/shipments/:id/redeliver`

### 445-buyer-order-return-request-tracker

*445 · Return Request — Krishi-Verse [BUYER] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Order #KV-2026-0198 canon (56-farmer-orders.html: Vadodara Mills · Yellow Maize 8 qtl · ₹15,280 · Delivered ✓; seller = Ramesh Patel, see 446). Unit price = ₹15,280 / 8 = ₹1,910/qtl. Buyer returns 2 of 8 qtl (`poor_quality`) → refund 2 × ₹1,910 = ₹3,820; seller keeps 6 × ₹1,910 = ₹11,460.
- NEEDS ENUM: `returns.status` is currently a comment-vocab VARCHAR (`requested | approved | in_transit | received | refunded | rejected`) — not yet a ratified Postgres ENUM. Return reasons REUSE the `dispute_reason` vocabulary (e.g. `poor_quality`) — no separate `return_reason` type exists; a designs-lead reuse decision, not DB truth.
- API: proposed `POST /v1/orders/:id/return-request` (writes to `returns`, `status='requested'`) · `GET /v1/returns/:id/tracker`

### 446-farmer-return-request-review

*446 · Review Return Request — Krishi-Verse [FARMER] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Ramesh Patel's side of #KV-2026-0198 (buyer request shown in 445): Vadodara Mills returning 2 of 8 qtl maize, reason `poor_quality`. Refund 2 × ₹1,910 = ₹3,820; Ramesh nets ₹11,460 (6 × ₹1,910).
- `returns.status` comment-vocab (needs ENUM, see 445). Contest routes into the SAME dispute pattern as 441 (`disputes.status` lifecycle), not a separate flow. Approving + confirming received goods triggers a `refund_txn` against escrow, which is what reduces Ramesh's payout on this order.
- API: proposed `POST /v1/returns/:id/approve` · `POST /v1/returns/:id/contest` (routes into disputes.status pattern) · `POST /v1/returns/:id/confirm-received` (triggers refund_txn against escrow)

### 447-farmer-returned-shipment-intake

*447 · Returned Shipment Intake (Logistics) — Krishi-Verse [FARMER] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- DISTINCT FROM 446: a LOGISTICS bounce-back — goods physically returned to the farmer after 2 failed delivery attempts on order #KV-2026-0251 (see 444), NOT a buyer-quality return like #KV-2026-0198 in 445/446. `shipments.status` ENUM = `returned` (this screen's state), following `failed` (444's first attempt).
- Buyer-charge note reflects a per-policy second-failure redelivery fee — DESIGNS-LEAD, not yet a ratified fee-schedule column. Tone: no-shame/no-blame throughout; farmer chooses relist / hold / dispose.
- API: proposed `POST /v1/shipments/:id/intake` (sets `shipments.status='returned'`) · `POST /v1/listings/:id/relist` · `POST /v1/shipments/:id/dispose`

### 448-buyer-shortage-reconciliation

*448 · Delivery Shortage — Krishi-Verse [BUYER] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Order #KV-2026-0201 canon (56-farmer-orders.html: Anand FPO · Wheat-Sharbati 10 qtl · ₹31,500 · In transit; seller = Ramesh Patel, see 449). Farmer declared before pickup he could only fulfil 8 of 10 qtl (yield shortfall). Shortfall = 2 qtl × ₹3,150/qtl = ₹6,300. Revised total = ₹31,500 − ₹6,300 = ₹25,200 exact.
- `order_status` ENUM includes `partially_fulfilled` (this order's state after farmer's declaration in 449). "Dispute Shortfall" routes into the same `disputes.status` pattern as 441.
- API: proposed `GET /v1/orders/:id/reconciliation` (reads `partially_fulfilled` state + revised total) · `POST /v1/orders/:id/dispute-shortfall` (routes into disputes.status pattern)

### 449-farmer-mark-partial-fulfilment

*449 · Mark Partial Fulfilment — Krishi-Verse [FARMER] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Ramesh Patel declares BEFORE pickup that order #KV-2026-0201 (Anand FPO, Wheat-Sharbati 10 qtl @ ₹3,150 = ₹31,500) can only be fulfilled at 8 qtl. Shortfall = 2 qtl × ₹3,150 = ₹6,300; revised total = ₹31,500 − ₹6,300 = ₹25,200 exact.
- `order_status` ENUM includes `partially_fulfilled` — this screen's confirm action sets it, mirroring the buyer's view in 448. Declaring pre-pickup (vs. a buyer discovering it post-delivery) is the honesty-first, declare-early trust path this screen is designed to encourage.
- API: proposed `POST /v1/orders/:id/mark-partial-fulfilment` (writes actual qty, computes shortfall/revised total, sets `order_status='partially_fulfilled'`)

---

## P0 Additions · Vet Role — screens 450–460 [D16 REMEDIATION · NEW ROLE] [batch #3 of the D16-audit P0 remediation plan; 11 screens, 1 brand-new role (Vet); closes the supply-side gap for farmer screens 205–207 — Krishi-Verse's first veterinarian-facing app: registration/VCI verification, booking queue with emergency triage, in-consult exam, prescription writing, consult-complete fee collection, earnings, services/rates management, an availability calendar fused with the AI-technician worklist, and profile]

> **Founder-approved 5-tab nav (2026-07-08) — 451 is the pattern-setting nav-gate screen for this new role, same gate process as D12/D13:**
> Home (451) · Bookings (452 tab root; 453/454/455/456 flow sub-screens) · Earnings (457 tab root) · Schedule (459 tab root, availability slots fused with the date-driven AI-technician/PD worklist) · Profile (460 tab root; 458 services/rates nests under it as a sub-page). Justification carried verbatim from 451's own header comment: (1) Home + Bookings earn the first two continuous-use slots — a vet checks her queue and accepts/declines emergency requests multiple times an hour, same reasoning as 401-transporter-home's Trips tab; (2) Earnings gets its own tab, not nested under Profile, because same-day settlement confidence is a distinct daily anxiety for a gig-style clinical worker, mirroring 390-partner-provider-earnings / 405-transporter-earnings precedent; (3) Schedule is the 4th tab (not buried 5th) because her open availability slots directly gate what farmers can book (206/386) AND the same calendar carries her date-driven AI-technician worklist (insemination_records.pd_due_date follow-ups) — one calendar surface, not two; (4) Profile is last per platform convention, with 458 (service rates, changes rarely) nested under it as a sub-page rather than earning continuous top-level real estate.

> **Canon — supply-side continuity with farmer screens 205–207 (verified exact, not re-invented):**
> Dr. Foram Vaghela, BVSc & AH, VCI reg. **GJ-VCI-14392**, rating_avg **4.8★**, `is_ai_technician=true`, `is_emergency_available=true`, base region Vanthali / Junagadh district, today Fri 3 Jul 2026. Her booking KV-VET-2026-0702-041 is the SAME booking farmer's 207 already shows: Ganga (Gir cow), owner Ramesh Patel, Vanthali — vaccination visit, urgency=routine, mode=visit, fee **₹400 = ₹250 visit + ₹150 dose**; requested 2 Jul 6:10 PM → accepted 6:25 PM → en_route (arriving ~9:40 AM) → in_consult 9:42 AM → prescribed → completed 10:04 AM (24-min visit) — timeline and the farmer's note ("Due for FMD booster, otherwise eating and milking normally.") are carried byte-for-byte across 451/452/453/454/455/456. Thursday morning camp slot is locked/not-bookable on 459, consistent with the 201/205 free-camp canon.
> New minor canon introduced this batch (vet-side only, does not alter 205–207's own record): Moti (buffalo), owner Jasuben Chavda, Keshod — emergency bloat, fee ₹500. Kalu (goat kid), owner Prakash Rabari — tele consult, fee ₹200 (gap-flagged — no published tele-rate `vet_services` row exists yet). Gauri (Gir cow) — an `insemination_records` PD-check worklist task, `pd_due_date` 28 Sep 2026, sourcing the SAME Gauri used in the 203/204 dairy canon; this is HER own task, not a farmer-initiated `vet_booking`.

> **Consult → Rx per-line guard demo (455):** booking KV-VET-2026-0702-041's prescription carries two lines that demonstrate the `is_schedule_h` PER-LINE (not whole-prescription) guard: Line 1 FMD booster vaccine, `is_schedule_h=true` ("Restricted — vet-administered only", copy verbatim from 207), batch **FMD-GJ-2026-B214** (a NEW batch — distinct from the 15-May dose-1 batch FMD-GJ-2026-B117 referenced on 207/453), `next_due` auto-chip **3 Jan 2027** (+6 months, a new dose event resetting the clock). Line 2 Meloxicam (post-vaccination antipyretic), `is_schedule_h=false`, orderable-from-pharma product link. `digital_signature_ref` mono stamp: Dr. Foram Vaghela, GJ-VCI-14392. Valid until 3 Aug 2026 (30-day standard Rx validity).

> **Earnings — July 46 jobs, ₹14,850 EXACT (457):** 17 vaccination-visits × ₹400 + 15 consults × ₹250 + 6 AI-insemination × ₹300 + 3 emergency × ₹500 + 5 tele-consult × ₹200 = ₹6,800 + ₹3,750 + ₹1,800 + ₹1,500 + ₹1,000 = **₹14,850**; 17+15+6+3+5 = **46 jobs** — both match 451-vet-home's hero exactly. Today so far **₹650** (Ganga ₹400 + an earlier routine consult ₹250). YTD **₹1,02,400** (illustrative season total, lakh grouping, same convention as 390/405).

> **DB TRUTH — REAL schema this batch reads/writes today (08_livestock_dairy.sql unless noted):**
> 1. `vet_profiles` — `user_id` PK/FK `users(id)`, `degree`, `vci_registration_no`, `rating_avg numeric(2,1)`, `consult_count int`, `is_ai_technician boolean`, `is_emergency_available boolean`, `base_region varchar`. Backs 450/451/460.
> 2. `vet_services` — `id uuid PK, vet_id FK vet_profiles(user_id), service_type_id/type, price_minor bigint NOT NULL, pricing_unit varchar(15) NOT NULL, is_active boolean, created_at, updated_at`. `pricing_unit` CHECK **EXACT 4 values** `per_visit | per_dose | per_animal | per_minute` — do not invent a 5th without founder approval. Full CRUD surface on 458. WRINKLE: Emergency is not its own pricing_unit — it is a flat call-out fee stored as `per_visit` (same unit as Consult), gated by `vet_profiles.is_emergency_available` (a vet-level boolean, not a per-service column).
> 3. `vet_bookings` — 8-value `vet_booking_status` ENUM EXACT `requested | accepted | en_route | in_consult | prescribed | completed | cancelled | no_show`; `urgency` `emergency | urgent | routine`; `mode` `visit | tele`; `fee_minor`, `payment_id uuid REFERENCES payments(id)`, `completed_at`. `ai_triage` jsonb — **FIRST UI EXPOSURE anywhere in the system**, surfaced on 453 as advisory-only text ("Routine ✓ suggested… human decides" note); never replaces the vet's own clinical judgment.
> 4. `prescriptions` + `prescription_items` — `is_schedule_h boolean` (per-line restricted-drug guard), `digital_signature_ref` (mono stamp). Demoed on 455 with one `is_schedule_h=true` line and one `false` line in the SAME prescription.
> 5. `insemination_records` — `performed_at`, `pd_due_date` — backs 459's AI-technician worklist strip (Gauri, PD check).
> 6. `disease_outbreaks` — referenced for 451's regional-advisory strip context (read-only this batch, not a primary write surface).
> 7. `reviews.target_type` (04_commerce.sql, varchar, NOT a hard CHECK enum) already lists `seller|buyer|product|delivery_partner|course|worker|farmer_employer` as examples; `target_type='farmer'` (456, the first vet-rates-farmer surface) is a compatible natural addition needing no schema change, flagged for backend confirmation.
> 8. `payments`/`payouts` (05_money.sql / 01_phase1_schema.sql) — 456's cash-in-hand toggle leaves `vet_bookings.payment_id` NULL (no gateway event); `fee_minor` + `completed_at` alone stand as the settlement record for offline-cash, the same pattern used for `service_bookings` platform-wide.

> **CONCEPT / BACKEND-PENDING schema (no backing tables — design proposals only, flagged for the backend team, not yet in Database_Architecture):**
> 1. **`vet_availability_slots`** — NO vet-availability table exists today (unlike labour's real `worker_availability`, see 356-worker-worker-availability-calendar.html). PROPOSED on 459, mirroring `worker_availability`'s shape but adapted for a recurring weekly template (a vet's week is structurally regular — fixed farm-round mornings — unlike day-labour's fully ad-hoc pattern): `id uuid PK, vet_id FK vet_profiles(user_id), day_of_week smallint CHECK (0–6), day_part varchar(10) CHECK ('morning','afternoon'), slot_type varchar(20) CHECK ('farm_rounds','tele_ai','camp','off'), is_bookable boolean DEFAULT true (false for the locked Thursday camp slot), label text`.
> 2. **Vet payout purpose** — no dedicated vet-payout table exists; 457's figures are DERIVED by summing `vet_bookings.fee_minor` WHERE `status='completed'` per vet_id. Settlement reuses the generic `payouts` table, whose `payout_purpose` lookup (`settlement|wage|commission|refund|milk_bill|loan_disbursal|claim`) has NO `vet_fee` value today — adding one is PROPOSED, pending backend confirmation.
> 3. **Tele session table** — no `vet_tele_sessions`/call-log table exists. When `vet_bookings.mode='tele'` (e.g. Kalu's booking), 454's in-consult screen would need a call/video UI instead of the exam checklist shown — flagged as a schema gap on 454, not fully designed.
> 4. **Tele rate row** — no published tele-consult `vet_services` row exists yet for Dr. Vaghela; the ₹200 shown on Kalu's request cards (451/452) and proposed for publishing on 458 is a designs-lead placeholder pending a real catalog entry for `mode='tele'`.
> 5. **`vet_profiles.service_radius_km` / `hosts_free_camp`** — no such columns exist today; "Service area · 25 km radius" and "Free camp hosting" on 460 are PROPOSED illustrative defaults, same gap-flagging pattern as items 1–4.
> 6. **`ai_triage` exposure contract** — the jsonb shape/backend source behind `ai_triage` is undetermined; 453 renders it defensively as advisory-only text rather than structured UI, pending a ratified contract.

> **E-audit closure notes:**
> 1. `cancelled` and `no_show` vet-side rendering had no example anywhere in the D19 canon set — closed with a small demo legend on 452 (muted cards, explicitly labeled "Demo cards — no_show and cancelled don't occur in today's queue," never presented as live canon bookings).
> 2. 453 renders its cancel action as an inline expandable reason-comment block (per spec: "cancelled state renders comment") rather than a separate screen, keeping booking detail a single coherent view.

> **201-vs-207 FMD canon tension (pre-existing, flagged not fixed here — carried to D25):**
> Ganga has two FMD vaccination records in prior canon: a 20 Nov 2025 camp dose (screens 200/201, which drives a "20-May-overdue-booster" reading) AND a 15 May 2026 "dose 1" (screen 207's prior-visit card, next due 15 Nov 2026). 453 treats these as separate records/courses and carries an explicit canon note flagging the tension for reconciliation in the **D25 final regression audit** — this batch documents the discrepancy, it does not resolve it.

> QA — 11/11 structural pass; nav byte-identical across all 5 tab-root screens (451, 452, 457, 459, 460); 2 fixes applied post-audit: **451** had 3 broken hrefs repaired (nav/CTA links corrected to point at the real D19 screen filenames); **453**'s overdue-booster chip copy was clarified against 201 canon and a canon-tension note was added flagging the pre-existing 201-vs-207 FMD discrepancy for D25 — a documentation clarification, not a figures/data fix.
>
> Source: screens' own header comments (450–460), cross-referenced against `Database_Architecture` (08_livestock_dairy.sql: vet_profiles, vet_services, vet_bookings, prescriptions/prescription_items, insemination_records, disease_outbreaks; 05_money.sql/01_phase1_schema.sql: payments/payouts; 04_commerce.sql: reviews) and Phase-1/prior-batch canon (200/201, 203/204, 205-farmer-vet-directory, 206-farmer-vet-booking, 207-farmer-vet-booking-detail, 356-worker-worker-availability-calendar, 390-partner-provider-earnings, 401-transporter-home, 405-transporter-earnings, 434-worker-registration-onboarding) for verbatim/format fidelity.

### 450-vet-registration-onboarding

*450 · Vet Registration — Krishi-Verse [VET] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE]*

- Back-header, no nav — entry flow precedes 451's bottom-nav home. Pattern source: 434-worker-registration-onboarding.html (hero → sections → KYC framing → success). Applicant Dr. Foram Vaghela, BVSc & AH, target VCI reg. GJ-VCI-14392, base region Vanthali/Junagadh. Touches `vet_profiles` (`vci_registration_no`, degree doc → `kyc_documents`, `is_ai_technician`, `base_region`, `service_radius_km` default 25) + draft `vet_services` rows (service_type, pricing_unit, price_minor).
- `pricing_unit` ENUM EXACT 4 values `per_visit|per_dose|per_animal|per_minute`. Vets do not self-list instantly — invite-or-application intake, reviewed by tenant admin + VCI registry check before `vet_profiles.is_verified`.
- API: proposed `POST /v1/vets/register` (writes vet_profiles + draft vet_services rows) · existing KYC document endpoints per 434's pattern

Hub: emergency-first queue snapshot, AI-technician follow-up strip (disambiguated from the platform's other "AI"=artificial intelligence badge — spelled out on first use, no purple `kv-badge-ai` used here), regional advisory, earnings snapshot (₹14,850 / 46 jobs, matches 457 exactly), Thursday free-camp reminder.
- Canon: Dr. Foram Vaghela, GJ-VCI-14392, 4.8★, `is_ai_technician=true`, emergency-available. Queue mirrors 452's top cards (Ganga en_route, Moti emergency, Kalu tele gap-flagged ₹200).

Canon cards: Moti (buffalo, Jasuben Chavda, Keshod) — bloat, emergency, requested 7:52 AM, ₹500, accept/decline + 30-min response nudge; Ganga (Ramesh Patel, Vanthali) — KV-VET-2026-0702-041, routine, en_route, arriving ~9:40 AM, ₹400 (start-consult CTA transitions en_route→in_consult, opens 454); Kalu (goat kid, Prakash Rabari) — tele, requested, ₹200 (tele-rate gap flag); one earlier completed routine consult, ₹250, settled.

*453 · Vet Booking Detail — Krishi-Verse [VET] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE] [canon clarification applied + D25 tension flag]*

- Back-header, no nav — vet-side mirror of farmer's 207. KV-VET-2026-0702-041 lifecycle carried exact (requested 2 Jul 6:10 PM → accepted 6:25 PM → en_route). Farmer note verbatim: "Due for FMD booster, otherwise eating and milking normally." Health-history chips: FMD dose 1 15 May ✓ (batch FMD-GJ-2026-B117, next due 15 Nov per 207) · overdue booster since 20 May · in-milk 8.0 L/day.

*454 · Vet In-Consult — Krishi-Verse [VET] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE]*

- Back-header, no nav. `status=in_consult`. Ganga's exam: consult started 9:42 AM, temp 101.2°F (normal cattle range ~101–102.5°F), appetite ✓, udder ✓, BCS 3.5 (body condition score, 1–5). Vaccine administration: FMD booster, batch FMD-GJ-2026-B214 (NEW batch, distinct from dose-1 batch FMD-GJ-2026-B117 on 207/453), subcutaneous neck site.

### 455-vet-prescription-writing

*455 · Vet Prescription Writing — Krishi-Verse [VET] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE]*

- Back-header, no nav. Status transitions `in_consult`→`prescribed`. PER-LINE `is_schedule_h` GUARD DEMO: Line 1 FMD booster, `is_schedule_h=true` ("Restricted — vet-administered only", verbatim 207 copy), batch FMD-GJ-2026-B214, `next_due` auto-chip 3 Jan 2027 (+6 months — a NEW dose event resetting the clock, distinct from the 15 Nov 2026 next-due on the OLDER 15 May dose-1 record). Line 2 Meloxicam (post-vaccination antipyretic), `is_schedule_h=false`, orderable-from-pharma product link.
- `digital_signature_ref` mono stamp: Dr. Foram Vaghela, GJ-VCI-14392. Valid until 3 Aug 2026 (30-day standard Rx validity).

### 456-vet-consult-complete-fee

*456 · Vet Consult Complete & Fee — Krishi-Verse [VET] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE]*

- Back-header, no nav. Transitions `prescribed`→`completed`, `completed_at` stamped. Fee collection shown with cash-in-hand (the most common rural pattern) pre-toggled ON, settled state visible in the same scroll — a single coherent "closing out the visit" screen. Visit began 9:40 AM, prescription completed 10:04 AM (24-min duration). Fee ₹400 = ₹250 visit + ₹150 dose (exact mirror of 451/455/207/453). Next dose due 3 Jan 2027 (matches 455's chip exactly).
- `payment_id` is populated only for gateway-mediated methods once `payments.status='captured'`; the cash-received toggle leaves `payment_id` NULL — `fee_minor` + `completed_at` alone stand as the settlement record (same offline pattern as `service_bookings` platform-wide). `reviews.target_type='farmer'` flagged as the first vet-rates-farmer surface, a compatible non-schema-changing addition pending backend confirmation.
- API: proposed `POST /v1/vet/bookings/:id/complete` (sets `status='completed'`, `completed_at`, `fee_minor`, optional `payment_id`) · `POST /v1/reviews` (`target_type='farmer'`, pending backend confirmation)

### 457-vet-earnings

*457 · Vet Earnings — Krishi-Verse [VET] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE]*

- TAB ROOT (Earnings), bottom nav byte-identical to 451 with `aria-current` moved. Follows 390-partner-provider-earnings / 405-transporter-earnings conventions (gradient summary hero, breakdown rows, today strip, rating-impact row, YTD, tax-simple note, NO wallet balance).
- ARITHMETIC EXACT: 17×₹400 + 15×₹250 + 6×₹300 + 3×₹500 + 5×₹200 = ₹6,800+₹3,750+₹1,800+₹1,500+₹1,000 = ₹14,850 (46 jobs) — matches 451's hero exactly. Today ₹650 (Ganga ₹400 + earlier ₹250). YTD ₹1,02,400 (illustrative, lakh grouping per 390/405 convention).
- DB TRUTH GAP: no dedicated vet-payout table — figures DERIVED by summing `vet_bookings.fee_minor` WHERE `status='completed'` per vet_id. Settlement reuses the generic `payouts` table; its `payout_purpose` lookup has no `vet_fee` value today (proposed here).
- API: proposed `GET /v1/vet/earnings?period=...` (derived aggregate, no payout table backing)

### 458-vet-services-rates-manage

*458 · Vet Services & Rates — Krishi-Verse [VET] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE]*

- Back-header sub-page, nests under Profile (460) per 451's nav justification #4 (rates change rarely). Full CRUD on the REAL `vet_services` table: `id, vet_id FK vet_profiles(user_id), service_type_id/type, price_minor NOT NULL, pricing_unit NOT NULL, is_active, created_at, updated_at`. `pricing_unit` CHECK EXACT 4 values `per_visit|per_dose|per_animal|per_minute` — do not invent a 5th without founder approval.
- EMERGENCY WRINKLE: Emergency is not its own pricing_unit — a flat call-out fee stored `per_visit` (same unit as Consult), gated by `vet_profiles.is_emergency_available` (vet-level boolean, not per-service) — the Emergency row's toggle edits `vet_profiles`, not this row's `pricing_unit`. TELE-RATE GAP: proposes an unpublished ₹200 tele row — saving it CREATES the real row and clears the gap flag seen on 451/452/457.
- API: proposed `GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /v1/vet/services` (full CRUD) · `GET /v1/service-types?category=vet_service` (lookup)

### 459-vet-availability-calendar

*459 · Vet Availability Calendar — Krishi-Verse [VET] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE]*

- TAB ROOT (Schedule), bottom nav byte-identical to 451. ONE surface for two obligations: open availability slots (gate what 206/386 let farmers book) AND the date-driven AI-technician/PD worklist (`insemination_records.pd_due_date`, vaccination next-dose reminders) — both are "what does my calendar require of me" questions.
- Thursday morning LOCKED (free camp, not bookable) — consistent with 201/205 canon; Thursday afternoon open. Worklist: Gauri (Gir cow) — `performed_at` 30 Jun 2026 ✓, `pd_due_date` 28 Sep 2026 pending PD check (HER own task, auto-books her 28 Sep morning slot, not farmer-initiated); Ganga — FMD booster next due 3 Jan 2027, reminder only (matches 455/456 exactly).
- NO-TABLE GAP: no `vet_availability_slots` table exists (unlike labour's real `worker_availability`, 356). PROPOSED, mirroring `worker_availability`'s shape adapted for a recurring weekly template: `id, vet_id, day_of_week smallint CHECK (0–6), day_part CHECK ('morning','afternoon'), slot_type CHECK ('farm_rounds','tele_ai','camp','off'), is_bookable boolean DEFAULT true, label text`.
- API: proposed `GET/POST /v1/vet/availability-slots` (backend-pending table) · `GET /v1/vet/worklist` (derives from insemination_records + vet_bookings next-dose)

### 460-vet-profile-settings

*460 · Vet Profile — Krishi-Verse [VET] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE — LAST screen of the role, 451–460 complete]*

- TAB ROOT (Profile), bottom nav byte-identical to 451. 458 nests here as a sub-page chevron row (rates change rarely, per 451's justification #4). 292-DIVERGENCE NOTE (intentional, not an inconsistency): unlike 292's separate surveyor-ID pill, the VCI registration number stays INLINE in the profile meta line ("BVSc & AH · GJ-VCI-14392"), consistent with 451's own precedent — the same identity fact repeats across many surfaces (queue cards, directory 205, this profile), so an inline treatment reads as professional credentialing rather than a one-time trust-badge moment.
- DB TRUTH: `vet_profiles` (`user_id`, `degree`, `vci_registration_no`, `rating_avg`, `consult_count`, `is_ai_technician`, `is_emergency_available`, `base_region`). GAP (mirrors 451/457/458/459 pattern): no `service_radius_km`/`hosts_free_camp` columns exist today — "Service area · 25 km radius" and "Free camp hosting" are PROPOSED illustrative defaults pending backend confirmation.
- API: proposed `GET/PATCH /v1/vet/profile` · `GET /v1/vet/services` (links to 458)
