## P0 Additions · Warehouse Operator Role — screens 461–470 [D16 REMEDIATION · NEW ROLE · LAST ZERO-COVERAGE ROLE CLOSED] [batch #4 of the D16-audit P0 remediation plan; 10 screens, 1 brand-new role (Warehouse Operator); closes the supply-side gap for farmer screens 408/409 — the SECOND and LAST zero-coverage role identified by the D16 audit (Vet closed in D19, Warehouse Operator closes here): registration/WDRA verification, home, a fused Intake tab (booking queue → goods intake → assay record), a fused Stock tab (occupancy dashboard + release authorization), eNWR issuance, and profile with earnings deliberately nested as a sub-page]

> **Founder-approved 5-tab nav (2026-07-08) — 462 is the pattern-setting nav-gate screen for this new role, same gate process as D12/D13/D19:**
> Home (462) · Intake (463 tab root; 464 goods-in + 465 assay sub-screens) · Stock (468 tab root; 467 release sub-screen) · eNWR (466 tab root) · Profile (470 tab root; 469 earnings nests here as a sub-page). Justification carried verbatim from 462's own header comment: (1) Intake fuses 463/464/465 into ONE tab because "a truck arrives → gets confirmed → goods are weighed in → assay is drawn" is a single continuous physical workflow for a godown clerk — the same "one obligation surface" logic as 451-vet-home's Bookings tab (booking → consult → prescription, one tab); (2) Stock fuses 468/467 into ONE tab because occupancy and release both answer "what is physically sitting in my godown right now, and what is leaving" — two views of the same inventory-on-hand question, not two separate daily anxieties; (3) eNWR earns its OWN top-level tab, never nested under Stock or Intake, because it is a distinct regulatory/legal artefact (negotiable instrument, pledge-eligible) that farmers and banks reference independently of physical stock state — the same continuous-top-level-visibility reasoning that earned 457-vet-earnings its own tab; (4) Earnings (469) is DELIBERATELY **NOT** a tab — a documented divergence from the vet precedent (451/457, where Earnings IS a tab root): a vet's fee settles per booking, multiple times a day, but a warehouse operator's `storage_fee` only crystallizes at an episodic `release()` event (may be days/weeks/months after intake), so most days the number does not move at all; giving it a continuous tab slot would manufacture a false sense of daily activity for a number that is honestly static most days. Earnings therefore nests under Profile (470) as a low-frequency-glance ledger, and Home (462) instead surfaces a lightweight "July so far" snapshot card so the figure stays visible without a dedicated tab.

> **Canon — supply-side continuity with farmer screens 408/409 (verified exact, not re-invented):**
> Paresh Thakkar, Warehouse Operator, Junagadh AgriStore, WDRA reg. **WDRA/GJ/00214**, capacity **500 MT**, `storage_kinds` `['ambient','cold_0_4']`, member of both NERL and CCRL repositories. RATE-PER-KIND GAP (flagged identically on 461/462/468/470, same warehouse used farmer-side): `warehouses.rate_per_qtl_month_minor` is a SINGLE bigint field, but this warehouse publishes TWO live per-kind rates — ambient **₹28**/qtl/month, cold **₹32**/qtl/month (the exact ₹32 rate farmer Ramesh Patel saw and booked against on 409-farmer-cold-storage-booking.html); a single scalar column cannot hold two rates at once, flagged not silently resolved. Occupancy: 500 MT capacity, **310 MT occupied (62%)** — ambient 260/380 MT (68%), cold 50/120 MT (42%), 190 MT free; the per-kind chamber split is a DESIGNS-LEAD placeholder (no `warehouse_chambers` table exists), and 468's cold-chamber temperature-log view references a `cold_chain_logs` table that is a PHANTOM REFERENCE — it does not exist anywhere in Database_Architecture (grepped across all of 05–13 full_platform/*.sql, zero hits).
> Ramesh Patel's Feb wheat NWR — **NERL-ENWR-2026-088412**, 8 qtl, **₹19,600** (8 × ₹2,450/qtl), status=issued, carried verbatim from 408-farmer-warehouse-receipt.html; its assay (moisture 11.2%, FM 0.8%, broken 2.1%) is reproduced byte-for-byte as a "completed reference" card on 465. Ramesh's veg cold-storage booking (409: 3 qtl leafy vegetables, cold, expected arrival 10 Jul 2026, ₹48 = 3 qtl × ₹32/qtl/month × 0.5 month) is the SAME `storage_bookings` row observed two days later from the operator side: 409 captures the moment of request (step 1 "Requested" active, steps 2–4 not yet reached); 462/463 show the same row two days on, already reviewed and confirmed by Paresh, status "confirmed ✓" — a documented TIME-LAPSE CONTINUITY NOTE (same row, two roles, two timestamps), not a canon contradiction, pre-emptively noted to forestall a false QA mismatch flag (same device as 462-vet-batch's own precedent for cross-role time-lapse).

> **NEW canon chain introduced this batch (vet-side precedent, does not alter 408/409's own record):**
> Kanji Bhalala, 12 qtl cotton — an intake ceremony spanning three fused-tab screens: 463 (status=confirmed, arrival today 8 Jul) → 464 (goods intake: weigh-in verification against the declared 12 qtl, "Ambient chamber A2" placeholder assignment, `storage_bookings.status` confirmed→stored) → 465 (assay: moisture 8.2%, trash/FM 3.1%, staple 27.5mm, Grade A shankar-6; assayer "Paresh Thakkar (in-house)"; `valid_until` = 8 Jul + 90 days = **6 Oct 2026** exact) → 466 (eNWR issuance: repository CCRL selected, quantity 12 qtl, MANUAL valuation ₹7,150/qtl × 12 = **₹85,800** auto-calculated, producing `enwr_no` **CCRL-ENWR-2026-091730**). Naresh Bhatti, 15 qtl groundnut, ambient — status=requested on 463, capacity-check chip "15 qtl fits ambient ✓", awaiting Paresh's confirm/decline (not carried further this batch). Junagadh Traders, 95 qtl groundnut, ambient, stored 12 May 2026, NWR NERL-ENWR-2026-089950 (issued, unpledged) — released on 467: fee = 95 qtl × ₹28/qtl/month × CEIL(2 months) = **₹5,320** (12 May→12 Jun = 1 complete month, 12 Jun→8 Jul a partial second month → CEIL 2; 95×28=2,660×2=5,320) — the REAL formula sourced from `storage-booking.entity.ts`, posting as a `storage_fee` wallet leg only at the moment `storage_bookings.status`→`released`. Earnings arc (469, snapshot-timing reconciliation vs 462's morning read): prior-to-today subtotal ambient 210 qtl-mo×₹28=₹5,880 + cold 15 qtl-mo×₹32=₹480 = **₹6,360** (462's 9:41 AM read, BEFORE the release) → +₹5,320 today's release = **₹11,680** July so far (469's later read, AFTER the release) — same ledger, two timestamps, not a contradiction. YTD 2026 **₹1,84,900** (illustrative season total, lakh grouping, same convention as 390/405/457). Staff: Bhavesh Koli, loader/weighman — cosmetic-only row on 470, no backing staff table.

> **DB TRUTH — REAL schema this batch reads/writes today (09_agri_infra_services.sql unless noted):**
> 1. `warehouses` — `wdra_reg_no varchar(60)`, `address_id → addresses`, `capacity_mt numeric`, `storage_kinds jsonb`, `rate_per_qtl_month_minor bigint` (SINGLE field, see rate-per-kind gap below), `insurance_policy_ref varchar(120)`, `operator_user_id → users`, `is_active`. One operator ↔ one warehouse in this canon (multi-warehouse operators out of scope for this batch). Backs 461/462/470.
> 2. `storage_bookings.status` varchar(20) DEFAULT `requested` — 6-vocab EXACT `requested | confirmed | stored | partially_released | released | cancelled`, no invented states. Drives 463/464/467/468.
> 3. `assay_reports` — `storage_booking_id → storage_bookings`, `assayer_name varchar`, `parameters jsonb` (commodity-shaped: cotton = moisture/trash-FM/staple-length, wheat = moisture/FM/broken — same column, two different commodity-appropriate shapes, by design), `grade_option_id → attribute_options`, `report_media_id → media_assets`, `assayed_at timestamptz`, `valid_until date`. Backs 465.
> 4. `nwr_receipts` / `nwr_status` ENUM EXACT 6 values `issued | pledged | partially_released | released | cancelled | defaulted`. Backs 466/467.
> 5. `payments`/`payouts` (05_money.sql) — 467's release fee posts as a `storage_fee` wallet leg at the moment `storage_bookings.status`→`released` — no gateway event required (same offline-settlement pattern used platform-wide).

> **CONCEPT / BACKEND-PENDING schema (no backing tables — design proposals only, flagged for the backend team, not yet in Database_Architecture):**
> 1. **Rate-per-kind table** — `warehouses.rate_per_qtl_month_minor` is a single bigint; this warehouse needs two live rates (ambient ₹28, cold ₹32) simultaneously. PROPOSED: a `warehouse_storage_rates` table keyed `(warehouse_id, storage_kind)`, or `storage_kinds` jsonb enriched with a per-kind `rate_minor`. Flagged on 461/462/468/470.
> 2. **`warehouse_chambers` table** — no table subdivides `capacity_mt` per storage kind; the 260/380 ambient / 50/120 cold split (462/464/468) is a DESIGNS-LEAD illustrative placeholder pending either a `warehouse_chambers(warehouse_id, storage_kind, capacity_mt)` table or a computed split once `storage_bookings` gains a `storage_kind` column (it currently only has `product_id` + `quantity`, no explicit FK back to `storage_kinds`).
> 3. **`cold_chain_logs`** — PHANTOM REFERENCE: 468's cold-chamber temperature-log view names a `cold_chain_logs` table that does not exist anywhere in Database_Architecture (grepped 05–13 full_platform/*.sql, zero hits) — flagged, not silently modelled.
> 4. **Weighbridge** — no `weighbridge_readings` table exists; 464's "declared vs weighed" comparison is a UI-only verification step for operator confidence before storing — only the confirmed quantity persists to `storage_bookings`.
> 5. **Staff table** — no `warehouse_staff` (or similar) table with role/shift/contact columns exists; Bhavesh Koli's row on 470 is cosmetic scaffolding only, tapping it has nowhere real to go until such a table exists — same treatment class as other cosmetic-only staff rows across the design system.
> 6. **Manual valuation / auto-valuation service** — `nwr_receipts.valuation_minor` (466) is populated by the OPERATOR typing a per-qtl rate; no automated mandi-price-lookup × quantity service exists. A real, load-bearing gap: two operators could value the same lot differently until a real pricing service exists.
> 7. **`release_requests` table** — no dedicated table exists in `09_agri_infra_services.sql`; a holder's "Request release" tap in their own app is modelled (467) as an inbound notification/message to the operator, not a persisted, state-tracked DB row. The only real state-changing event is Paresh's own Authorize action.
> 8. **`gate_passes` table** — "Print gate pass" (467) is a UI-only physical/paper handoff action for goods leaving the godown gate, not a persisted row.
> 9. **`storage_fee` payout purpose** — no dedicated warehouse-earnings table exists; 469's figures are DERIVED by summing the `storage_fee` wallet leg posted against `storage_bookings` rows for this `warehouse_id` at `released`/`partially_released`. Settlement is proposed to reuse the generic `payouts` table with a new `storage_fee` purpose value — identical gap shape to 457-vet-earnings' proposed `vet_fee` value.
> 10. **Repository membership** — NERL/CCRL membership is not a `warehouses` column in this schema pass; modelled (461) as onboarding-intake metadata pending a `warehouse_repository_memberships` join table.

> **BACKEND-DEFERRED discipline (a defining feature of this batch — muted demo legends carrying explicit state-machine quotes, never silent omissions):**
> 1. `storage_bookings.status` 6-vocab: this batch's live queue only ever shows `requested`/`confirmed` (463) and drives `confirmed`→`stored` (464) and `stored`→`released` (467). `partially_released` has NO reachable UI transition anywhere in this screen set — demoed on 467 as a muted legend card quoting the enum inline, same 452-vet-booking-queue-precedent "render the pattern, label it demo" treatment. `cancelled` is demoed as a muted legend row on 463 (explicitly labeled non-canon, "doesn't occur in today's queue").
> 2. `nwr_status` 6-enum: this batch's screens (466 issuance, 467 release) only ever produce/observe `issued` and `released`/`partially_released` as forward flow. Following the same discipline as `storage-booking.state.ts`/`nwr-receipt.state.ts`-style state-machine comments, the legends are quoted inline as **"not reachable in this build"** — `pledged` (a farmer action against a KCC-style loan product, see 408's pledge card — a hypothetical not-yet-taken loan) and `defaulted` (loan default against a pledged receipt) have no reachable UI transition anywhere in this screen set, quoted so QA does not mistake "not shown" for "not modelled."
> 3. PLEDGE = HARD GUARD on 467, not a demo card: a bilingual `role="note"` warning block reads "Pledged NWR lots cannot release without lender NOC · गिरवी रसीद बिना ऋणदाता NOC के रिलीज़ नहीं होगी" — if `nwr_status = pledged`, Authorize is blocked until a lender no-objection-certificate step clears it. No lot in today's canon is pledged, so the guard has no reachable demo state; it is flagged as a deferred flow, not built out — architecturally distinct from the muted demo-legend treatment given to `partially_released`/`cancelled` because it BLOCKS the flow rather than merely being unshown.
> 4. CANCELLED = live-reachable distinction: unlike `pledged`/`defaulted`, `cancelled` on the `nwr_status` enum is called out as live-reachable in principle (an operator/admin can void a mis-issued receipt) even though no canon receipt is cancelled today — a documented distinction between "not modelled yet" (pledged/defaulted) and "modelled but no live example today" (cancelled), same distinction 466 draws inline.

> **Canon-guard summary:**
> 1. 462 is the pattern-setting nav-gate screen (founder-approved 2026-07-08); bottom nav is byte-identical across all 5 tab-root screens (462, 463, 466, 468, 470).
> 2. Occupancy/rate-per-kind/cold-chain gap language is re-quoted verbatim across 461/462/464/468/470 rather than re-invented per screen — one gap, one wording, repeated at every surface that touches it.
> 3. 466/467's `nwr_status` state-machine comments and 463/467's `storage_bookings.status` comments are exact copies of the same 6-value enums — no drift between the two screens that both quote them.
> 4. 469's snapshot-timing reconciliation explicitly documents why its numbers differ from 462's — the same device used for 462's own Ramesh-cold-booking TIME-LAPSE CONTINUITY NOTE — pre-empting a false canon-mismatch flag in QA.
> 5. Kanji Bhalala's cotton lot is the single continuous NEW-canon thread running through 463→464→465→466 (confirm→weigh→assay→issue) — one story, traced across four consecutive screens with consistent figures at every stage.

> QA — 10/10 CLEAN PASS; zero fixes required (cleanest remediation batch to date); nav byte-identical across all 5 tab-root screens (462, 463, 466, 468, 470); all canon threads (time-lapse continuity, snapshot-timing reconciliation, tonnage plausibility, rate-per-kind gap) were pre-emptively documented ahead of audit rather than caught by it. 2 cosmetic-only notes logged (inline Hindi-font styling on 2 files) — not treated as fixes. This batch closes the LAST zero-coverage role identified by the D16 audit (Vet closed in D19; Warehouse Operator closes here) — every platform role now has at least baseline screen coverage.
>
> Source: screens' own header comments (461–470), cross-referenced against `Database_Architecture` (09_agri_infra_services.sql: warehouses, storage_bookings, assay_reports, nwr_receipts/nwr_status; 05_money.sql: payments/payouts) and Phase-1/prior-batch canon (408-farmer-warehouse-receipt, 409-farmer-cold-storage-booking, 433-shared-role-picker-v2, 450-vet-registration-onboarding, 451-vet-home, 452-vet-booking-queue, 457-vet-earnings) for verbatim/format fidelity.

### 461-warehouse-operator-onboarding

*461 · Warehouse Operator Onboarding — Krishi-Verse [WAREHOUSE OPERATOR] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE]*

- Back-header, no nav — entry flow precedes 462's bottom-nav home. Pattern source: 450-vet-registration-onboarding.html (hero → invite-context → stepped sections → review-pending state). Role-picker cross-ref: 433 ("Vet, Warehouse Operator, Sardar and staff roles are added by invitation or your FPO/tenant"). Depicts Paresh Thakkar's ORIGINAL onboarding submission for Junagadh AgriStore — a historical instance filled with his real canon data (WDRA/GJ/00214, capacity 500 MT, storage_kinds ambient+cold_0_4, ambient ₹28/cold ₹32 per qtl/month, NERL+CCRL repository memberships) — shown WITHOUT an in-UI "today" stamp (unlike 462) because by the batch's "today" his application was approved long ago and he is already fully operational (see 462); same narrative device as 408-farmer-warehouse-receipt (historical vs "today").
- DB FIELDS TOUCHED: `warehouses` (`wdra_reg_no`, `address_id`, `capacity_mt`, `storage_kinds` jsonb, `rate_per_qtl_month_minor`, `insurance_policy_ref`, `operator_user_id`, `is_active`) + draft `vet_services`-style rate rows (service_type, pricing_unit, price_minor equivalents for storage rates). Repository membership (NERL/CCRL) is not a `warehouses` column in this schema pass — modelled as onboarding-intake metadata pending a `warehouse_repository_memberships` join table, flagged not silently invented as a real column.
- RATE-PER-KIND GAP flagged inline at the rates step (same flag as 462/468/470/408/409): this form collects two per-kind rates because the operator genuinely sets two prices — the schema needs a rate-per-kind table before this is wired to a real save.
- API: proposed `POST /v1/warehouses/register` (writes warehouses + draft rate metadata) · existing KYC document endpoints per 434/450's pattern

### 462-warehouse-operator-home

*462 · Warehouse Operator Home — Krishi-Verse [WAREHOUSE OPERATOR] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE · NAV GATE]*

- FOUNDER-APPROVED 2026-07-08, pattern-setting 5-tab nav screen for the whole warehouse-operator app (full tab map + justification in the batch nav block above). Hub: today intake/pending/upcoming queue, occupancy snapshot, a lightweight "July so far" earnings snapshot (₹6,360, captured 9:41 AM — BEFORE today's release, see 469's snapshot-timing reconciliation), eNWR strip, alerts.
- Canon: Paresh Thakkar, Junagadh AgriStore, WDRA/GJ/00214. TIME-LAPSE CONTINUITY NOTE: Ramesh Patel's cold booking (409) is shown here as "confirmed ✓", two days after 409's own "requested" moment — same `storage_bookings` row, two roles, two timestamps, documented here to pre-empt a false canon-mismatch flag in QA. CHAMBER-SPLIT + RATE-PER-KIND gaps re-quoted (ambient 260/380 MT, cold 50/120 MT split; single `rate_per_qtl_month_minor` column vs two live per-kind rates).
- API: proposed `GET /v1/warehouse/home-summary` (queue snapshot + occupancy + earnings snapshot + eNWR strip)

### 463-warehouse-booking-queue

*463 · Warehouse Booking Queue — Krishi-Verse [WAREHOUSE OPERATOR] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE · NAV GATE]*

- TAB ROOT for Intake (`aria-current`, bottom nav byte-identical to 462). Full `storage_bookings.status` 6-vocab surfaced via filter chips + per-card status badges. Canon cards: Naresh Bhatti (15 qtl groundnut, ambient, `requested`, capacity-check chip "15 qtl fits ambient ✓"); Kanji Bhalala (12 qtl cotton, ambient, `confirmed`, arrival today 8 Jul, "Start intake" CTA → 464); Ramesh Patel (3 qtl leafy veg, cold, `confirmed`, expected 10 Jul, ₹48 — same row as 409, time-lapse continuity per 462).
- E-AUDIT CLOSURE: `cancelled` demoed as a muted legend row, same 452-vet-booking-queue-precedent treatment for states absent from today's live queue. `stored`/`partially_released`/`released` have no reachable transition from this screen (belong to 464/467).

### 464-warehouse-goods-intake

*464 · Warehouse Goods Intake — Krishi-Verse [WAREHOUSE OPERATOR] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE]*

- Back-header, no nav — floor task, part of the fused Intake tab (463 confirm → 464 weigh-in → 465 assay, one continuous physical workflow, see 462's nav-structure comment). Canon: Kanji Bhalala's cotton lot, `status=confirmed` (from 463), arrival today 8 Jul 2026. Intake ceremony: arrival/depositor check, weigh-in verification against the declared 12 qtl, condition photos, a moisture quick-check preview (full parameters recorded later at 465), storage assignment, and the status transition `confirmed`→`stored` itself (`stored_at` timestamptz set).
- WEIGHBRIDGE UI-ONLY NOTE: no `weighbridge_readings` table exists anywhere in Database_Architecture — the "declared vs weighed" comparison is a UI-only verification step for the operator's own confidence before storing; only the confirmed 12 qtl persists to `storage_bookings`. CHAMBER ASSIGNMENT DESIGNS-LEAD NOTE: "Ambient chamber A2" is an illustrative placeholder (same flag as 462/468) — no `warehouse_chambers` table exists to hold a real per-chamber assignment.
- API: proposed `POST /v1/warehouse/bookings/:id/intake` (writes weigh-in verification + photos, sets `status='stored'`, `stored_at`)

### 465-warehouse-assay-record

*465 · Warehouse Assay Record — Krishi-Verse [WAREHOUSE OPERATOR] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE]*

- Back-header, no nav — floor task, part of the fused Intake tab (463 confirm → 464 weigh-in → 465 assay). Canon: assay for Kanji Bhalala's cotton lot, freshly stored today (from 464). `assayer_name` = "Paresh Thakkar (in-house)" — this warehouse's own operator doubles as assayer for this deposit (external-lab option shown as the alternative path). NEW cotton assay: moisture 8.2%, trash/FM 3.1%, staple 27.5mm, Grade A shankar-6; `valid_until` = assayed_at (8 Jul 2026) + 90 days = 6 Oct 2026 exact (Jul 8→31=23d, +Aug 31=54d, +Sep 30=84d, +6=90d).
- `parameters` jsonb for COTTON (moisture/trash-FM/staple-length) differs by design from the WHEAT shape (moisture/FM/broken) shown as a COMPLETED REFERENCE card: Ramesh Patel's Feb wheat assay (moisture 11.2%, FM 0.8%, broken 2.1%), reproduced verbatim from 408-farmer-warehouse-receipt.html canon — same commodity's receipt (NERL-ENWR-2026-088412) is issued and referenced again at 466.
- API: proposed `POST /v1/warehouse/bookings/:id/assay` (writes `assay_reports`: `assayer_name`, `parameters` jsonb, `grade_option_id`, `report_media_id`, `assayed_at`, `valid_until`)

### 466-warehouse-nwr-issuance

*466 · Warehouse NWR Issuance — Krishi-Verse [WAREHOUSE OPERATOR] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE · NAV GATE]*

- TAB ROOT for eNWR (`aria-current`, bottom nav byte-identical to 462) — earns its own top-level tab (not nested under Stock/Intake) because eNWR is a distinct regulatory/legal artefact (negotiable instrument, pledge-eligible) independent of physical stock state, same reasoning as 457-vet-earnings' own tab. Canon: Ramesh Patel's wheat receipt NERL-ENWR-2026-088412 (8 qtl, ₹19,600, status=issued) shown from the operator's issuing side, identical to 408. Kanji Bhalala's cotton — assay just completed at 465 — NEW issuance flow expanded inline: repository select (NERL|CCRL, CCRL chosen), quantity 12 qtl, MANUAL valuation ₹7,150/qtl → ₹85,800 auto-calculated (12 × 7,150), producing `enwr_no` CCRL-ENWR-2026-091730.
- MANUAL VALUATION GAP: `nwr_receipts.valuation_minor` populated by the operator typing a per-qtl rate — no automated valuation service (e.g. mandi-price lookup × quantity) exists. A real, load-bearing gap — two different operators could value the same commodity/quantity differently until a real pricing service exists.
- `nwr_status` 6-enum quoted inline; only `issued` is a live forward state this screen produces — `pledged`/`defaulted`/`partially_released`/`released` have NO reachable UI transition here (belongs to 467, out of this screen's scope); `cancelled` is flagged live-reachable in principle (mis-issue void) though no canon receipt is cancelled today. Demo legend follows the same 452-vet-booking-queue-precedent "render the pattern, label it demo" treatment.
- API: proposed `GET /v1/warehouse/nwr?status=...` · `POST /v1/warehouse/nwr/issue` (repository, quantity, valuation_minor → `enwr_no`, `status='issued'`)

### 467-warehouse-release-authorization

*467 · Warehouse Release Authorization — Krishi-Verse [WAREHOUSE OPERATOR] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE]*

- Back-header sub-action nested under the Stock tab (see 462's nav-structure comment: Stock = 468 occupancy + 467 release, two views of the same inventory-on-hand question). Reached from 468's Junagadh Traders stored-lot row → "Release →" CTA. Canon: Junagadh Traders, 95 qtl groundnut, ambient, stored 12 May 2026, NWR NERL-ENWR-2026-089950 (issued, unpledged); holder requested release via the app this morning, Paresh verifies + authorizes here.
- FEE ARITHMETIC (exact, REAL formula sourced from `storage-booking.entity.ts`: fee = qty × rate_per_qtl_month × CEIL(months)): 12 May→8 Jul 2026 = 1 complete month + a partial second month → CEIL 2; 95 qtl × ₹28/qtl/month × 2 months = ₹5,320 (95×28=2,660; ×2=5,320). Posts as a `storage_fee` wallet leg only at the moment `storage_bookings.status`→`released` — matches 462's "fees settle only on release" framing and 469's "today's release ₹5,320" line item.
- PLEDGE GUARD (hard block, not a demo card): bilingual `role="note"` warning — "Pledged NWR lots cannot release without lender NOC · गिरवी रसीद बिना ऋणदाता NOC के रिलीज़ नहीं होगी." If `nwr_status=pledged`, Authorize is blocked until a lender NOC step clears it; no lot in today's canon is pledged, so the guard has no reachable demo state — flagged as a deferred flow, not built out. RELEASE-REQUEST GAP: no `release_requests` table exists — a holder's "Request release" is modelled as an inbound notification, not a persisted row; the only real state-changing event is Paresh's own Authorize action. GATE-PASS GAP: "Print gate pass" is a UI-only physical handoff action, no `gate_passes` table exists. `partially_released` demoed as a muted BACKEND-DEFERRED legend card (452-precedent).
- API: proposed `POST /v1/warehouse/nwr/:id/release` (transitions `storage_bookings.status`→`released`, `nwr_receipts.status`→`released`, posts `storage_fee` wallet leg) · client-only "Print gate pass" action

### 468-warehouse-occupancy-dashboard

*468 · Warehouse Occupancy Dashboard — Krishi-Verse [WAREHOUSE OPERATOR] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE · NAV GATE]*

- TAB ROOT for Stock (`aria-current`, bottom nav byte-identical to 462), fuses this occupancy view with 467-warehouse-release-authorization (see 462's nav-structure comment). Canon: 310/500 MT occupied (62%) — ambient 260/380 MT (68%), cold 50/120 MT (42%), 190 MT free. Four named stored lots (Ramesh Patel wheat 8 qtl since 15 Feb, Kanji Bhalala cotton 12 qtl stored today, Anand FPO wheat 180 qtl ambient since Mar, Junagadh Traders groundnut 95 qtl ambient since 12 May) plus one reserved-not-yet-stored row (Ramesh Patel leafy veg, cold, incoming 10 Jul).
- TONNAGE PLAUSIBILITY NOTE (designs-lead): the four named lots sum to 8+12+180+95 = 295 qtl (29.5 MT) — a small fraction of the 310 MT aggregate occupancy total. Intentional and consistent with a summary dashboard, not a full ledger: this warehouse serves many smaller depositors beyond the four with a reachable story on other screens; the remaining ≈280 MT is real in the aggregate figure but not itemised lot-by-lot here, same "top-N under an aggregate total" treatment as any hub dashboard.
- CHAMBER-SPLIT gap re-quoted (no `warehouse_chambers` table exists); cold-chamber temperature-log view references a `cold_chain_logs` table that is a PHANTOM REFERENCE — does not exist anywhere in Database_Architecture (grepped 05–13 full_platform/*.sql, zero hits).
- API: proposed `GET /v1/warehouse/occupancy` (aggregate capacity_mt + storage_kinds, illustrative per-kind split) · `GET /v1/warehouse/stored-lots`

### 469-warehouse-earnings

*469 · Warehouse Earnings — Krishi-Verse [WAREHOUSE OPERATOR] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE — DELIBERATE DIVERGENCE FROM VET PRECEDENT]*

- Back-header sub-page NESTED under Profile (NOT a bottom-nav tab) — deliberate divergence from the vet precedent (457-vet-earnings, a tab root): a vet's fee settles per booking, multiple times a day; a warehouse operator's `storage_fee` only crystallizes at an episodic `release()` event, so most days the number does not move — a tab would manufacture false daily activity. Reached from 462's "Full ledger →" link and 470's "Earnings & statements" chevron row; back button returns to 470.
- SNAPSHOT-TIMING RECONCILIATION: 462's morning read ("July so far ₹6,360 · Today ₹0", captured 9:41 AM, BEFORE release) vs this screen's later read (AFTER Paresh's 467 release, same day) — July so far now ₹11,680, Today ₹5,320; both are the correct, consistent state of the same ledger at two different moments, not a canon contradiction. ARITHMETIC EXACT: ambient 210 qtl-mo×₹28=₹5,880 + cold 15 qtl-mo×₹32=₹480 = ₹6,360 prior-to-today; + today's release 95×₹28×2=₹5,320 = ₹11,680 July so far. YTD ₹1,84,900 (illustrative, lakh grouping per 390/405/457 convention).
- DB TRUTH GAP: no dedicated warehouse-earnings table exists — figures DERIVED by summing the `storage_fee` wallet leg posted against `storage_bookings` rows for this `warehouse_id` at `released`/`partially_released`. Settlement proposed to reuse the generic `payouts` table with a new `storage_fee` purpose value — identical gap shape to 457-vet-earnings' proposed `vet_fee` value.
- API: proposed `GET /v1/warehouse/earnings?period=...` (derived aggregate, no payout table backing)

### 470-warehouse-profile-settings

*470 · Warehouse Profile — Krishi-Verse [WAREHOUSE OPERATOR] [P0 — D16 remediation · NEW ROLE · NAV GATE — LAST screen of the role, 461–470 complete · LAST ZERO-COVERAGE ROLE CLOSED]*

- TAB ROOT (Profile), bottom nav byte-identical to 462. 469 nests here as a sub-page via the "Earnings & statements" chevron row (see 462's DELIBERATE DIVERGENCE note — earnings is NOT a tab). Canon: Paresh Thakkar, Junagadh AgriStore, WDRA/GJ/00214, capacity 500 MT, storage_kinds ambient+cold_0_4, member of both NERL and CCRL repositories (Ramesh's wheat issued via NERL, Kanji's cotton via CCRL, same warehouse). Insurance ref AGRI-WH-INS-2025-3387. Staff: Bhavesh Koli, loader/weighman (cosmetic-only row, see gap note below).
- RATE-PER-KIND GAP (verbatim from 462, re-quoted because this is the screen where the operator would EDIT rates): `warehouses.rate_per_qtl_month_minor` is a single bigint field, but this warehouse publishes TWO live per-kind rates — ambient ₹28/qtl/month, cold_0_4 ₹32/qtl/month. A single scalar column cannot hold two rates at once; the schema needs a rate row per `(warehouse_id, storage_kind)`. The edit row shows both figures as the warehouse's real published pricing; the single-column schema gap is flagged, not silently resolved.
- STAFF COSMETIC-ONLY GAP: Bhavesh Koli (loader/weighman) is shown as a single-row summary with NO underlying staff table anywhere in Database_Architecture — no `warehouse_staff` (or similar) table with role, shift, or contact columns. Cosmetic scaffolding only; tapping it has nowhere real to go until such a table exists — same treatment class as other cosmetic-only staff rows across the design system. This is the LAST screen of the D16-audit's two zero-coverage-role remediations (Vet in D19, Warehouse Operator here).
- API: proposed `GET/PATCH /v1/warehouse/profile` · `GET /v1/warehouse/services` (rate CRUD, links to the edit row above)

## P0 Additions · Money & Legal Lifecycle — screens 471–482 [D16 REMEDIATION] [batch #5, FINAL batch of the D16-audit P0 remediation plan; 12 screens, 4 roles (farmer ×8, buyer-org ×1, transporter ×1, shared ×2); no new role, no nav gate; closes "40+ unrendered enum states in the money/legal core" — loan decisions, insurance lifecycle, claim/scheme appeals, contract exit terms, warehouse-receipt default, transporter cash custody, bank accounts, refunds]

> **No nav gate this batch — unlike D12/D13/D19/D20:** D21 introduces no new role and proposes no bottom-nav tab structure. Every screen is a back-header sub-action view (471–474, 477–481) or a standalone status view (475, 476, 482) layered onto an existing farmer/buyer-org/transporter/shared app. Governing rationale, carried in every screen's own header comment: **"every enum state deserves pixels"** — every state rendered here was already real in `Database_Architecture`; none required a schema change to draw.

> **Canon — continuity with prior-batch screens (verified exact, not re-invented):** 471 quotes 266's KCC ····4412 loan (₹68,713 outstanding) inside its `decision_note`, without modifying 266's own record. 472 continues 276's restructure offer verbatim (Option A: 12→18 months, EMI ₹8,365→₹5,720) and explicitly does NOT re-derive the inherited "~₹1,900 more interest" figure against 471's ₹68,713 outstanding figure — two different questions, left unreconciled by design. 473/474 continue 285's Health+Life bundle (KV-POL-2026-0110-233, ₹99/month, Ramesh Patel + Meera Ben Patel). 475 continues policy KV-POL-2026-0401-054 (₹60,000 sum insured, 40% assessed loss → ₹24,000 paid). 476 continues 254's scheme portfolio (KV-SCH-2026-00347, "Closed Feb 2026 · Not approved") and reuses 264's Bhulekh-verified survey 214/2 as new evidence. 477/478 continue 363/364/366's contract thread verbatim (KV-CON-2026-0231, Ramesh Patel ↔ Mehta Oil Industries, 10 qtl GG-20 groundnut, ₹6,200/qtl + oil premium) — 478 renders the SAME 4-message thread from Mehta Oil's own side. 479 continues 408/466's real NWR receipt (NERL-ENWR-2026-088412, ₹19,600) unmodified, shown SAFE. 480's guard line is quoted verbatim from 404-transporter-pod-capture.html. 481 continues 121's exact bank-account canon (SBI ····2247 default, HDFC ····9831 secondary) and 180's penny-drop copy verbatim. 482 continues 443's ₹9,550 refund preview, 445's ₹3,820 partial refund, and 398's ₹80 D2C wallet credit.

> **THE DUAL-RAIL APPEAL ASYMMETRY — this batch's central enum-honesty device (475 vs 476):** `claim_status` (enum: `intimated, docs_pending, survey_scheduled, surveyed, approved, partially_approved, rejected, paid, closed`) has NO `appealed` value — so 475's appeal is a **SUPPORT ACTION** (a review request logged against the claim), layered on top of an unchanged status; `claim_status` reads `partially_approved` before, during and after the appeal is filed. By contrast, `scheme_applications.status` (enum: `draft, submitted, under_verification, clarification_needed, approved, rejected, disbursed, closed, appealed`) DOES ratify `appealed` as a real value (see 249's enum comment) — so 476's appeal performs a genuine **STATE TRANSITION**, `rejected → appealed`, persisted in `scheme_application_events`. Both screens name this asymmetry explicitly in their own footnotes and cross-reference each other — it is disclosed as a real, current difference in how two modules modelled "appeal," never hidden or smoothed into false symmetry.

> **DEMO-LEGEND DISCIPLINE — first-ever rendering of contract breach/terminated/disputed (477):** `farming_contracts.status` is a 9-value enum: `draft|proposed|negotiating|signed|active|fulfilled|breached|terminated|disputed`. Live canon (363/364/366) only ever reaches `negotiating → signed → active`. `breached`, `terminated` and `disputed` have ZERO live examples anywhere in the screen set — this is their FIRST-EVER rendering, as DEMO LEGENDS ONLY, following the exact 466/452-vet-booking-queue-precedent treatment D20 established (muted cards, illustrative/anonymized data, BACKEND-note captions, no live CTAs). The real contract, KV-CON-2026-0231, STAYS `active` throughout 477 — this screen is a VIEW of the active contract's exit-terms (reusing 363's "what if..." clauses), never an action screen that breaches or terminates it. Tone: talk-before-terminate — platform mediation and a human helpline sit ahead of any exit action; no live "Terminate" CTA exists anywhere on this screen.

> **THE HDFC CROSS-BATCH NOTE — corrected premise, no fix needed on 481:** 481 ties to screen 121's exact bank-account canon: SBI ····2247 (SBIN0001247, DEFAULT) + **HDFC ····9831** (HDFC0000287, secondary), account holder Ramesh Bhanubhai Patel. The real cross-batch inconsistency is between 121's **HDFC ····9831** and 21-transactions.html's "Bank withdrawal — **HDFC ●●4521**" line — two different last-4 digits rendered for what reads as the same HDFC relationship, across two different batches. **481's own footnote already stated this correctly** (renders 121's ····9831 as-is, source of truth for the Bank Accounts screen, and flags the ····9831-vs-····4521 mismatch for a future cross-batch data-consolidation pass) — no change was needed on the screen itself. The correction QA made was to the audit brief's own framing of this item (which had described it as an unresolved defect on 481), not to 481.

> **DB TRUTH — REAL schema this batch reads/writes today:** 1. `loan_applications.status` + `credit_decisions.factors` jsonb (471). 2. `loans.status` incl. `restructured` (472). 3. `insurance_policies.policy_status` enum `proposed|active|lapsed|cancelled|expired|claimed` (473). 4. `claim_status` 9-vocab, no `appealed` value (475). 5. `scheme_applications.status` 9-vocab incl. ratified `appealed` (476). 6. `farming_contracts.status` 9-vocab (477/478). 7. `nwr_receipts`/`nwr_status` 6-vocab (479, extends D20's 09_agri_infra_services.sql coverage). 8. `payments.method='cod'` + `shipments.cod_minor` (480). 9. Bank-account tables (121's canon, 481). 10. `payments.payment_status` 9-vocab incl. `refund_initiated`/`refunded`/`partially_refunded` (05_money.sql, 482) + `wallet_ledger` as a structurally separate rail (482).

> **CONCEPT / BACKEND-PENDING schema (no backing tables — design proposals only, flagged for the backend team):**
> 1. **`policy_nominees` table** — no table and no nominee column on `insurance_policies` exists; nominee data lives only as free text inside the 285 enrollment capture. Flagged on 474 — UI-only change request, human-confirmed, not instant-apply.
> 2. **`surrender_value` column** — does not exist anywhere in `insurance_policies`; this product line never accrued a cash value. Flagged on 473 — cancellation always ships as zero-payout, never implies a refund.
> 3. **Claim-appeal state modeling** — `claim_status` has no `appealed` (or similar) value; an appeal is a support-action side-rail only. Flagged on 475, explicitly contrasted against 476's real transition.
> 4. **Contract termination/breach flow states** — `breached`/`terminated`/`disputed` are ratified enum values with no designed UI flow, mediation process, or notification chain. Flagged on 477 — view-only, no live mutating CTA until a product/ops decision lands.
> 5. **`eod_cash_reconciliation` table** — no ratified table exists; 480 mocks the deposit-confirm + variance workflow directly against `payments.method='cod'` rows, same illustrative convention as D20's own backend-pending items.
> 6. **Refund-rail unification** — marketplace refunds (`payments`/`orders`) and D2C wallet credits (`wallet_ledger`) are two structurally separate rails today; flagged on 482 as a two-rails disclosure, not silently merged, pending a product decision.

> **No "suspension of disbelief" items this batch:** every gap above is a named, real schema/product-decision gap with a documented on-screen workaround — none required inventing canon data or faking a live example. Zero suspension-of-disbelief items logged, the same clean-disclosure standard D20 set.

> **Canon-guard summary:**
> 1. The dual-rail appeal asymmetry (475 vs 476) is disclosed identically in both screens' own footnotes — no drift in wording between the two cross-references.
> 2. 477's three demo-legend states (`breached`/`terminated`/`disputed`) carry BACKEND-note captions worded consistently, and KV-CON-2026-0231 is never shown transitioning out of `active` anywhere in this batch.
> 3. 480's cash-handling guard is a verbatim quote of 404's own guard line — reused, not reworded.
> 4. 481's HDFC ····9831 vs ●●4521 note is repeated with identical framing to 121's own canon — the mismatch is named, not hidden, and not "fixed" by silently changing either figure.
> 5. 482's two-rails disclosure labels the 398 wallet credit "other rail" consistently, so it is never read as a marketplace payment-gateway refund.

> QA — 12/12 PASS (11 clean + 1 one-word fix): 480's cash-handling guard corrected to read "instructs" verbatim, matching 404's original guard exactly. Separately, QA self-corrected the audit brief's own premise regarding 481 — the real cross-batch mismatch is 121's HDFC ····9831 vs 21-transactions' HDFC ●●4521, and 481's own footnote already stated this correctly, so no change was made to the screen itself, only to how the audit brief framed the finding. This batch closes the FINAL P0 batch of the D16 remediation plan.
>
> Source: screens' own header comments (471–482), cross-referenced against `Database_Architecture` (loan/credit_decisions, insurance_policies/claim_status, scheme_applications, farming_contracts, nwr_receipts/nwr_status, payments/shipments, bank-account tables, payments.payment_status/wallet_ledger) and Phase-1/prior-batch canon (121, 180, 249, 254, 264, 266, 276, 285, 344-346, 363, 364, 366, 398, 404, 405, 408, 442, 443, 445, 466) for verbatim/format fidelity.

### 471-farmer-loan-rejected

*471 · Loan Application Rejected — Krishi-Verse [FARMER] [P0 — D16 remediation · never-shame]*

- Back-header, no nav. `loan_applications.status = rejected`, KV-LN-2026-05203 (Gold loan, Suvarna Gold Finance NBFC), ₹40,000 requested. `decision_note` verbatim: "Existing loan obligation (KCC ····4412, ₹68,713 outstanding) reduces free repayment capacity for a new ₹40,000 request." `credit_decisions.factors` jsonb rendered as plain-language right-to-explanation cards — never raw score/model output. `cooling_off_until` is NULL (PRD §59.4: only populates on approved offers; rejections carry no cooling-off period). KV-LN-2026-04412 (the KCC loan quoted) is NOT modified by this screen — its own arc (screen 266) stays exactly as-is.
- NEVER-SHAME TONE: no penalty framing, no "DENIED" stamp; two concrete next steps offered (apply again with a co-applicant / apply for a smaller amount).
- API: `GET /v1/loans/applications/:id` · `GET /v1/loans/applications/:id/decision`

### 472-farmer-loan-restructured-schedule

*472 · Loan Restructured Schedule — Krishi-Verse [FARMER] [P0 — D16 remediation]*

- Back-header, no nav. `loans.status` enum `active|overdue|restructured|closed|written_off|transferred` — continues screen 276 verbatim: farmer picked "Smaller EMI, longer time" (Option A) on KCC loan ····4412 (KV-LN-2026-04412); NBFC approved it, `loans.status → restructured`. Figures inherited exactly from 276: tenure 12→18 months, EMI ₹8,365→₹5,720. "~₹1,900 more total interest" is 276's OWN illustrative comparison for the ORIGINAL ₹1,00,000 loan — explicitly NOT re-derived against the ₹68,713 currently-outstanding figure quoted on 471; the two numbers describe different things and must not be reconciled.
- Schedule: 4 instalments already paid at the old ₹8,365 rate (4×₹8,365=₹33,460) before the restructure took effect, followed by the new 14-instalment restructured plan at ₹5,720 (14×₹5,720=₹80,080) to complete the 18-month tenure.
- API: `GET /v1/loans/:id` · `GET /v1/loans/:id/schedule`

### 473-farmer-policy-cancel-surrender

*473 · Policy Cancel / Surrender — Krishi-Verse [FARMER] [P0 — D16 remediation · no-cash-back honesty]*

- Back-header, no nav. `insurance_policies.policy_status` enum `proposed|active|lapsed|cancelled|expired|claimed` — this screen cancels KV-POL-2026-0110-233 (Health+Life bundle, ₹99/month, members Ramesh Patel + Meera Ben Patel — canon screen 285). HONESTY NOTE: there is NO `surrender_value` column anywhere in `insurance_policies` — this product never accrued a cash value, so "surrender" is framed plainly as cancellation with zero pay-out, never implied refund/cash-back.
- Cancelling stops the ₹99 auto-debit immediately and ends cover today for both members; re-enrolling later restarts BOTH waiting periods from screen 285 (30-day accident-only window, 2-year pre-existing-illness exclusion) — not a resume, a fresh start. No pause/hold state exists in the schema — the only retention lever is a human support conversation.
- API: `POST /v1/insurance/policies/:id/cancel`

### 474-farmer-policy-nominee-edit

*474 · Policy Nominee Edit — Krishi-Verse [FARMER] [P0 — D16 remediation · BACKEND-DEFERRED]*

- Back-header, no nav. SCHEMA GAP flagged upward, not solved here: there is no `policy_nominees` table and no nominee column on `insurance_policies` — nominee name/relationship for KV-POL-2026-0110-233 today lives only inside the 285 enrollment form capture as free text, with no dedicated storage or update path. This screen is UI-only: it lets the farmer (Meera Ben Patel's nominee record) submit a change request, but there is no backend field yet for the app to write it into. The on-screen banner tells the farmer honestly that this is confirmed by a human, not instantly applied — because instant apply is not technically possible until the schema exists.

### 475-farmer-claim-appeal

*475 · Claim Appeal — Krishi-Verse [FARMER] [P0 — D16 remediation · SUPPORT ACTION, non-transition]*

- Back-header, no nav. `claim_status` enum `intimated|docs_pending|survey_scheduled|surveyed|approved|partially_approved|rejected|paid|closed` — there is NO "appealed" value in this enum. So an appeal here is a SUPPORT ACTION (a review request logged against the claim) layered on top of the unchanged status — `claim_status` stays `partially_approved` before, during and after the appeal is filed. Contrast: screen 476 (scheme appeal) has an enum that WAS ratified to include "appealed" as a real status, so a scheme appeal performs a genuine status transition — that asymmetry is real today and is called out to the farmer in the footnote below, not hidden.
- KV-CLM-2026-0530-098: livestock accident claim, policy KV-POL-2026-0401-054, sum insured ₹60,000, assessed 40% loss → payout ₹24,000 (60,000×0.40=24,000) already paid/approved-at-40%; farmer's own estimate is 70% loss.
- API: `POST /v1/insurance/claims/:id/appeal` (writes a support ticket / review-request row, does NOT change `claim_status`)

### 476-farmer-scheme-rejected-appeal

*476 · Scheme Rejected → Appeal — Krishi-Verse [FARMER] [P0 — D16 remediation · REAL enum transition]*

- Back-header, no nav. `scheme_applications.status` enum `draft|submitted|under_verification|clarification_needed|approved|rejected|disbursed|closed|appealed` — "appealed" IS a ratified real value here (see screen 249's enum comment), so filing an appeal below performs a genuine status transition `rejected → appealed`, persisted in `scheme_application_events`. Contrast: screen 475 (claim appeal) has no such value in `claim_status`, so that appeal is only a logged support action with the status left unchanged — this asymmetry between the two modules is intentional today and is called out on screen 475's footnote, not hidden here either.
- KV-SCH-2026-00347 = the same KCC scheme application shown "Closed Feb 2026 · Not approved" in canon screen 254's portfolio list. `rejection_reason` verbatim: "7/12 land record could not be verified before the 31 January window closed." New evidence attached reuses the Bhulekh-verified survey 214/2 record from screen 264 (kv-badge-success "Verified · Bhulekh" chip).
- API: `POST /v1/schemes/applications/:id/appeal` (writes `scheme_application_events`, transitions `status` to `appealed`)
