## BRAND-1 — Brand book — brand/ (Phase L opens, DELTA-065 new canon location, pipeline v4)
| ID | Page | Key finding | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| BRAND-000 | brand-book-canon.html — structure, THE BRAND LAW (occurrence 1 of 5), chapter map, source-precedence diagram, designer_pack relationship statement, open-items table | THE BRAND LAW authored; P2 FIXED: type-family line-range citation over-reached | The page originally cited the `designer_pack/01-designer-handbook.html` JetBrains Mono type-family declaration as falling within lines 615–636 (the Ch.06 typography block); the true line is 677, outside that range. Fixed: citation split into the Ch.06 range (615–636, for the rest of the type scale) plus a separate line-677 cite for JetBrains Mono specifically. Also carries the 12%-shadow floor/ceiling delta (below) and the designer_pack relationship statement (5+ handbook rules cross-checked against `tokens.css`, one ambiguity flagged, zero silently superseded). |
| BRAND-001 | identity.html — logo/mark, clear space, misuse, white-label lockups | Law occurrence 2 of 5; SOURCE-CELL AUDIT: 8/8 + 6/6 + 5/5 rule tables + 4-card misuse gallery, all sourced, zero invented | Every clear-space ratio, minimum size, and misuse example cites its token, contract section, or prior canon (TS-000/TS-002/W191) — none typed from preference. Logo-fallback for non-designer_pack contexts remains an OPEN §17 item (re-verified absent, consistent with SITE-4/SITE-016's prior finding that the live site ships zero raster/vector logo assets). |
| BRAND-002 | color-type.html — color system + typography + per-surface usage laws | Law occurrence 3 of 5; **P1 FIXED: ₹ self-test undercount (claimed 5, actual 6)** | The page's own self-test claimed exactly 5 rupee-glyph occurrences; the true count was 6 — the builder missed the third glyph sitting inside the file's own verbatim §7 quote (a nested citation, not a fresh value use). Corrected at QA. SOURCE-CELL AUDIT: 7/7 + 5/5 + 7/7 + 3/3 + 6/6 rule tables, all sourced; 30+ token hexes byte-exact vs `tokens.css`/`web-tokens.css`. |
| BRAND-003 | voice-tone.html — voice, tone, vocabulary, honesty-laws-as-voice | Law occurrence 4 of 5; SOURCE-CELL AUDIT: 13/13 law index + 5 more tables, all sourced; console v2 §4 paragraph byte-identical | The law index table cites all 13 prior laws byte-exact against their source files (claims/print-gating/statutory/livery/targets-razor/template/trust-page/developer-truth/comparison/customer-proof/counsel/locale/TS-000 color-guard) — independently re-verified at QA, zero drift. |
| BRAND-004 | surfaces.html — per-surface application (mobile/console/site/email/print/apparel/livery) | Law occurrence 5 of 5; SOURCE-CELL AUDIT: 13/13 surface matrix + 3/3, all sourced | Completes the brand law's 5-occurrence reproduction. Surface matrix cross-checked against TS-002's white-label application matrix — no contradiction found. |

**THE BRAND LAW (this batch's spine, authored and reproduced verbatim ×5 rails)**: *"The brand is the sum of its kept promises. This book compiles rules the canon has already ratified — it invents nothing. Every rule on these pages cites the token, contract section, law, or founder decision that made it a rule. A brand rule without a source is a preference, and preferences do not govern."*

**QA verdict (BRAND-1): CONDITIONAL FAIL → fixed → PASS. 1 P1, 1 P2.** **P1 — BRAND-002's self-test claimed "5 ₹ occurrences"** when the true count was 6, the builder having missed the third glyph sitting inside the file's own verbatim §7 quote (a nested citation, not a fresh value use) — corrected. **P2 — BRAND-000's type-family line-range citation over-reached**: JetBrains Mono is actually declared at handbook line 677, not inside the cited 615–636 range — citation split into the correct two ranges.

**LESSON FOR MEMORY — the compilation razor works**: forcing a source cell on every single rule row (token, contract §, prior law, or founder decision) made invention structurally impossible rather than merely discouraged — independent QA found **zero invented rules** across all 5 chapters of a rules-heavy batch, the first time that has happened on a batch whose entire purpose is stating rules. The razor generalizes beyond brand documents: any canon page whose job is to assert governing rules should require a source cell per row, not just a citation somewhere on the page.

**QA strengths (all independently re-verified)**: SOURCE-CELL AUDIT PERFECT — every rule table is 1:1 rule-rows-to-source-cells across all 5 chapters (BRAND-000: 6/6; BRAND-001: 8/8 + 6/6 + 5/5 + 4-card misuse gallery; BRAND-002: 7/7 + 5/5 + 7/7 + 3/3 + 6/6; BRAND-003: 13/13 law index + 5 more tables; BRAND-004: 13/13 surface matrix + 3/3) — ZERO invented rules anywhere in the batch. ALL 13 law quotes byte-exact vs. their sources (claims/print-gating/statutory/livery/targets-razor/template/trust-page/developer-truth/comparison/customer-proof/counsel/locale/TS-000 color-guard laws). 30+ token hexes byte-exact vs. `tokens.css`/`web-tokens.css`. The console v2 §4 paragraph is byte-identical to its source. THE BRAND LAW reproduced hole-free across all 5 rails (no missing-occurrence gap, per the SITE-5 numbering-sequence lesson). Tag balance perfect (HTMLParser-verified) across all 5 files.

**Findings/delta (founder-facing, not adopted as canon fact)**: the handbook's "No drop shadows below 12% opacity" reads as a FLOOR, but `tokens.css`'s shadow scale runs 0.04–0.12 opacity — mostly BELOW that floor — and SITE-016 (media-kit) previously paraphrased the same rule as a CEILING. Logged on BRAND-000 as an open delta-pending-founder row (not given a delta number — described as an open item awaiting a founder ruling on which reading, if either, is correct). Separately, the spec's own text ("EM-030 dunning") was inexact: EM-030 is the payment-received email; the real dunning canon is EM-028 — the builder caught and corrected this with a grep before it reached the page, joining the growing spec-pointer-error family (cf. SITE batches' absence-claim family). Logo-fallback for non-designer_pack contexts remains OPEN §17 (re-verified still absent, not newly discovered).

**Judgment calls accepted**: sonic/motion direction and the brand-color-amendment item are listed as OPEN §17 items on BRAND-000 rather than invented or silently resolved, per the spec's own instruction. The designer_pack relationship is stated explicitly (5+ handbook rules cross-checked against `tokens.css`) rather than silently superseded — any conflict becomes a delta ticket, never a silent override.

**§17 escalations / backend-pending**: none — these are internal design-language documents with no runtime dependency. Founder-facing queue: the 12%-shadow floor-vs-ceiling ambiguity (BRAND-000, needs a founder ruling); logo-fallback for non-designer_pack contexts (open since SITE-4, still open).

**PHASE L (BRAND & SYSTEMS) OPENED at BRAND-1: 5 of 5 brand-book chapters built (BRAND-000…004), 1 of 6 Phase L batches.** Program totals now: 973 canon screens + 74 channel artifacts + 21 documents + 18 collateral + 25 website pages + brand & systems: 5 artifacts (Phase L opened).
**BRAND-1 COMPLETE, QA-PASSED (packaged) — 1 P1 fixed (BRAND-002 ₹ self-test undercount) + 1 P2 fixed (BRAND-000 line-range citation), founder ⬜ pending. Next: BRAND-2 illustration library + icon expansion (37→150), per `Design_Program/08_DP2_BATCH_PLAN_AND_PROMPTS.md` — Phase L continues (queue: BRAND-2/3, LOC-1/2, SYS-1).**

## BRAND-2 — Icon expansion (37→150) + illustration library — brand/ (Phase L continues, pipeline v4)
| ID | Page | Key finding | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| BRAND-005 | icon-canon.html — icon law + style spec (from the real 37) + full 150-icon registry | Law occurrence 1 of 5; **P1 FIXED — FALSE ABSENCE CLAIM AGAIN (3rd incident: SITE-3, SITE-4, now BRAND-2)** | Builder asserted `system/icons.svg` held only 26 symbols with 11 files "missing" (named all 11 by filename) — QA + senior recount found all 37 present on disk, none missing. The REAL, narrower finding underneath the false claim survives and is what actually mattered: 5 icons (star/dots/check/plus/close) show a visible rendering drift between the sprite and their individual canon files — logged as an open delta-pending-founder (which source wins permanently), replacing the false 11-missing claim. Correction written directly into BRAND-005, quoting the original wrong assertion verbatim alongside the true recount. |
| BRAND-006 | icons-commerce-fintech.html — 38 new icons drawn | Law occurrence 2 of 5; **P1 FIXED — fabricated citation slugs (part of the 14-slug / 28-occurrence defect class)** | W-numbers cited were real and topically correct, but filenames were paraphrased rather than copied verbatim (e.g. `W019-admin-catalogue` cited for the real `W019-admin-categories`) — a citation is a filename, not a description of one. Fixed by copy-paste from the source file. |
| BRAND-007 | icons-farm-ops.html — 38 new icons drawn | Law occurrence 3 of 5; remaining citation-slug fixes closed out here | **P2 flagged (queued as polish, not blocking)**: 4 derivative icons (chc-booking / notification-delivery family) read near-identical to their base icon at 24px — a differentiation pass is recommended. |
| BRAND-008 | icons-channels-system.html — 37 new icons drawn | Law occurrence 4 of 5; completes the 113 new icons (38+38+37) drawn this batch | All 113 new icons attr-conformant (24px viewBox, stroke-width 2, currentColor, round caps/joins) — 226/226 SVGs (113 new + 113 duplicate style-check pairs) byte-exact to the existing 37's style. |
| BRAND-009 | illustration-library.html — illustration canon + 10 PROPOSED spot illustrations | Law occurrence 5 of 5; illustrations folder was EMPTY pre-batch (inventory line 109) — first illustrations drawn for the canon | 10/10 illustrations verified zero `<text>` elements, hexes = real design tokens, 1 declared gradient. All 10 marked PROPOSED, awaiting founder visual approval — not adopted as final canon art until signed off. |

**THE ICON LAW (this batch's spine, authored and reproduced verbatim ×5 rails)**: *"An icon is a word in the interface's oldest language. Every icon in this set names a real thing the platform has — a module, an object, an action, a state. An icon for a feature that does not exist is a promise the interface cannot keep. And an icon never speaks alone: color never carries the meaning, and a label is never optional for actions."*

**QA verdict (BRAND-2): CONDITIONAL FAIL → fixed → PASS. Zero P0, 2 P1 classes, 1 P2.** **P1 class 1 — FALSE ABSENCE CLAIM AGAIN**: builder claimed the sprite had only 26 of 37 symbols with 11 named files "missing"; independent QA + senior recount confirmed all 37 present — the false claim is struck, and the real underlying finding (5-icon sprite/file rendering drift on star/dots/check/plus/close) is preserved as a founder-facing delta instead. **P1 class 2 — 14 fabricated citation slugs (28 occurrences across 4 files)**: real, topically-correct W-numbers cited with paraphrased rather than copied filenames (e.g. `W019-admin-catalogue` vs. the real `W019-admin-categories`); all 28 occurrences replaced with copy-pasted slugs across BRAND-006/007/008/009. **P2 — 4 derivative icons** (chc-booking/notification-delivery) near-identical to their base at 24px; differentiation pass queued as polish, not blocking.

**LESSON FOR MEMORY #1 — the absence-claim rule is now hardened, third incident**: this is the THIRD time a builder has asserted something canon-critical was absent without a pasted grep to prove it (SITE-3, SITE-4, now BRAND-2) — and the third time the claim was false on independent recount. **Rule hardened going forward: an absence claim without a pasted grep showing the negative result is REJECTED at QA automatically**, no exceptions, regardless of how specific or confident the claim reads (naming all 11 "missing" files did not make this one true).

**LESSON FOR MEMORY #2 — citation slugs are copy-paste, never paraphrase**: the 14-slug defect class (28 occurrences) shows that a citation can be topically correct and directionally helpful while still being factually wrong, because a citation's job is to name an exact file, not describe one accurately. **New rule: citation slugs must be copied verbatim from the source, never typed from memory or paraphrased** — a citation is a filename, not a description, even when the description is accurate.

**QA strengths (all re-verified in python)**: registry math perfect — 150 rows, all unique, 38+38+37=113 new icons, bidirectional sheet↔registry match confirmed. Attr conformance 226/226 SVGs byte-exact to the existing-set style attributes. Zero green-at-rest (icons-at-rest-never-green console law holds across all new icons). Illustrations 10/10 clean: zero `<text>` elements, hexes = real tokens, 1 declared gradient. Mirroring math: 18 mirrored + 131 non-mirrored + 1 exempt = 150. All 37 pre-existing icon files independently re-verified present on disk, unmodified — not redrawn.

**Findings/delta (founder-facing, not adopted as canon fact)**: sprite-vs-file rendering drift on 5 icons (star/dots/check/plus/close) — which source (individual file vs. `system/icons.svg` sprite) wins permanently is an open founder decision; batch convention adopted meanwhile: individual files are canonical over the sprite. 10 illustrations are PROPOSED pending founder visual approval, not final. 4 derivative-icon pairs (chc-booking/notification-delivery) flagged for a differentiation pass — polish, not a blocker.

**Judgment calls accepted**: THE ICON LAW's "a label is never optional for actions" clause is stated as a rule for the icon set to obey, not retrofitted onto every existing screen in this batch (that remains a separate audit). The sprite-drift and derivative-icon findings are logged as open items rather than silently resolved one way — any resolution becomes a delta ticket, never a silent pick.

**§17 escalations / backend-pending**: none new — assets seed `designer_pack/assets/illustrations/` (currently empty, per inventory line 109) and sprite regeneration happen at adoption, not in this design-only batch. Founder-facing queue: sprite-vs-file rendering-drift decision (5 icons); 10 illustrations awaiting visual approval; derivative-icon differentiation polish (queued, non-blocking).

**PHASE L (BRAND & SYSTEMS) CONTINUES at BRAND-2: 113 new icons + 150-icon registry + illustration canon + 10 PROPOSED illustrations built (BRAND-005…009), 2 of 6 Phase L batches.** Program totals now: 973 canon screens + 74 channel artifacts + 21 documents + 18 collateral + 25 website pages + brand & systems: 10 artifacts (5 brand-book chapters + 5 icon/illustration chapters); icon set 37→150 designed.
**BRAND-2 COMPLETE, QA-PASSED (packaged) — zero P0, 2 P1 classes fixed (false-absence-claim-again + 14 fabricated citation slugs) + 1 P2 queued as polish (derivative-icon differentiation), founder ⬜ pending. Next: BRAND-3 app-store assets + social kit + motion/sonic, per `Design_Program/08_DP2_BATCH_PLAN_AND_PROMPTS.md` — Phase L continues (queue: LOC-1/2, SYS-1).**

**PHASE L (BRAND & SYSTEMS) CONTINUES at BRAND-3: app-store assets + social kit + motion/sonic, 3rd of 6 Phase L batches — per `Design_Program/08_DP2_BATCH_PLAN_AND_PROMPTS.md` (queue: BRAND-3 app-store assets + social kit + motion/sonic DONE · LOC-1 9 language font packs + RTL implementation + Nastaliq · LOC-2 country compliance variants (EU/US/GCC/JP-KR) + multi-currency display spec · SYS-1 dark-mode console pass + Senior Mode + tablet layouts + web QA automation).**

## BRAND-3 — App-store assets + social kit + motion/sonic — brand/ (Phase L continues, pipeline v4)
| ID | Page | Key finding | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| BRAND-010 | app-icon.html — adaptive/iOS icon construction, clear-space grid, size matrix | Law occurrence 1 of 5; mark SVG coordinates byte-match the real logo file | The adaptive-icon safe-zone geometry and full iOS/Android size matrix are derived directly from the existing mark, not redrawn from preference — QA independently re-verified the mark's SVG path coordinates against the real logo asset and found byte-exact agreement. |
| BRAND-011 | store-listings.html — screenshots, feature graphic, copy anatomy (store title/short/full description, iOS subtitle/promotional text) | Law occurrence 2 of 5; slug diff 37/37 real canon-screen paths, zero fabricated | Every referenced screenshot cites a real canon screen by exact filename (BRAND-2's copy-paste-citation lesson applied and held: 37/37 real paths, zero fabricated slugs this batch). Char-count labels independently python-verified 13/13 true: store title 28, short description 60, full description 1355, iOS subtitle 12/promotional text 27, 8 screenshot captions. Copy carries only adjudicated claims — invented-metric scan CLEAN. |
| BRAND-012 | social-kit.html — profile avatars, cover art, post + story templates across platforms | Law occurrence 3 of 5; invented-metric scan CLEAN across the templates | Zero ratings, download counts, or superlatives anywhere in the template copy or placeholder captions; handle/hashtag conventions left as `{{vars}}`, not invented, and flagged to the founder queue. |
| BRAND-013 | motion-system.html — motion tokens, transition patterns, reduced-motion behavior | Law occurrence 4 of 5; **ARBITRATED — motion tokens ruled REAL, table correctly COMPILED not PROPOSED** | `tokens.css` genuinely defines `--duration-fast` (150ms) / `--duration-base` (220ms) / `--duration-slow` (320ms) / `--duration-slower` (500ms) plus 3 easing curves — the builder's COMPILED (not invented) framing was upheld on arbitration. Reduced-motion audit independently re-verified 6/6: every keyframe block is wrapped in `prefers-reduced-motion`, and only `transform`/`opacity` properties animate. A second, genuinely different token set was found in `designer_pack/10-motion-principles.md` (`--motion-fast:120ms` etc., different bezier curves) — logged as an open delta-pending-founder (which set is law), not silently reconciled either way. |
| BRAND-014 | sonic-brand.html — earcons, notification sounds, IVR voice direction (compiles IVR-007) | Law occurrence 5 of 5; **P1 FIXED — ₹ rendered twice via `&#8377;`, a byte-exact quote from IVR-007's own ratified example** | QA ruled a quoted-from-elsewhere ₹ is still a rendered ₹ under this batch's zero-exception rule, regardless of source; the passage was reworded to preserve the pedagogy (why the glyph matters in IVR) without reproducing the symbol itself. Sonic direction otherwise COMPILED, not invented: voice-casting notes, the 60%-pace law, and the pentatonic earcon motif are all byte-exact quotes vs. IVR-007. PU-000 notification-tier names are byte-exact; `promo` tier is silent; `weather.alert_severe` is the sole exemption from the silence default — both independently re-verified. |

**THE LAUNCH-ASSET LAW (this batch's spine, authored and reproduced verbatim ×5 rails)**: *"A store listing is the platform's first promise to a stranger. Screenshots show real canon screens, copy carries only adjudicated claims, and no rating, download count, or award appears anywhere before it exists. The same law dresses every public asset: social posts, preview videos, sounds — nothing launches before the thing it promises."*

**QA verdict (BRAND-3): CONDITIONAL FAIL → fixed → PASS. Zero P0, 2 P1, 1 P2.** **P1 — BRAND-014 rendered ₹ twice via `&#8377;`**, a byte-exact quote lifted from IVR-007's own ratified example; QA ruled that a quoted-from-elsewhere ₹ is still a rendered ₹ under the batch's zero-exception rule, and the passage was reworded rather than exempted. **P2 — one citation was missing its `krishi-verse/` path prefix** — fixed.

**LESSON FOR MEMORY — the zero-₹ rule is absolute per batch spec, with no quoting exception**: this batch's spec called for ZERO rupee glyphs anywhere, and the builder's defense that the ₹ in BRAND-014 was a byte-exact quote from an already-ratified IVR-007 example did not survive QA — **quoting a ratified example does not exempt the glyph; rewording preserves the pedagogy without the symbol.** This hardens the ₹-confinement discipline already seen across BRAND-1/2 and the SITE batches: there is no "it's just a quote" carve-out. Separately, **BRAND-2's citation-slug lesson held at 37/37 this batch** — zero fabricated slugs, confirming the copy-paste-citation rule generalizes cleanly to a second batch running under it.

**QA strengths (all independently re-verified)**: invented-metric scan CLEAN — zero ratings, download counts, awards, or superlatives anywhere in the batch; ~24 apparent hits were false positives (SVG bezier-curve coordinates, not text). Slug diff 37/37 real canon-screen paths (zero fabricated slugs this batch — BRAND-2's lesson held). Char-count labels 13/13 python-true (store title 28, short description 60, full description 1355, iOS subtitle 12/promotional text 27, 8 screenshot captions). Reduced-motion audit 6/6 keyframes wrapped in `prefers-reduced-motion`, transform/opacity only. Sonic COMPILED quotes byte-exact vs. IVR-007 (voice casting, 60%-pace law, pentatonic earcon motif). PU-000 tier names byte-exact; `promo` silent; `weather.alert_severe` the sole exemption. Mark SVG coordinates byte-match the real logo file.

**Arbitration items (both resolved in the builder's favor)**: (1) motion tokens are REAL — `tokens.css` `--duration-fast` (150ms) / `--duration-base` (220ms) / `--duration-slow` (320ms) / `--duration-slower` (500ms) + 3 easing curves — so the motion table's COMPILED (not PROPOSED) framing stands. (2) `designer_pack/10-motion-principles.md` genuinely carries a DIFFERENT token set (`--motion-fast:120ms` etc., different bezier curves) — a TRUE delta-pending-founder, not a builder error: **which motion values are law, `tokens.css` or the `designer_pack/10-motion-principles.md` doc?**

**Judgment calls accepted**: tenant app-icon approval workflow, sonic production gate (vendor/format/recordings), and social handle/hashtag conventions are all left as `{{vars}}`/open items rather than invented — each flagged to the founder/§17 queue instead of silently decided in the design layer.

**§17 escalations / backend-pending**: none — these are internal design-language documents with no runtime dependency. Founder-facing queue: motion-token values conflict (`tokens.css` vs. `designer_pack/10-motion-principles.md`, needs a founder ruling on which is law); tenant app-icon approval workflow (OPEN); sonic production gate (vendor/format/recordings, not yet commissioned); social handle/hashtag conventions (`{{vars}}`, not yet decided).

**PHASE L (BRAND & SYSTEMS) CONTINUES at BRAND-3: app-icon canon + store-listing anatomy + social kit + motion system (COMPILED) + sonic brand (compiles IVR-007) built (BRAND-010…014), 3 of 6 Phase L batches.** Program totals now: 973 canon screens + 74 channel artifacts + 21 documents + 18 collateral + 25 website pages + brand & systems: 15 artifacts (5 brand-book chapters + 5 icon/illustration chapters + 5 app-store/social/motion/sonic pages).
**BRAND-3 COMPLETE, QA-PASSED (packaged) — zero P0, 2 P1 fixed (₹-quote-exception rejected + citation path prefix) + 1 P2 fixed, founder ⬜ pending. PHASE L (BRAND & SYSTEMS): 3 of 6 Phase L batches remain (LOC-1/2, SYS-1). Next: LOC-1 9 language font packs + RTL implementation + Nastaliq, per `Design_Program/08_DP2_BATCH_PLAN_AND_PROMPTS.md` — Phase L continues.**

## LOC-1 — 9 language font packs + RTL implementation + Nastaliq — brand/ (Phase L continues, pipeline v4)
| ID | Page | Key finding | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| BRAND-015 | language-font-packs.html — 9 font-pack cards (mr/pa/bn/te/ta/kn/ml/or/as), ALL PROPOSED §17 | Law occurrence 1 of 5; Hind-sibling-first principle asserted where real siblings exist | Hind Siliguri asserted for bn/as, Hind Guntur for te, Hind Madurai for ta (real Hind-family siblings, not generic substitutes); the Baloo-2 family is proposed for pa/kn/ml/or but every Baloo-2 candidate carries an explicit `{{verify availability}}` flag because the network was down at build time and font-CDN availability could not be confirmed live — a disclosed gap, not a silent assumption. All 9 cards are ALL PROPOSED, §17, awaiting founder sign-off; none adopted as canon type. |
| BRAND-016 | script-metrics.html — cross-script harmonization (x-height/cap-height/baseline) + rural font-budget law | Law occurrence 2 of 5; rural font-budget value flagged PROPOSED for founder ruling | Cross-script metrics table harmonizes the 9 new scripts against the existing Latin/Devanagari/Gujarati baseline; the rural font-budget law (a byte-weight ceiling per language pack, tied to low-end-device/low-bandwidth realities) is authored but its numeric value is PROPOSED, not adopted — a founder-facing delta, same discipline as BRAND-1's shadow-floor item. |
| BRAND-017 | rtl-implementation.html — design-system `[dir=rtl]` spec beyond SITE-023's demo page | Law occurrence 3 of 5; **PROGRAM WIN — REVERSE ABSENCE CATCH**; **P1 FIXED ×2 (missing PROPOSED chip/rows on Noto Naskh Arabic load; fabricated self-test category)** | The spec's own assumption — that zero `[dir=rtl]` rules exist anywhere in the live system CSS — was WRONG, and the builder caught it: a grep found 3 REAL `[dir=rtl]` rules already in production (`web-components.css:84`, `web-components.css:184`, `web-frame.css:195`), and the spec's assumption was corrected in-file with the pasted grep evidence rather than silently trusted. This is the mirror image of the SITE-3/4/BRAND-2 false-absence-claim family: here the builder disproved a FALSE absence assumption baked into the spec itself, protecting the canon in the opposite direction from the usual failure mode. Separately, two P1s were fixed at QA: (1) the page loaded Noto Naskh Arabic for a demo specimen with no PROPOSED chip and two missing self-test audit rows — chip and rows added; (2) the page's own `direction:ltr` self-test claimed 11 occurrences under a fabricated header-comment category — the true, grep-verified count is 12 (2 of them live) — corrected. All system CSS files independently checksum-verified UNTOUCHED by this batch — the spec is additive-only PROPOSED patches, not applied edits. |
| BRAND-018 | nastaliq-urdu.html — Urdu (ur) Nastaliq/bidi direction, machine-draft specimens, beyond-12 honesty | Law occurrence 4 of 5; **P1 FIXED — watermark gap, precedent misquoted to excuse it** | The page originally rendered 3 Urdu specimen blocks but chipped only 1 as a machine-draft, justifying the gap by citing SITE-023's watermarking pattern — a citation that, on independent re-check, is the OPPOSITE of what SITE-023 actually does (SITE-023 chips every single rendered block, no exceptions). The 2 unchipped blocks were not machine-draft-honest and the precedent used to defend the gap did not say what it was cited as saying. Fixed: chip count now 3:3, and the self-test corrected to state the true count. Urdu (`ur`) is explicitly framed as a beyond-12-languages FORWARD canon entry — not claimed live, not one of the 12-language Phase-3 target set, a deliberately named future-facing addition. |
| BRAND-019 | loc-rollout-canon.html — the language-shipping playbook (what "a language ships" means) | Law occurrence 5 of 5; ties audit_w51/EM subject-length limits + IVR-007 into one checklist | Completes THE FONT-PACK LAW's 5-occurrence reproduction. The playbook operationalizes the law's "type, lengths, and words, in that language" clause into a concrete 3-part gate: a chosen/harmonized face (type), a length audit against `audit_w51.py`'s GSM/UCS-2 SMS-segment math and the EM subject-line character limits (lengths), and a real in-language content audit (words) — with IVR-007's existing voice-casting/pacing canon cited as the audio-channel analogue of the same 3-part discipline. No language is marked shippable in this canon until all three gates are named as passed. |

**THE FONT-PACK LAW (this batch's spine, authored and reproduced verbatim ×5 rails, hole-free)**: *"A font pack is not a language launch, and a fallback square is not a font. Every script the platform serves gets a face chosen for it, harmonized with the family, and audited in it — type, lengths, and words, in that language. No language ships until all three pass, and no face becomes canon without the founder's yes."*

**QA verdict (LOC-1): CONDITIONAL FAIL → fixed → PASS. 0 P0, 3 P1.** (1) **BRAND-017 loaded Noto Naskh Arabic with no PROPOSED chip and two missing self-test audit rows** — chip/disclosure added, both rows added. (2) **BRAND-017's `direction:ltr` self-test claimed 11 occurrences under a fabricated header-comment category — the true, grep-verified count is 12 (2 live)** — the false-self-test-count family recurring a SECOND time within the very batch (BRAND-1's ₹ undercount, and now this) that had already pledged corrections earlier in the program; corrected to the grep-true figure. (3) **BRAND-018's watermark gap** — 3 rendered Urdu blocks, only 1 chipped, defended by misquoting SITE-023's own pattern (which in fact chips every block, no exceptions) — corrected to 3:3, self-test reworded to match.

**PROGRAM WIN — REVERSE ABSENCE CATCH (recorded prominently)**: this batch's spec itself assumed no `[dir=rtl]` rules existed anywhere in the live system CSS; the builder grepped before trusting that assumption and found 3 REAL ones already in production — `web-components.css:84`, `web-components.css:184`, `web-frame.css:195` — and corrected the spec's own false assumption in-file with the pasted evidence, rather than building on top of a wrong premise. Every prior absence-claim lesson in this program (SITE-3, SITE-4, BRAND-2) was about a builder falsely claiming something canon-critical was ABSENT when it existed; this is the mirror case — a spec falsely assumed something was absent, and the builder's own grep discipline caught it before it shipped as an error. **The absence-claims rule now demonstrably protects the canon in both directions: against false claims of absence, and against false assumptions of absence baked into a spec.**

**LESSONS FOR MEMORY**: (1) **false self-test counts recur even after an in-batch correction pass** — BRAND-017's fabricated `direction:ltr` category count is the second false-self-test-count incident this program has logged (after BRAND-1's BRAND-002 ₹ undercount), and it happened inside a batch whose own spine law explicitly promises accuracy — proof that QA's independent recount is not a one-time fix but a permanently mandatory step, batch after batch, with no exemption for batches that have already "learned the lesson" once. (2) **precedent citations can be misquoted to excuse a gap** — BRAND-018 cited SITE-023's watermarking pattern to justify only chipping 1 of 3 Urdu blocks, but SITE-023 actually chips every block with no exceptions; the citation was backwards. **A precedent cite is a factual claim like any other and must be independently verified against the cited file, never trusted at face value just because it names a real prior batch.**

**Findings/delta for the founder queue (not adopted as canon fact)**: TS-001's existing font-column convention specifies a generic "Noto Sans {Script}" per language, while this batch's candidates are Hind-sibling-first (Hind Siliguri/Guntur/Madurai) or Baloo-2-family — a genuine §17 conflict between two real, competing canon principles (generic pan-script coverage vs. brand-family harmonization), logged for the founder to rule which wins, not silently resolved either way. Separately, Fraunces (the display typeface) is honestly documented as Latin-script-only — it has no non-Latin harmonization — so a per-script display-face strategy for headlines in the 9 new scripts is flagged PROPOSED, an open design question, not invented.

**Judgment calls accepted**: all 9 font-pack candidates + the Nastaliq candidate + the demo Naskh specimen are left as ALL PROPOSED §17 items pending founder visual sign-off, never silently adopted as canon type. The rural font-budget numeric ceiling, the per-script display-face strategy, and the TS-001-vs-sibling-family principle conflict are all logged as open founder-facing items rather than decided in the design layer.

**§17 escalations / backend-pending (founder/engineering queue)**: all 9 font-pack candidates + the Nastaliq candidate + the demo-Naskh specimen (founder visual sign-off); the TS-001-generic-Noto-Sans vs. sibling-family principle conflict (which wins); the rural font-budget numeric value; the per-script display-face strategy (Fraunces-is-Latin-only); the gate-32b RTL checklist; the white-label-vs-legibility rule; numerals/kasheeda handling for Nastaliq. **Backend-pending**: the font-subsetting pipeline (not yet built); Baloo-2 availability verification (network was down at build time, all Baloo-2 candidates carry `{{verify availability}}`).

**PHASE L (BRAND & SYSTEMS) CONTINUES at LOC-1: 9 font-pack cards + cross-script metrics/rural-font-budget law + design-system RTL spec + Nastaliq/Urdu forward canon + language-shipping playbook built (BRAND-015…019), 4 of 6 Phase L batches.** Program totals now: 973 canon screens + 74 channel artifacts + 21 documents + 18 collateral + 25 website pages + brand & systems: 20 artifacts (5 brand-book chapters + 5 icon/illustration chapters + 5 app-store/social/motion/sonic pages + 5 font-pack/RTL/Nastaliq/rollout pages).
**LOC-1 COMPLETE, QA-PASSED (packaged) — 0 P0, 3 P1 fixed (missing PROPOSED chip/rows + fabricated self-test category + watermark-gap/misquoted-precedent), founder ⬜ pending. PHASE L (BRAND & SYSTEMS): 2 of 6 Phase L batches remain (LOC-2, SYS-1). Next: LOC-2 country compliance variants (EU/US/GCC/JP-KR) + multi-currency display spec, per `Design_Program/08_DP2_BATCH_PLAN_AND_PROMPTS.md` — Phase L continues.**

## LOC-2 — Country compliance variants (EU/US/GCC/JP-KR) + multi-currency display spec — brand/ (Phase L continues, pipeline v4)
| ID | Page | Key finding | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| BRAND-020 | country-variant-framework.html — variant architecture + THE COUNTRY-PACK LAW (occurrence 1 of 5) + a self-audit grep table of every country/currency reference in the batch | Law occurrence 1 of 5; **P1 FIXED — 2 stray rupee entities in the batch's own grep table** | The framework's self-audit grep table, built to prove every non-INR/foreign reference was accounted for, itself carried 2 stray `&#8377;`/rupee-glyph entities inside its own rows — quoted glyphs count under the program's standing ₹-confinement discipline (BRAND-3's "a quote doesn't exempt the glyph" ruling applied again), even inside a table whose whole job is auditing glyphs. Fixed: both entities removed, table re-verified glyph-clean. |
| BRAND-021 | eu-variant.html — GDPR/e-invoicing/EUR/metric, every statutory field `{{counsel}}` | Law occurrence 2 of 5; **P1 FIXED — DPDP "verbatim" cite missing inner quotation marks** | The page's DPDP cross-reference labeled itself a verbatim quote but rendered without the source's own inner quotation marks — a byte-exactness gap on a page whose citation standard is "verbatim means verbatim." Fixed by restoring the quote marks byte-for-byte against the source. |
| BRAND-022 | us-variant.html — state sales-tax patchwork/USD/imperial units | Law occurrence 3 of 5; clean, zero fixes | Every state sales-tax rate and threshold is an unfilled `{{counsel}}`/`{{confirm}}` socket — the 50-state patchwork is named as a jurisdiction-count fact, never populated with a single asserted rate. |
| BRAND-023 | gcc-variant.html — RTL+Arabic ties, VAT/ZATCA, Hijri-calendar question, AED/SAR formats | Law occurrence 4 of 5; **P0 FIXED, DOCTRINE-MAKING — RATE-BY-NEGATION** | The page's own compliance-honesty line — "NEVER printed as 5%/15%" — meant to prove UAE/KSA VAT rates were withheld, instead RENDERED the exact real rates it claimed to withhold, by naming them in order to disclaim them. QA extended the quoted-glyph doctrine to numbers: **"a disclaimed rate is still a rendered rate."** Fixed: the digits themselves are withheld entirely — no VAT percentage appears anywhere on the page, in assertion or in denial; the disclaimer now names no rate at all, only `{{counsel}}`. GCC Arabic: 3 specimens, 3 machine-draft chips (3:3); 4 bidi exceptions, all 4 verified justified. Hijri dual-calendar display flagged as a §17 scope question, not attempted. |
| BRAND-024 | multi-currency-spec.html — Law 2 made visual: 10-currency table incl. JPY/KRW zero-decimal, FX-honesty law | Law occurrence 5 of 5; **P1 FIXED — invented "citation-class" carve-out struck** | The builder had excused §7's quote glyphs via a self-invented "citation-class" exception, not a real sanctioned zone in the spec. QA struck the invented category — the spec's sanctioned zones are exhaustive, and a builder may not add one — and re-tagged the block as a captioned FORMAT-EXAMPLE, the spec's only actual sanctioned glyph zone. Minor-unit math independently re-verified 10/10 Law-2-true, including the zero-decimal trap (¥1,234 stored as `1234`, never `123400`). FX rates timestamped + sourced + marked INDICATIVE throughout, per the FX-honesty law. |

**THE COUNTRY-PACK LAW (this batch's spine, authored and reproduced verbatim ×5 rails, hole-free)**: *"A country is not a translation — it is a jurisdiction. Every country variant ships as a pack: master data, counsel-approved clauses, statutory fields, and formats — versioned, dated, and owned by counsel in that jurisdiction. This canon draws the sockets; the law of the land fills them. No rate, threshold, or statutory field on these pages is asserted — every one is a `{{var}}` until counsel in that country signs it."* Reproduced alongside two supporting laws carried from prior batches: the dated-fact law ("a rate is a dated fact, not a constant") and the FX-honesty law (every FX rate shown is timestamped, sourced, and marked INDICATIVE).

**QA verdict (LOC-2): CONDITIONAL FAIL → fixed → PASS. 1 P0 (doctrine-making), 3 P1.** **P0 — RATE-BY-NEGATION**: BRAND-023's own compliance line, "NEVER printed as 5%/15%," rendered the real UAE/KSA VAT rates by naming them in order to disclaim them — QA ruled a disclaimed rate is still a rendered rate, extending the quoted-glyph doctrine to numbers; fixed by withholding the digits entirely, with no rate named even in denial. **P1s**: (1) BRAND-020's grep table carried 2 stray rupee entities — quoted glyphs count, removed; (2) BRAND-024's §7 quote-glyphs were excused via an invented "citation-class" carve-out — struck and re-tagged as a captioned FORMAT-EXAMPLE block, the spec's only sanctioned zone; (3) BRAND-021's DPDP "verbatim" cite was missing its inner quotation marks — byte-exactness enforced, restored.

**LESSON FOR MEMORY — RATE-BY-NEGATION, the quoted-glyph doctrine now covers NUMBERS**: this batch establishes that assertion-by-negation is still assertion. A page that writes "we never print the rate as 5%" has printed 5% — the disclaiming frame does not launder the digit out of the rendered page. **The doctrine hardens: specific statutory digits never render, anywhere, even inside a sentence built to disclaim them.** This generalizes BRAND-3's "a quote doesn't exempt the glyph" ruling (rupee symbols) to numeric statutory facts (VAT rates, thresholds, periods) — there is no rhetorical frame, disclaimer, or citation-class exception that exempts a real number from the confinement discipline. Filed alongside a second, narrower rule from BRAND-024: **no invented exception classes** — a builder may not manufacture a new "sanctioned zone" (like "citation-class") to excuse a violation; the spec's own sanctioned zones are exhaustive, and only QA/founder can add one.

**SECOND REVERSE-ABSENCE WIN (confirmed, joins LOC-1's `[dir=rtl]` catch as the program's second instance of this pattern)**: rather than assuming non-INR currency usage was absent from the canon and building a spec around that assumption, the builder tested for absence and found it FALSE — non-INR canon usage is real and pre-existing: DOC-014 is a full-USD export invoice, `screens/420` carries byte-verified USD/MT prices, the schema's `currencies` table already carries a `minor_units` column, and migration 09 sets `DEFAULT 'USD'` on at least one column. The builder verified all four citations before claiming multi-currency precedent existed, rather than asserting it from memory. **Absence tested before claiming, a second consecutive time this program (after LOC-1's `[dir=rtl]` reverse-absence catch) — the absence-claims rule continues to protect the canon in both directions: against false claims of absence, and via active testing that confirms a true prior existence instead of guessing at one.**

**QA strengths, independently re-verified**: minor-unit math 10/10 Law-2-true across BRAND-024's currency table, including the zero-decimal trap (¥1,234 = `1234` stored, never `123400`, and the equivalent KRW case). Law 2 and the §7 quotes are byte-exact against the contract. Country scan CLEAN across all 5 files — every market referenced (EU/US/GCC/JP-KR) wears its own TARGET chip, zero "we operate in" language anywhere. Statutory-fact scan CLEAN post-fix — zero asserted rates, periods, or thresholds anywhere in the batch; every one is an unfilled `{{counsel}}`/`{{confirm}}` socket. GCC Arabic specimens 3:3 machine-draft chipped. Bidi exceptions 4/4 independently verified justified. JP-KR scope (whether JP-KR gets dedicated pages like EU/US/GCC, or stays framework-only) is flagged as an open founder scope call, not silently decided either way.

**Judgment calls accepted**: JP-KR's lighter treatment (framework-referenced rather than a dedicated variant page, unlike EU/US/GCC) is flagged as an explicit founder-facing scope question — build dedicated JP-KR pages in a future batch, or leave JP-KR framework-only — not resolved silently in the design layer. Hijri dual-calendar display, Arabic-Indic numerals, the rounding-mode (engineering) decision, a per-jurisdiction counsel network, and mandi-vocabulary localization for EU/US are all logged as open §17/delta items rather than invented.

**§17 escalations / deltas pending founder**: JP-KR dedicated pages (the scope call above); Hijri dual-calendar display; Arabic-Indic numerals for GCC; rounding mode (an engineering decision, not a design one); a counsel network per jurisdiction (EU/US/GCC/JP-KR each need their own signing counsel, not one global reviewer); mandi-vocabulary localization for EU/US markets. **Backend-pending**: the country-pack config schema (not yet built — this canon draws the sockets, per THE COUNTRY-PACK LAW); FX rate source/timestamp plumbing (the `fx_rates` table already exists in schema — cited, not newly built).

**PHASE L (BRAND & SYSTEMS) CONTINUES at LOC-2: 5 country-compliance variant pages (BRAND-020…024) built — variant framework + EU + US + GCC + multi-currency spec, 5 of 6 Phase L batches.** Program totals now: 973 canon screens + 74 channel artifacts + 21 documents + 18 collateral + 25 website pages + brand & systems: 25 artifacts (5 brand-book chapters + 5 icon/illustration chapters + 5 app-store/social/motion/sonic pages + 5 font-pack/RTL/Nastaliq/rollout pages + 5 country-variant/multi-currency pages).
**LOC-2 COMPLETE, QA-PASSED (packaged) — 1 P0 fixed (RATE-BY-NEGATION, doctrine-making) + 3 P1 fixed (stray rupee entities + invented citation-class carve-out + DPDP verbatim quote-marks), founder ⬜ pending. PHASE L (BRAND & SYSTEMS): 1 of 6 Phase L batches remains (SYS-1). Next: SYS-1 dark-mode console pass + Senior Mode + tablet layouts + web QA automation, per `Design_Program/08_DP2_BATCH_PLAN_AND_PROMPTS.md` — Phase L FINAL batch.**

## SYS-1 — Dark-mode console pass + Senior Mode + tablet layouts + web QA automation — brand/ (PHASE L FINAL BATCH, pipeline v4)
| ID | Page | Key finding | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| BRAND-025 | dark-mode-console.html — dark token layer (ALL values PROPOSED §17) + rendered dark console demo | Spine page; **14 contrast ratios independently recomputed exact by qa_gates.py, including 3 REAL AA failures the page itself surfaces** | The dark candidate palette is honest about its own gaps: primary-600 (2.57:1), danger (2.92:1) and info (3.13:1) all fail WCAG AA on the dark surface as currently drafted, and the page reports this rather than hiding it. Builder #1 died mid-batch after finishing this file (clean-relaunch protocol invoked, disk state verified intact before resuming). Every dark value is chip-marked PROPOSED — none is canon until founder visual sign-off (red-chrome rejection history, W-D2.1, makes this non-negotiable). |
| BRAND-026 | senior-mode.html — Senior Farmer Mode system spec + before/after screen pair | **P1 FIXED — two-builder seam: `.compiled` chip class missing** | BRAND-026 was missing the `.compiled` chip class present in BRAND-025/027/028 — a seam left by the builder #1→#2 handoff. Fixed: class added, both occurrences styled to match its siblings, **and its own self-test row was found to have miscounted "2 Compiled rows" against the true count of 1 — the false-count family recurring again, caught during the seam fix itself, not a separate incident.** Grounds in screen 529 + contract §8. |
| BRAND-027 | tablet-layouts.html — tablet canon rules, compiling kiosk W380–389 + §6 breakpoints | Clean, zero fixes | Built by builder #2 after the builder #1 death; no defects found on independent QA. |
| BRAND-028 | web-qa-automation.html — 40-gate automation map + REAL `qa_gates.py` run results pasted in | **THE TOOL IS REAL (QA's headline finding)** — independent re-run reproduced this page's pasted results almost byte-for-byte | The one delta between the independent re-run and the pasted results is explained on the page itself: `--expect-files 27` was the pre-027/028 snapshot count, explicitly labeled as historical, not a discrepancy. Built by builder #2. |
| qa_gates.py | Real, runnable, stdlib-only Python auditor at farmer root (not in `brand/`) | `py_compile` clean, no network calls, dogfooded against the live canon | Implements the program's accumulated mechanical gates (no-script/₹-scan/logical-props/tag-balance/law-numbering-holes/footer-constancy/hex-audit/@media/file-counts). 40 gates classified: 10 AUTOMATABLE / 20 PARTIAL / 9 JUDGMENT (= 39; the 40th is the §14 "Gates 1-25" parenthetical gap itself, honestly disclosed as a contract-text hole rather than silently rounded up to 40). |

**THE VARIANT LAW (this batch's spine, authored and reproduced verbatim ×5 rails, hole-free — plus once more in `qa_gates.py`'s own docstring, the first law to be carried into runnable code)**: *"Dark, senior, tablet — a variant is not a theme, it is the same promise kept for different eyes and hands. Every variant preserves every law of the canon: contrast, tap sizes, money rules, states, honesty. A variant that loses a law is not a variant — it is a regression wearing a costume."*

**QA verdict (SYS-1): CONDITIONAL FAIL → fixed → PASS. 0 P0, 1 P1, 2 P2.** **P1 — two-builder seam**: BRAND-026 was missing the `.compiled` chip class that BRAND-025/027/028 all carry (the builder #1 death mid-batch left a style gap at the handoff to builder #2) — fixed by adding the class, styling both occurrences, and correcting BRAND-026's own self-test row, which had separately miscounted "2 Compiled rows" against the true count of 1 (the false-count family surfacing a third time this program, found while fixing an unrelated seam defect). **P2s**: (1) `prefers-contrast` is unaddressed across the dark-mode candidates — now recorded as a PROPOSED future-variant question on BRAND-025, not silently deferred; (2) a historical run-command reproducibility note on qa_gates.py's documented invocation.

**THE TOOL IS REAL — QA's headline finding**: `qa_gates.py`, re-run independently by QA rather than trusted from BRAND-028's pasted output, reproduced those results almost byte-for-byte. The one delta is explained on the page itself: `--expect-files 27` was the pre-027/028 snapshot, explicitly labeled historical rather than a live discrepancy. `py_compile` ran clean, the script is stdlib-only with no network calls, and all 14 contrast ratios on BRAND-025 were independently recomputed exact — including the 3 REAL AA failures the page itself surfaces (primary-600 2.57:1, danger 2.92:1, info 3.13:1 on dark candidates). This is the program's first mechanically-verified, re-runnable QA tool, not a one-off audit narrative.

**CANON FINDINGS the tool surfaced (for the founder, not fixed in this batch)**: (1) **BRAND-023 carries an orphan `</tr>` around line 172** — a LOC-2 escape that human QA missed and the tool caught; (2) a **systemic literal `#fff` vs. `--color-text-inverse`** token-bypass pattern across 26 of 27 brand files and 25 of 25 site files; (3) **zero `pointer:`/`hover:` media queries anywhere in `system/web`** — DELTA-001's density work cannot currently detect touch; (4) **contract §14's "Gates 1-25" parenthetical lists only 24 items** — a contract-text gap, flagged for a possible founder v1.3 fix; (5) **screen 529's CSS is `font-size:118%` while its own comment claims "+30-45%"** — a canon-internal discrepancy between code and comment, found while grounding Senior Mode in 529.

**LESSON FOR MEMORY — automation catches what eyes miss, and the false-count family struck twice more**: the tool caught a real defect (BRAND-023's orphan tag) inside canon that had already been QA-passed by humans — the clearest evidence yet that a mechanical gate and a human reviewer catch different classes of error, and neither substitutes for the other. **`qa_gates.py` is PROPOSED for adoption as a standing pre-QA step in pipeline v5.** Separately, the false-count family (BRAND-1's ₹ undercount, LOC-1's fabricated `direction:ltr` category count) struck twice more this batch alone: once as builder #2's own dogfood catching a miscount pre-ship, and once when the BRAND-026 seam fix uncovered a second, unrelated miscount ("2 Compiled rows" vs. the true 1) in the very self-test row being repaired. **QA's independent recount remains mandatory forever — a lesson "learned" once, twice, or even inside a fix for something else, does not retire the rule.**

**§17 escalations / backend-pending (founder/engineering queue)**: ALL dark-mode values (founder visual approval required — red-chrome rejection history makes this non-negotiable); the senior-type size multiplier; the farmer-app-on-tablet scaling decision; a `pointer:coarse` strategy (currently absent from `system/web` entirely); a `prefers-contrast` variant (PROPOSED, unaddressed); `qa_gates.py` pipeline adoption (PROPOSED for v5); approval to fix BRAND-023's orphan `</tr>`; a program-wide `#fff`→token systemic cleanup decision; §14's 24-item contract-text gap (candidate for v1.3). **Backend-pending**: dark-mode toggle/auto plumbing; Senior Mode activation flow.

**PHASE L (BRAND & SYSTEMS) CONTINUES at SYS-1: dark-mode console + Senior Mode + tablet layouts + web QA automation built (BRAND-025…028) + `qa_gates.py`, 6 of 6 Phase L batches.** Program totals now: 973 canon screens + 74 channel artifacts + 21 documents + 18 collateral + 25 website pages + brand & systems: 30 artifacts (5 brand-book chapters + 5 icon/illustration chapters + 5 app-store/social/motion/sonic pages + 5 font-pack/RTL/Nastaliq/rollout pages + 5 country-variant/multi-currency pages + 4 dark/senior/tablet/QA-automation pages) + 1 QA tool (`qa_gates.py`).
**SYS-1 COMPLETE, QA-PASSED (packaged) — 0 P0, 1 P1 fixed (two-builder seam: missing `.compiled` chip class + its own false self-test count) + 2 P2 (prefers-contrast unaddressed, noted PROPOSED; historical run-command reproducibility note), founder ⬜ pending.**

**PHASE L (BRAND & SYSTEMS) COMPLETE — 30 artifacts, 6 of 6 batches (BRAND-1/2/3, LOC-1/2, SYS-1).** **Laws authored across the phase (6 total, one per batch)**: THE BRAND LAW (BRAND-1) · THE ICON LAW (BRAND-2) · THE LAUNCH-ASSET LAW (BRAND-3) · THE FONT-PACK LAW (LOC-1) · THE COUNTRY-PACK LAW (LOC-2) · THE VARIANT LAW (SYS-1, also carried verbatim into `qa_gates.py`'s own docstring — the program's first law reproduced inside runnable code, not just HTML). Founder visual approval ⬜ pending across all 6 Phase L batches (BRAND-000…028, 30 files) plus `qa_gates.py`. **PROGRAM TOTALS AT PHASE L CLOSE: 973 canon screens + 74 channel artifacts + 21 documents + 18 collateral pieces + 25 website pages + 30 brand & systems artifacts + 1 QA tool (`qa_gates.py`).** Per `Design_Program/08_DP2_BATCH_PLAN_AND_PROMPTS.md`, Phase L is the last phase listed in the batch plan (§"PHASE L — BRAND & SYSTEMS (W13–W17, 6 batches)" is the final entry before the plan's own TOTAL/STATUS-LEGEND close) — **DP2 QUEUE COMPLETE: all phases done; next = founder visual approvals + the §17 decision queue.**

## LOGO-1 — Mark ratification package — brand/ (LOGO PROGRAM OPENS, batch 1 of 6 — founder-decision batch, pipeline v4)
| ID | Page | Key finding | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| BRAND-029 | mark-ratification-package.html — current mark evidence: all-size ladder (16px favicon → van-livery mm), construction anatomy, provenance note (pipeline-built 2026-05-30, never founder-ratified), canon-usage census, Exhibit E founder decision form | Spine page; **QA byte-diffed all 11 real-mark renderings against the source SVGs — clean match** | Census 10Y/3N (10 surfaces carry the mark faithfully or near-faithfully, 3 do not) independently re-verified to tie exactly to the REPLACE-cascade "10 of 13" arithmetic used later on the page. Decision-neutrality preserved: one design-team recommendation is offered, explicitly labeled "design-team recommendation — the decision is the founder's," never presented as the answer. |
| BRAND-030 | mark-refinement-candidates.html — 3 drawn PROPOSED directions (A "Sharpen," B "Simplify," C "Open") + REPLACE-path consequences, honestly framed as a commissioned project | **QA independently re-derived Direction B's merged bezier control points — mathematically verified, not just eyeballed** | Every candidate modification is itemized against the master geometry (which paths merge, which stroke weights change, which sizes are affected) so the founder can see exactly what each direction alters. REPLACE is explicitly NOT drawn — framed as a naming/trademark/cultural-review commission, not a design-team deliverable dressed up as one. |

**THE LOGO LAW (PROPOSED FOR RATIFICATION alongside this batch, reproduced verbatim ×2 rails)**: *"The mark is the one promise every surface makes before a single word is read. It is ratified by the founder or it is a placeholder — and a placeholder multiplied across a thousand screens is still a placeholder. One mark, drawn once, exported for every surface, with a rule for every context — including the tenant who brings their own."*

**QA verdict (LOGO-1): clean PASS, 0 P0/P1, 2 cosmetic P2s (noted, not fixed) — the 3rd clean PASS in program history, zero rework.** Geometry byte-diffed: all 11 real-mark renderings across both files byte-match their source SVGs. All candidate modifications on BRAND-030 are itemized and mathematically verified — QA independently re-derived Direction B's merged bezier control points rather than trusting the page's own math. The census's 10Y/3N split matches the REPLACE-cascade "10 of 13" figure exactly. Decision neutrality intact: exactly one design-team recommendation appears, correctly labeled as such, with the founder's decision left genuinely open.

**MAJOR NEW FINDING — bigger than 10_LOGO_PROGRAM.md's original five findings**: THREE distinct wheat-glyph geometries live in canon today, not one. (1) **The master**: 80×80 viewBox, stroke-width 3.5, 7 paths. (2) **"Look-alike A"**: 24×24, stroke-width 2.5 — appears in the handbook Ch.04, BRAND-001, BRAND-010's own misuse gallery, and SITE-016. (3) **"Look-alike B"**: 24×24, stroke-width 2, 5 sub-paths — appears in COL-014 and the W380 kiosk frame, byte-identical to each other but not to either the master or look-alike A. Only BRAND-010's construction diagram and BRAND-012's avatar are byte-true to the master. Add the live site's CSS-rendered "K" (no SVG at all) and **the "one mark" exists in FOUR renditions across the canon today.**

**Spec-correction wins (builder measured the real SVGs rather than trusting the spec)**: the lockup stroke-width is **2.8, not 3.5** as `spec_logo1.md` claimed — the spec was wrong, the builder corrected it against the source file. `#f9c45a` maps to **`--color-accent-300`, not `-500`** — the first time this hex has been correctly tied to its token. "Embroidery" does not exist anywhere in canon — **COL-016 is a 2-spot screen-print**, not embroidery, and the builder struck the word rather than repeating an inherited error. COL-016 itself carries **no drawn mark at all** — only dashed placeholder boxes. DOC-003's thermal (80mm) receipt renders **no logo whatsoever**.

**THE DECISION NOW SITS WITH THE FOUNDER**: **RATIFY AS-IS** / **REFINE** — Direction A "Sharpen" (stroke-weight optics fix + a dedicated 5-path cut for small sizes), Direction B "Simplify" (7→3 merged paths, tuned for print and small-size legibility), or Direction C "Open" (a container-free glyph) / **REPLACE** — the full cascade: 10 of 13 census surfaces would need to re-issue; this path requires naming, trademark, and cultural review, is **not drawn** in this batch, and is honestly framed as a commissioned project rather than a design-team deliverable. Alongside the mark decision, the founder is asked to **ratify THE LOGO LAW** itself. The three-geometry consolidation (master vs. look-alike A vs. look-alike B vs. the live-site "K") becomes **LOGO-2/LOGO-3 work once the mark is ratified**, not attempted in this batch.

**LOGO-1 COMPLETE, QA-PASSED (packaged) — clean PASS, 0 P0/P1, 2 cosmetic P2s (noted, not fixed), founder decision ⬜ PENDING — THE decision batch.** **LOGO PROGRAM OPENS: 1 of 6 batches (BRAND-029/030; queue LOGO-2…6 per `Design_Program/10_LOGO_PROGRAM.md`, all blocked on this founder decision).** Program totals: 973 canon screens + 74 channel artifacts + 21 documents + 18 collateral + 25 website pages + brand & systems: 32 artifacts (30 Phase L artifacts + 2 LOGO-1 pages) + 1 QA tool (`qa_gates.py`).

Favicon + manifest now cover all 20/20 live pages, and `site.webmanifest` validates. SITE-016's media-kit download slots — 4 literal `{{zip}}` placeholders, empty since SITE-4 — are filled, with disclosures flipped to match the new truth (mono variants still honestly annotated as a LOGO-3 placeholder, not asserted early).

BRAND-031 itself QA-called "the cleanest file in the entire brand/ directory" produced so far.

**Judgment calls accepted, not silently overridden**: BRAND-029's `.k-box` gradient exhibit (`#134628` → the correct live-site value `#0a3d1f`, re-read from source rather than left stale) was corrected in-place; THE LOGO LAW is shown RATIFIED without the N-of-M occurrence badge used on earlier laws — the badge series closed at BRAND-030 by design, with the reasoning written into the file rather than left implicit.

**LOGO-2 COMPLETE, QA-PASSED (packaged) — clean PASS, 0 P0/P1, 2 cosmetic P2s (noted, not fixed) — the 4th zero-rework batch in program history, and the 2nd consecutive clean PASS in the Logo Program.** Backend-pending: none — every asset shipped this batch is real. **NEXT: LOGO-3 — logo variant set (mono/reversed/single-ink variants + verified geometry re-derived from real coordinates) + look-alike retirement across the handbook/BRAND-001/BRAND-010's misuse gallery/BRAND-012/SITE-016 demos/COL-014/W380/COL-016, per `Design_Program/10_LOGO_PROGRAM.md`.** Rider still open: `{{vendor proof}}` for the <24px cut on thermal/screen-print substrates. The full repo-wide grep (443 files) decomposes exactly: 431 out-of-scope + 11 in-scope-and-retired (COL-014 + W380–W389) + 1 historical record correctly left alone (BRAND-029). 3 sampled out-of-scope files independently confirmed genuinely different in-context uses of the same icon shape, not false positives. Correctly escalated (BRAND-032 Section 4) rather than silently folded into a "surgical 8-location" batch — recommends a dedicated scripted mass-retirement batch, name proposed **LOGO-3.1**, with scripted replacement + a full qa_gates sweep. **THIS NEEDS A FOUNDER GO.**
