**1. Counts.** 8 dirs × 15 files = 120 confirmed (`ls` count each dir); banners/provenance-comments/`lang` attrs/font `<link>` all grep-recounted 15/15 per dir (all 8 dirs, all 4 checks — zero misses).

**2. Language samples (16 files read across all 8 languages, 2 per language minimum, full-file reads plus targeted grep sweeps of every 52-farmer-mandi-prices.html across all 8 dirs for the crop-variety check below).** All 8 read as genuinely their own language, not Hindi-structured or script-swapped: Assamese verified distinct from Bengali (own genitive `-ৰ`/`আপোনাৰ` vs Bengali `-র`/`আপনার`, own verb forms, the letter ৰ used correctly throughout, cross-checked against BRAND-015's own specimen sentence); Punjabi correctly in Gurmukhi (not Shahmukhi); each language's own copula/imperative grammar held in hero lines (Odia `-ନ୍ତୁ` polite imperative, Malayalam `-ുക` imperative, etc.). Agri register (mandi/quintal terms) correct and natural per language. Never-translate glossary (brand/IDs/enums) held. Numerals Western throughout, sampled. Money format (₹ + Indian grouping) intact everywhere sampled.

**3. Font truth.** Every dir's font-family var + Google Fonts `<link>` matches BRAND-015's pack assignment (table above) — confirmed by direct grep of each dir's font declaration. BRAND-015's 4 Baloo-family `{{candidate — verify availability}}` chips (pa/kn/ml/or rows) surgically updated in place to **"VERIFIED LIVE at APPLY-4/5 (fetch 200, dated 2026-07-21)"**, with the per-card rationale paragraphs, the claims table, the self-test table, and the top-of-file judgment-call comment all updated consistently; historical `{{candidate — verify availability}}` wording is left as an accurate record of what the file's original authoring session could confirm, explicitly reframed as "at the time" / "originally flagged" rather than a live claim. Tag balance re-verified clean after edit (94/94 div, 226/226 span).

**4. Audits — 2 genuine defects found and fixed, 1 tooling-bug investigation closed clean.**
- **DEFECT (fixed): missing `--font-body-{code}` custom-property definitions.** APPLY-3's own report flagged (but left unfixed, "out of scope") that `screens/lang-ta/*.html` referenced `var(--font-body-ta)` without ever defining it in a `:root` rule — a silent no-op meaning Tamil screens never actually rendered in Hind Madurai. Independent verification found the **identical bug present in all 15 `screens/lang-as/*.html` files too** (APPLY-3's own sibling batch, same root cause — the `:root{--font-body-{code}:...}` line was omitted from both language halves of that batch). Fixed across all 30 files (ta + as) by inserting the missing `:root` definition with a dated review-agent annotation comment; re-verified present in all 30 files, tag structure intact.
- **DEFECT (fixed): crop-variety-code never-translate violation.** TS-003 §c's never-translate glossary lists crop variety codes (Lokwan/Sharbati/Teja) alongside order/doc IDs — meaning Latin script, untranslated. APPLY-1's mr exemplar transliterated these into Devanagari instead, and every subsequent batch that "matched the mr exemplar's conventions exactly" (bn, te, as, ta, pa, kn — 6 languages) inherited the same deviation. Only APPLY-5 (ml/or) caught and self-corrected it, explicitly flagging the inconsistency with te in its own report ("Stricter than the te exemplar... TS-003 §c outranks exemplar") without going back to fix the earlier batches. This review fixed all 6 affected languages: 90 occurrences across 54 files (9 files/language × 6 languages) restored from native-script transliteration to the Latin "Lokwan"/"Sharbati"/"Teja" forms, matching TS-003 §c and the ml/or precedent exactly — ordinary crop nouns (Wheat/Maize/Chilli etc.) were left correctly translated, only the cultivar proper-noun suffix changed. Each touched file's provenance comment gained a one-line review-agent annotation explaining the fix. mr (APPLY-1, already independently QA-passed in a prior cycle) was left untouched — out of this batch's scope.
- **Tooling-bug investigation (no data corruption found, closed clean).** APPLY-4's report found and fixed a real bug in its own new expansion-audit scripts: the inherited strip order (style/script blocks stripped before HTML comments) could swallow real markup when a provenance comment's own prose contained the literal substring `<style`, silently zeroing extraction on 2 kn files (`03-otp.html`, `09-farmer-home.html`, both 0/187 pairs on first run). APPLY-5 independently found and fixed the identical bug in its own scripts. **Checked whether APPLY-2 (bn/te) and APPLY-3 (ta/as) — which never fixed this in their own scripts — silently suffered the same corruption:** confirmed the bug IS latently present in all 4 of those scripts (same buggy strip order), and confirmed by direct regex simulation that bn's `01-welcome.html` provenance comment does contain the collision string and does get its `<head>`/status-bar/banner markup incorrectly swallowed when parsed with the original strip order. However, re-running the full audit for **all 4 languages (bn, te, as, ta)** with both the original (buggy) and fixed (comments-first) strip order produced **byte-identical results in every case** — same `compared_pairs` (187/187 for all 4) and same flag counts (bn 0, te 1, as 0, ta 11) under both orders, because the swallowed markup in every affected file happened to fall outside the button/chip/title element classes the audit tracks. **Conclusion: apply2/apply3's published audit numbers are correct and unaffected by the latent tooling bug** — this was independently verified by direct simulation, not assumed from the absence of a reported symptom.
- **Recompute spot-checks (python-true, independent):** ta's 3-segment OTP claim (135 chars, UCS-2, `ceil(135/67)=3`) recomputed and confirmed exact; kn (101→2), pa (95→2), as (98→2) also recomputed and confirmed exact as a bonus.

**5. Mechanical.** `qa_gates.py --profile mobile` re-run on all 8 dirs: only `physical_props` fails, and it fails with **identical per-file hit-counts** to the English source screens (directly re-diffed side-by-side: `01-welcome.html` 2 hits both, `09-farmer-home.html` 2 hits both, `112-...-detail.html` 2 hits both, etc.) — confirmed pre-existing, source-inherited, byte-identical CSS never touched by any language batch, not a regression. Every other check (no_script, currency_glyphs, tag_balance, law_numbering, hex_audit, pii_patterns, file_count) PASS 15/15 all 8 dirs. Zero source modification: sha256 of 4 sampled source files (01-welcome, 09-farmer-home, 112-my-listing-detail, 529-senior-mode) matches apply5_report.md's own recorded baseline exactly, prefix-for-prefix. No shared-record races: `DP2_TRACKER.md`'s APPLY-2..5 STATE block sat unflipped (⬜ rows) exactly as the builders' reports claimed, `SCREEN-DATA-CATALOG.md` and `KRISHI_VERSE_MASTER_CONTEXT.md` carried no APPLY-2..5 content before this entry, and the portal `index.html` had zero APPLY mentions — confirmed no builder wrote to shared records.

**THE LANGUAGE WALL IS DOWN.** All 9 of contract §7's target languages (mr/bn/te/ta/as/pa/kn/ml/or) now have machine-draft renders of the same 15-screen farmer core journey. Native-review gates (TS-003 §d `reviewed_by`) remain the explicit, un-cleared gate before any of these 9 is LIVE rather than TARGET — this batch, like APPLY-1, produces strings ready for that review, not launch-ready copy.

## APPLY-6 — ar (Modern Standard Arabic), the first RTL screen set + BRAND-017's patches applied for real, pipeline v5

**Builder:** 1 implement-agent, following `Design_Program/14_LANGUAGE_RECIPE.md` + BRAND-017's ratified RTL implementation plan (gate-32b, G0-2 Q37). **Independent QA + records consolidation: this entry (2026-07-21, Review-Agent).**

**RTL IS REAL.** Krishi Verse's first right-to-left language ships as 15 recipe screens (`screens/lang-ar/`, `dir="rtl" lang="ar"`) plus a console proof (`web-screens/lang-ar/W128-tenant-listings-bulk.html`), and — for the first time — BRAND-017's long-PROPOSED `[dir="rtl"]` CSS patches are APPLIED for real into the shipping system canon (`system/screen.css`, `system/web/web-components.css`), not just demonstrated in a brand-book `<pre>` block. BRAND-017 itself was surgically updated (PROPOSED→APPLIED chips, gate-32b marked RATIFIED per G0-2 Q37, two self-test rows annotated "Superseded at APPLY-6") — verified present and accurate.

**1. CSS patches (system scope, highest risk).** Read `system/screen.css` + `system/web/web-components.css` + `system/web/web-frame.css` in full: every new/edited rule is scoped `[dir="rtl"]` (the `.icon-mirrors` utility's unscoped line is the documented no-op default `transform:none`, flipping only under `[dir="rtl"]`) — zero unscoped physical-property additions confirmed by direct read, matching BRAND-017's own patch-block text verbatim (diffed against the `<pre class="proof-pre">` source in `brand/BRAND-017-rtl-implementation.html`). `web-frame.css` line 195 confirmed correctly annotated PRE-EXISTING (not a new APPLY-6 rule). BRAND-017's PROPOSED→APPLIED flips (patch-block heading, gate-32b heading, 2 "Superseded at APPLY-6" self-test rows) all verified present and worded accurately. **LTR-unchanged re-proof (independently re-run, not just trusted):** `qa_gates.py --checks tag_balance,no_script,pii_patterns,physical_props` on the full English `screens/` (546 top-level files): 347 fail-lines / 345 unique files — matches the report's claimed 346/546 baseline (off by one file, immaterial, same pre-existing physical-CSS/aadhaar-false-positive condition, zero new failures introduced). Full `web-screens/` (439 files): exactly 4 fail (W062/W063/W064/W065, aadhaar-shaped-number false positives) — byte-exact match to the report's claim. Sha/mtime spot-check confirmed: `screens/01-welcome.html` (Jul 8, untouched), `system/tokens.css` (May 31, untouched), `screens/lang-mr/01-welcome.html` (Jul 21 18:39, predates this session's 22:31–22:54 edit window) — all consistent with the claimed zero-collateral-damage scope.

**2. The ar set — recounted, not just trusted.** All 15 files carry `dir="rtl" lang="ar"`, `.md-banner-ar` (15/15, 1 actual element each), and `.md-chip` per-block watermarks — independently recounted at the actual-element level (excluding CSS-rule/comment string hits): **75 total**, per-file breakdown byte-identical to the report's table (3/5/6/6/6/4/6/4/5/6/6/5/4/6/3). Bidi isolation (`.bidi{direction:ltr;unicode-bidi:isolate}`) recounted the same way: **90 total**, per-file breakdown also byte-identical to the report. Sampled 4 bidi blocks (money/UTR/timestamp cells in 59-farmer-payout-history and 12-my-listings): CSS rule present and correct, content inside genuinely LTR-run (₹ amounts, UTR digit strings, dates) — not a mislabeled Arabic span. Arabic-Indic digit scan (`٠-٩`, U+0660–0669) across all 15 mobile files + W128: **zero occurrences** — Western numerals confirmed (Q51). Never-translate glossary held (Krishi-Verse/KrishiMitra, crop-variety codes Lokwan/Sharbati/Teja/Jeera/Bold kept Latin, dispute/order IDs, UTR digits). Logo mark (01-welcome, the only file carrying one) confirmed never-mirrored — no class on the wheat-glyph `<svg>`, explicit "NEVER MIRRORS" comment retained. Back-arrow/chevron icons across all 15 files correctly carry `.icon-mirrors`. **Arabic language-quality sample (4 files: 01-welcome, 52-farmer-mandi-prices, 440-farmer-dispute-respond, 530-farmer-krishimitra-home):** natural, professional MSA farmer/dispute register throughout — correct imperative verb forms, correct agreement, correct agri vocabulary (قنطار/مندي used with an explicit `{{unit-term review}}` flag on the Gulf ar-AE ambiguity, exactly as claimed — not silently normalized), no gibberish, glossary-Latin variety names correctly preserved inline with translated produce nouns (e.g. "كمون — Jeera", "قمح — Lokwan"). No further `{{unit-term review}}` flags needed.

**3. Console proof (W128) — 1 defect found + fixed (documentation-only).** Confirmed: no local `[dir="rtl"]` CSS added in the file (only a provenance-comment mention of the pre-existing/newly-applied system rules); sidebar/table/breadcrumb mirroring genuinely consumed from system CSS, zero per-file direction hacks. `.md-chip` recount: 5 actual elements (matches). Bidi recount: 11 actual elements (matches). **`.md-banner-ar` recount: 1 actual element** — the report's own text said "3 `.md-banner-ar` markers", which was the raw string-occurrence count (comment + CSS rule + 1 real element), not the element count; the underlying build is correct (1 banner, as intended), only the report's phrasing was imprecise — corrected in `apply6_report.md`. **`.icon-mirrors`: 0 actual elements, not 1 as originally claimed** — grepped the file directly; the only occurrence of the string `icon-mirrors` is inside the provenance comment, no element in the markup carries `class="icon-mirrors"`. Read the full file to confirm this isn't a missed-mirror defect: W128 has no back-arrow or disclosure-chevron element anywhere in its markup (sidebar nav icons are non-directional category glyphs; the breadcrumb separator is a pure CSS `::before{content:"/"}`, not an icon) — there was genuinely nothing on this screen needing the class, so the console-CSS-mirrors proof itself is unaffected. **Fixed:** `apply6_report.md` §4 corrected to state the true counts (1 banner element / 0 icon-mirrors elements, with the reasoning above) instead of the inaccurate "3 markers / 1 icon" claim.

**4. Audits — independently re-run, not just read.** `apply6_ar_expansion_audit.py` re-executed fresh: **187/187 pairs**, first-run 44 → after regex fix 10 → final 5 flags — byte-identical to the report's claimed numbers. `apply6_ar_template_length_sample.py` re-executed fresh: EM-002 subject 31/50, preheader 59/90; SMS-001 OTP ar 93 chars/UCS-2/2 segments vs en 84 chars/GSM-7/1 segment; PU-001 title 18/40, body 90/120 — all match exactly.

**5. Mechanical.** `qa_gates.py --profile mobile --checks tag_balance,no_script,pii_patterns --recursive` on `screens/lang-ar/`: **PASS 15/15** all three checks. Same checks on `web-screens/lang-ar/`: **PASS 1/1** (W128). No shared-record races: `DP2_TRACKER.md`'s APPLY-6 STATE block sat unflipped (⬜ rows) exactly as expected before this entry; `SCREEN-DATA-CATALOG.md` and `KRISHI_VERSE_MASTER_CONTEXT.md` carried no APPLY-6 content before this entry; the portal `index.html` had zero `lang-ar`/W128 mentions — confirmed no builder wrote to shared records ahead of this QA pass.

**INDEPENDENT QA VERDICT (2026-07-21, Review-Agent — APPLY-6): CONDITIONAL PASS → fixed → PASS.** All structural claims (CSS-patch scoping, banner/chip/bidi counts, qa_gates PASS, LTR-unchanged proof, audit numbers) independently re-verified byte-exact or immaterially close. **2 P2 documentation-only defects found + fixed**, both confined to `apply6_report.md`'s §4 (W128 console-proof tallies): (a) the "1 `.icon-mirrors` icon" claim was false — 0 elements actually carry the class in that file, because it has no directional icon to mirror; (b) the "3 `.md-banner-ar` markers" phrasing conflated a raw string-count with the actual element count (1 banner, correctly built). Neither defect touched any shipped screen file — both are corrected in the report text only. Zero P0/P1 found. **RTL IS REAL — the system mirrors** (proven by an independently re-run LTR-regression sweep across the full 546+439-file English canon, zero new failures); an ar-AE candidate screen set is built and machine-draft-watermarked end-to-end; GCC Y6-7 is unblocked at the design layer. ar itself needs its own font-pack ratification (Noto Naskh Arabic remains an explicit DEMO face) and native-speaker review (TS-003 §d `reviewed_by` still empty) before any launch consideration — this batch, like every APPLY batch before it, produces strings ready for that review, not launch-ready copy.

## APPLY-8 — senior-mode application: G0-2 Q48's 1.30x multiplier shipped for real via a shared `.senior-mode` class, screen 529 verified + prose/tap fixed, farmer top-10 + 529 verified senior-ready, prefers-contrast sibling built (G0-2 Q41), pipeline v6

**Builder:** single-pass build, spec + report written same session. **Independent QA + records consolidation: this entry (2026-07-22, Review-Agent).**

**THE Q48/Q41 MANDATE IS SATISFIED.** Founder order: "fix 529's 118%-vs-'+30-45%' discrepancy per ratified multiplier; apply senior variants to the farmer top-10 journey screens" plus the ratified Q41 sibling (prefers-contrast, "shares Senior-Mode machinery"). G0-2 Q48 (`12_G0-2_DECISION_REGISTER.md` line 23) had already fixed 529's own CSS 118%→130% at the G0-2 sitting itself — this batch's job was to VERIFY that (not redo it), fix 529's own stale prose that still called the multiplier an unresolved "JUDGMENT CALL", and PROMOTE the same ratified 1.30x value into a reusable, shared, opt-in mechanism (`system/screen.css`'s `.screen.senior-mode` class) so it could actually reach the other 9 farmer top-10 screens without duplicating 10 more dedicated demo files.

**1. Screen 529 — independently re-verified, not re-trusted.** Read in full: line 28's `.vsm-screen{font-size:130% /* RATIFIED at G0-2 Q48... */}` confirmed already correct pre-batch, exactly as both the decision register and the build claim. Grepped the whole file: **zero** remaining "JUDGMENT CALL" text (the stale header + inline-style-block comment were rewritten in place); the only surviving "118%" string is historical prose ("was 118% by CSS slip", inside the ratification comment itself) — not a live/rendered value, correctly not a remnant. `.screen-back` independently confirmed at `width:56px;height:56px` with a dated `FIXED at APPLY-8` annotation (was 52px, below BRAND-026's ratified 56px-for-ALL-controls floor). Div-tag-balance recounted by hand (29/29) and independently re-confirmed via `qa_gates.py --profile mobile --checks tag_balance` (PASS). Demo obeys its own mechanism: 529 keeps its own already-correct local 130% rule (disclosed, not silently switched to the shared class, since it was already right — "verify don't redo").

**2. `system/screen.css` mechanism — read in full, math independently recomputed.** The `.screen.senior-mode` section is additions-only (appended after the pre-existing RTL section, nothing above it edited) — brace-balance independently recounted at 30/30, matching pre-batch state, no truncation. **All 8 type-scale redefinitions recomputed by hand from `tokens.css`'s own `:root` rem values, not just trusted from the inline comments:** 0.75rem×1.30=0.975rem=15.6px, 0.875×1.30=1.1375rem=18.2px, 1×1.30=1.30rem=20.8px, 1.125×1.30=1.4625rem=23.4px, 1.25×1.30=1.625rem=26px, 1.5×1.30=1.95rem=31.2px, 1.875×1.30=2.4375rem=39px, 2.25×1.30=2.925rem=46.8px — **every figure exact**, 1.30 really is 1.30, not a rounded-off label. Scoping independently confirmed correct: `.screen.senior-mode` requires BOTH classes present (zero effect on any `.screen` without the `.senior-mode` class), and the shared `@media (prefers-contrast: more)` block independently re-read: reuses the existing `--border-strong` token (no new hex invented) and adds a redundant solid focus outline — correctly framed in both `screen.css`'s own comment and BRAND-026's new doctrine paragraph as BUILT/conservative, not founder-ratified, since "no ratified BRAND value existed to compile from" (Q41's own text is quoted accurately, not overclaimed as settled).

**3. Farmer top-10 + 529 — sampled 4 files deep (529, 09-farmer-home, 521-farmer-voice-listing-speak, 72-kyc-aadhaar-start read in full), remaining 6 cross-checked structurally.** Every literal hardcoded-`font-size:Npx`/tap-target-px hit surviving in all 9 companion-bearing files has a matching `.screen.senior-mode` companion rule (grep cross-check across all 9: 09=6, 52=3, 521=4, 19=1, 54=2, 56=3, 440=3, 530=4, 61=2 senior-mode-scoped rules, exactly matching each file's own literal-px hit count) — zero orphaned breakers. Tokens cited in every companion (`--tap-large`, `--border-strong`, `--shadow-focus`, all 8 `--text-*`) independently byte-verified against `tokens.css` at the exact cited line numbers (95-97, 119-126, 185, 220-222). `72-kyc-aadhaar-start.html`'s "CLEAN, zero breakers" claim independently confirmed by direct read (all text uses `var(--text-*)`/rem, zero hardcoded px; `.screen-back` uses the shared class's default, no per-file companion needed). All 11 touched files independently pass `qa_gates.py --profile mobile --checks tag_balance` (11/11 PASS, re-run directly, not the builder's own summary trusted). `SENIOR-READY — verified APPLY-8` grep-counted across ALL 546 `screens/*.html` (lang-*/ excluded, subject-grepped per HARD RULE 1): **exactly 1 occurrence in exactly the 10 non-529 top-10 files, 0 anywhere else in the 546-file population** — matches the claim exactly, no duplication risk, no stray copies. The 2 genuine `aria-label` fixes (09's bell, 19's back button) independently confirmed present and correctly scoped as the ALREADY-RATIFIED contract §8 rule, not an over-claim of the still-PROPOSED "always-visible labels" extension.

**4. Top-10 pick honesty — challenged, held up.** Cross-checked against APPLY-1's own prior 15-pick farmer-core-journey list (`spec_apply1.md`): 7 of APPLY-8's 10 picks (09, 52, 521, 19, 56, 440, 530) are the identical file APPLY-1 already picked for the same category, and the 3 fresh picks (54-weather, 72-kyc, 61-profile) each carry a disclosed, defensible rationale (weather wasn't in APPLY-1's 15; KYC's Aadhaar-entry screen chosen over the plainer welcome/OTP screens as "the harder, more accessibility-critical half of onboarding"; profile chosen over help as the more frequently-visited pairing member). No more-core farmer-facing category or file was found missing from the founder's own 10 named categories during an independent `screens/*.html` naming sweep. Judgment calls throughout are flagged in-file, not silently made.

**5. BRAND-026 — read in full.** Status chips independently recounted: exactly 2 rows carry `APPLIED — APPLY-8` (type-scale multiplier, tap targets), exactly 2 rows carry `PROPOSED` (contrast AAA-aspiration, always-visible-labels extension), 1 row stays its pre-existing unrelated "Compiled from 529" status (simplified nav, untouched this batch, correctly not flipped) — matches the "exactly 2 APPLIED, others honestly PROPOSED" claim precisely. The new doctrine paragraph documenting the prefers-contrast build and the 2 aria-label fixes independently re-read: frames both correctly (prefers-contrast as built-conservative not ratified; aria-label fixes as the narrower already-law rule, not the broader PROPOSED extension). Tag balance independently recounted (div/table/tr/td/th/tbody/thead/span/p/ol/li) — all balanced.

**6. Census — independently re-derived by full population reconstruction, not the builder's script re-run as proof.** Wrote an independent verification pass: copied the full 546-file `screens/` population to scratch, manually reverted this batch's own 11 disclosed per-file content changes (529's tap-target value, 09/19's aria-label additions, 09/440/530's 10 inline-to-class tokenizations) to reconstruct the exact pre-batch state, then ran `apply8_census.py` fresh against that reconstruction. **4 of the 5 claimed figures reproduce EXACTLY**: 391 files-with-finding, 273 inline font-size, 27 tap-target px, 34 icon-only-no-label — all byte-exact. **1 figure does NOT reproduce**: style-block font-size independently recounts to **1,128**, not the claimed 1,123 — a confirmed, genuine +5 undercount in the original census report (not a timing artifact, since this batch's own `.senior-mode` companion rules use `calc()` syntax that doesn't match the census's own `font-size:Npx` pattern either way). **Flagged and fixed**, not fabricated: corrected in `apply8_report.md` with a dated note, and the corrected reconstruction (not the original mis-counted run) is what's packaged as `apply8_census.json`. Not blocking: the actual fix-scope (the 11 touched files) was independently verified complete and correct at the file level regardless of this population-wide figure's small (0.4%) imprecision.

**7. Regression proof — independently re-run, not trusted.** `qa_gates.py --profile mobile` re-run against the full current 546-file `screens/` population: OVERALL FAIL, but every failing check independently confirmed pre-existing/unrelated to this batch — `hex_audit` (1 file, `20-add-money.html`, untouched, exact match to claim), `pii_patterns` (2 files, `72-kyc-aadhaar-start.html`/`334-worker-pf-esi-enrollment.html`, both a pre-existing placeholder digit-string at a byte offset far from this batch's only edit to `72` — a header comment near the top; independently confirmed at line 82, deep in body content), `law_numbering` (1 cross-file "M of N" hole, unrelated, exact match to claim). `physical_props`, however, independently recounts to **344 of 546 files failing**, not the "~230" the build's own report claimed — a materially low approximation, corrected in `apply8_report.md` with a dated annotation. The batch's own conclusion ("zero NEW physical_props failures introduced by this batch") still independently holds: both `screen.css`'s two new sections and all 9 per-file `.senior-mode` companion blocks were re-grepped for `margin-left/right`, `padding-left/right`, `border-left/right`, `text-align:left/right` and show zero matches.

**INDEPENDENT QA VERDICT (2026-07-22, Review-Agent — APPLY-8): PASS.** Zero P0/P1. **2 P2 defects found + fixed, both documentation-only in `apply8_report.md`, no shipped CSS/HTML file affected:** (a) an internal arithmetic error in §3's totals sentence ("21 ... 17+10") that didn't sum and undercounted the report's own per-file table (corrected to 31 = 21 style-block + 10 inline); (b) a materially low physical_props baseline ("~230" vs. the independently-recounted 344/546) in §6's regression-proof section, corrected with the exact grep-verified count. **THE SENIOR MODE MECHANISM IS REAL, NOT DEMONSTRATED:** a single shared, scoped, opt-in `.screen.senior-mode` class now carries the founder-ratified 1.30x type-scale + 56px-universal-tap-floor to any farmer-facing screen that opts in — verified mathematically exact, verified additions-only (zero default-mode change, full regression sweep clean of new failures), and verified applied correctly and completely to a representative farmer top-10 + the platform's one dedicated senior-mode demo screen (529). The prefers-contrast sibling (G0-2 Q41) ships alongside it, correctly disclosed as a conservative, non-ratified floor rather than an invented standard. Next Phase M batches (tablet, country pack) inherit a working, verified precedent for "one shared mechanism + per-file readiness pass", not a re-invention risk.

## APPLY-7 — dark-mode application: G0-2 Q40's 3 self-surfaced AA failures corrected, dark scope shipped for real, 20 console screens verified dark-ready, pipeline v5

**Builder:** ran in two lives — the first build agent died mid-execution ("Byte deltas look sane. Let's execute:" its last words); a relaunch agent performed byte-by-byte forensics before any new edit, found the dead agent's mass-edit script had already finished writing all 20 target files, and supplied only the two genuinely-missing files (`spec_apply7.md`, `apply7_report.md`). **Independent QA + records consolidation: this entry (2026-07-22, Review-Agent).**

**THE Q40 CONTINGENCY IS SATISFIED.** G0-2 Q40 (`Design_Program/12_G0-2_DECISION_REGISTER.md` line 15): "RATIFIED, contingent: BRAND-025's set becomes dark canon AFTER its 3 self-surfaced AA failures are corrected (primary-600/danger/info on dark). APPLY-7 executes correction + application." This batch's own wider recompute found 10 real AA failures total (the 3 named + 7 more), all corrected — BRAND-025's dark values are no longer PROPOSED-only chrome, they are ratified-and-shipped canon.

**1. AA math — independently recomputed from scratch, not the builder's own script re-run.** Wrote a fresh WCAG 2.1 relative-luminance script (`contrast=(L1+0.05)/(L2+0.05)`, sRGB channel linearization, independent of `apply7_aa_check.py`) and recomputed every corrected pair: **all 16 AFTER/corrected pairs independently reproduced PASS at the exact claimed ratio** — primary-interactive-dark 6.02:1 (card) / 6.83:1 (page, large-text), danger-text-dark 5.32:1 (card) / 6.03:1 (page) / 5.36:1 (own badge bg), info-text-dark 6.81:1 (card) / 7.72:1 (page) / 6.41:1 (own badge bg), success-text-dark 8.36:1 (card) / 7.53:1 (own badge bg), warning-text-dark 8.36:1 (card) / 7.27:1 (own badge bg), ai-text-dark 5.22:1 (card) / 5.38:1 (own badge bg), primary-avatar-dark 5.03:1 (on primary-100-dark), focus-ring 6.02:1 (UI threshold). **All 13 BEFORE/original-failing pairs independently reproduced FAIL** at the exact claimed ratio (primary-600-as-text 2.57:1, danger-as-text 2.92:1, info-as-text 3.13:1, danger-dark-as-text 2.04:1, success-dark 3.03/3.37:1, warning-dark 3.14/3.61:1, danger-dark-badge 2.05:1, info-dark-badge 2.03:1, ai-dark 1.35/1.39:1, primary-700-avatar 1.28:1). The one diagnostic row (primary-400 alone tested against primary-100-dark) independently recomputed at exactly **4.00:1** — correctly below the 4.5 text threshold, confirming primary-300 (not primary-400) was the right choice for the avatar-initials role. **Three-way byte match confirmed:** the corrected hexes in `system/web/web-tokens.css`, BRAND-025's dark-token-table swatches/"was X → now Y" trails, and `apply7_aa_report.json` all agree exactly, hex-for-hex, across all 8 corrected/new tokens. The two disclosed out-of-scope hairline-border findings (`--border-default`/`--border-subtle` vs. their dark surfaces, 1.39–1.49:1) were independently recomputed and confirmed genuine — and independently confirmed to measure the near-identical 1.10–1.62:1 in LIGHT mode too (recomputed both `body.web` light values and the `earth-*` scale directly) — correctly a pre-existing, theme-independent characteristic, not a dark-mode regression, and correctly left as flagged known debt rather than silently fixed (a real fix touches border contrast canon-wide, outside Q40's charter) or silently dropped.

**2. Dark scope — read in full.** `[data-theme="dark"]` in `web-tokens.css`: surfaces/ink/border/table remaps carried forward unchanged, plus the 6 corrected/new tokens (`--color-success-text-dark`, `-warning-text-dark`, `-danger-text-dark`, `-info-text-dark`, `-ai-text-dark`, `-primary-avatar-dark`) and the `--chart-1` remap to primary-400. New `body.web[data-theme="dark"]` reconciliation block (specificity 0-2-1) verified to sit correctly between the base dark stub and the `body.web{}` light rule — confirmed by direct read that `body.web{}`'s own light-mode block (lines 250–284) is untouched, byte-for-byte, and every new dark rule lives strictly inside `[data-theme="dark"]`/`body.web[data-theme="dark"]`/the pre-existing `@media(prefers-color-scheme:dark){:root.dark-enabled{...}}` opt-in — resolving the long-logged W15 KNOWN ISSUE (light-mode `body.web` rules had always out-specificity'd the bare dark attribute selector, so no console page had ever actually rendered dark before this batch). Brace balance independently recounted: `web-tokens.css` 8/8, `web-components.css` 277/277 — both balanced, matching the report's claim exactly. `web-components.css`'s "DARK-MODE TEXT-ROLE OVERRIDES" section (~25 `[data-theme="dark"] .kvw-*{color:...}` rules) read in full: every rule retargets only a `color`/`border-color` declaration on a component that reuses a base semantic color directly as text; button/chip/badge backgrounds paired with `--color-text-inverse` are correctly left untouched (theme-independent by construction, confirmed via the independent AA recompute's "sanity-unaffected" rows). `--role-text-*` indirection tokens (for the per-file inline-`style` cases CSS alone can't override) confirmed defined in BOTH the `:root` (light, resolves to the pre-existing base token — zero visual change) and `[data-theme="dark"]` (resolves to the corrected dark-safe token) blocks.

**3. BRAND-025 — read in full.** All 19 dark-token-table rows carry an `APPLIED — APPLY-7` chip (independently recounted: 20 raw string matches, but 1 is the summary paragraph's own re-mention of the class — the table itself has exactly 19 rows, matching the self-test claim); 8 rows carry a "was X → now Y, ratio a.bc:1" trail, matching the AA report exactly. Contrast table independently recounted: 20 PASS rows + 12 FAIL rows = 32 total — byte-exact to the page's own self-test claim ("32 pairs shown on this page"). The `.dc-badge-live`/`.dc-badge-pending` rendered-demo hexes (`#6fcf97`/`#f0b055`) confirmed to match the newly-formalized `--color-success-text-dark`/`--color-warning-text-dark` tokens exactly — the demo and its own contrast table agree for the first time, as claimed. Two mis-tested prose paragraphs (the ones that originally called success/warning "reusable as-is") confirmed corrected in place with a `CORRECTED AT APPLY-7:` prefix — original wrong claim left visible, not silently deleted, per this canon's established self-correcting-in-place convention. Tag balance independently re-run via `qa_gates.py --profile brand --checks tag_balance` on `brand/BRAND-025-dark-mode-console.html`: **PASS, balanced.**

**4. The 20 screens — two-lives risk, checked directly.** `grep -c "DARK-READY"` independently re-run across ALL 439 `web-screens/` files: **exactly 1 for each of the 20 target files, 0 for all other 419** — no duplicate insertion from the two-lives build, confirmed clean. 5 of the 20 sampled directly (W128-tenant-listings-bulk, W193-tenant-mandi-pulse, W200-tenant-auditor, W325-gov-dashboard, W436-tenant-auditor-ledger): every annotated tokenization fix is real and present (`--role-text-danger`/`-success`/`-warning`/`-info` inline styles physically in the markup, matching each file's own DARK-READY note's claimed occurrence count), every referenced var confirmed defined in both `:root` and `[data-theme="dark"]` in `web-tokens.css`, and a targeted grep for any surviving raw `color:var(--color-success-dark|warning-dark|danger|info-dark)` inline style in these 5 files returned **zero** — the fix is complete, not partial. Realm spread independently recounted from each file's own route-comment `route:` path: admin 4 (W001/W002/W008/W017), tenant **5** incl. 2 auditor (W113/W128/W193 + W200/W436 auditor), partner 1 (W205), store 4 (W273/W284/W316/W317), gov 2 (W325/W337), ops-pos 2 (W360/W361), ops-kiosk 2 (W380/W384) — 20 total, honest and matching the per-file table. **1 P2 arithmetic defect found + fixed:** both `apply7_report.md` §5 and `spec_apply7.md` §3's realm-spread summary sentence read "tenant (4, incl. 2 auditor)", which under-sums the stated breakdown to 19 rather than 20 — the per-file table itself was always correct (5 tenant-realm files); only the one summary sentence's count was wrong. Fixed in both files with a dated correction note; no CSS/HTML file was affected, arithmetic-only.

**5. Census + regression — independently re-derived, not just read.** Wrote a fresh hex-scanning script (own regex, own gradient-context exclusion, independent of `apply7_hex_census.py`) over all 439 `web-screens/*.html` files, scoped to CSS contexts (`<style>` blocks + `style=""` attributes) only: **0 non-gradient hardcoded hex** (checked against the full known-token hex set parsed from `tokens.css` + `web-tokens.css`), **138 gradient-exempt occurrences across 19 distinct decorative hex values** — byte-exact match to `apply7_hex_census.json`'s claim on every number. `qa_gates.py --profile console --checks tag_balance,hex_audit` independently re-run on the full 439-file population: **878/878 individual check-passes (439 files × 2 checks), 0 fails, OVERALL PASS** — reproduces the builder's claimed "439/439 both checks, 0 fail" exactly, not just trusted.

**INDEPENDENT QA VERDICT (2026-07-22, Review-Agent — APPLY-7): PASS.** Zero P0/P1. **1 P2 fixed** (the realm-spread arithmetic slip above, documentation-only, no shipped file affected). AA math, three-way hex match, tag/brace balance, hex census, and the full console regression sweep were all independently reproduced from first principles rather than trusted from the builder's own tooling — every number matched exactly. **THE DARK SCOPE IS REAL, NOT DEMONSTRATED: Q40's contingency is satisfied**, the W15 KNOWN ISSUE (console pages never actually rendering dark) is resolved, and a 20-screen representative console sample spanning 8 realms is verified dark-ready with zero light-mode regression. ## APPLY-9 — tablet farmer-app decision + application: G0-2 Q49 bound onto BRAND-027's own two candidates (Candidate B ratified/applied, Candidate A retained as fallback), `.screen.screen-split` + pointer:coarse mechanism shipped for real, 5 list+detail pairs (10 files) verified tablet-ready, pipeline v6

Neither value was invented. Every annotation in the new section correctly cites Q49 and/or BRAND-027 by name.

**3. The 10 files — all read in full, independently re-verified at the element level.** All 5 list files (`52-farmer-mandi-prices.html`, `56-farmer-orders.html`, `12-my-listings.html`, `517-shared-unified-notification-center.html`, `530-farmer-krishimitra-home.html`) carry `.screen-split` on the `.screen` root, their pre-existing list markup wrapped unchanged in `.screen-split-list`, and a new `.screen-split-detail` static preview pane (default `display:none`, revealed only inside the tablet+coarse media query) — mobile rendering is structurally byte-identical below 768px, confirmed by direct read of each file's CSS (no rule touches `.screen-split-list`/`.screen-split-detail` outside the two new media queries). **4. Census — independently reproduced, one methodology correction along the way.** A first-pass independent census (checking each file's raw text for `@media` rules ≥600px) initially found 5 false-positive hits (`12`/`517`/`52`/`530`/`56` — because their own new header-comment prose literally quotes the string `@media (min-width:768px) and (pointer:coarse)` as documentation) — corrected by stripping HTML comments before the live-CSS check, per HARD RULE 1's "grep the SUBJECT, not the label." ## APPLY-10 — country-pack pilot: UAE, the first country with BRAND-020's 9 WHAT-VARIES sockets filled end-to-end (COUNSEL-PENDING/VERIFY, never a bare statutory value), currency code-form mechanism shipped for real (AED live via a new token/component pair), repeatable recipe written (`16_COUNTRY_PACK_RECIPE.md`), PHASE M CLOSES, pipeline v6

**Builder:** single-pass build, spec + report written same session. **Independent QA + records consolidation: this entry (2026-07-22, Review-Agent).**

**Founder order:** "Country-pack pilot: fill ONE country's sockets end-to-end with counsel (recommend UAE — nearest target): localized landing + document variants + currency live. Repeatable recipe for the rest." Counsel is NOT available in this pipeline (G0-2 Q14, the 14-item counsel brief, ROUTED/unactioned) — the binding CRITICAL LAW this batch operates under: every statutory element ships as a structured COUNSEL-PENDING socket with a crisp counsel question inline, never an invented value (LOC-2's rate-by-negation doctrine, HARD RULE 4 — a disclaimed rate is still a rendered value). UAE was not a new market judgment: BRAND-020's own "4 variant pages indexed" table already tagged `BRAND-023-gcc-variant.html` "TARGET Y6-7 — nearest," so the founder's own recommendation matched ratified canon exactly.

**What shipped:** `brand/BRAND-038-uae-country-pack.html` (all 9 BRAND-020 WHAT-VARIES sockets instantiated; AED format per BRAND-024; Hijri dual-calendar sample per Q50, flagged illustrative; ar-AE+en pairing reusing APPLY-6's CSS verbatim; 8-row UAE agri glossary starter per Q52; 7-emirate list stated plainly as stable geography), `site/SITE-028-country-uae-comingsoon.html` (honest pre-launch/waitlist page, LAUNCH GATE: NOT CLEARED, zero fake customers/GMV/live-in-UAE language), `documents/DOC-021-trade-invoice-uae.html` + `DOC-022-payment-receipt-uae.html` (AED trade invoice with VAT line structurally present but rate withheld + TRN sockets with no format claim + dual-date rows + bilingual EN+AR captions; the receipt carries zero VAT/tax fields structurally, per DOC-003's own receipt≠invoice law), `Design_Program/16_COUNTRY_PACK_RECIPE.md` (11-step repeatable playbook, counsel-question template, per-country-class effort table for GCC/EU/US/SAARC), and a real currency code-form mechanism in `system/web/web-tokens.css`/`web-components.css` (`--currency-code-display` token + `.affix-currency-code`/`.kvw-money-code` components, `[data-currency="AED"]` scope) — BRAND-024's AED row flipped `{{code-form preferred}}` → `APPLIED — APPLY-10`, SAR/LKR left honestly unwired.

**1. Statutory absence — independent subject-grep, own run, not the builder's pasted output.** Re-ran the VAT-%/TRN-digit patterns (`vat[^%]{0,40}[0-9]+\s*%`, `trn[^a-z]{0,30}[0-9]{2,}`) plus a broader `[0-9]{1,2}\s*%` sweep across all 4 new HTML files + the recipe: every `%` hit traced by hand to a CSS width or effort-estimate percentage (`width:60%`, "40-50% of this batch's own effort"), never VAT-adjacent. Zero Arabic-Indic `٪` glyph, zero "five percent"/"khamsa" variant anywhere. Every VAT/TRN occurrence across all 4 files is the literal `{{vat_rate_pct}}`/`{{seller_trn}}`/`{{buyer_trn}}` socket, each paired with a crisp, inline `{{counsel — <question>}}` — confirmed directly in BRAND-038's socket-checklist table, DOC-021's counsel-pending callout (2 distinct counsel questions plus per-line-item repeats), and DOC-022's structural absence. **Pass, independently confirmed — zero rate-by-negation violations.**

**2. SITE-028 claims law + system conventions.** Read in full: zero fake customers/GMV/"available in UAE" language anywhere; the "What's real today vs. target" table states each absence explicitly (not silently omitted); LAUNCH GATE chip correctly danger-toned "NOT CLEARED" (not the success-toned style SITE-022 uses for India). Nav + footer logo mark independently byte-diffed (Python string-extract, not eyeballed) against `SITE-022-country-india.html`'s own nav/footer `<svg>` blocks: **identical, byte-for-byte**, both occurrences — the ratified small-cut mark (5 paths, stroke-width 5, `#f9c45a`), not an invented or placeholder glyph. hreflang candidate rows (en-AE/ar-AE/x-default) extend SITE-020 §c's own matrix pattern, all correctly flagged TARGET, none routable. THE LOCALE LAW/TEMPLATE LAW/targets-razor quotes independently byte-checked against SITE-020/SITE-021/COL-017 — exact matches (see §7). **Pass.**

**3. Currency mechanism — read in full, not trusted from the report's prose.** `system/web/web-tokens.css`: brace count independently recounted at 9/9 (matching pre-batch, confirming additions-only); the new `[data-currency="AED"] { --currency-code-display: "AED"; }` block is a standalone scoped rule, touches no other token, so light/dark themes are structurally unaffected. `system/web/web-components.css`: brace count 279/279; the 2 new rules (`.affix-currency-code::before`, `.kvw-money-code::before`) both resolve `content:var(--currency-code-display)` — zero physical-property lines in either file's additions (independently grepped for `left:`/`right:`/`-left`/`-right`). BRAND-038's own worked demo: the `.kvw-money-code` span in the markup is independently confirmed **empty** (`<span class="kvw-money-code" style="..."></span>`) — the rendered "AED" text is CSS-supplied, not literal. Handoff-doc accuracy — every path/count/selector/citation independently re-derived, not trusted.** Extracted and tested every file/dir path named in `07-developer-handoff.md` (designer_pack/01-designer-handbook.html, designer_pack/tokens/, user-flows.html, component-library.html, system/web/web-component-library.html, screens/, web-screens/, all 7 `system/*.css` sources, designer_pack/assets/{icons,logo,illustrations}, screens/lang-ar, web-screens/lang-ar, all 7 templates/ subdirs, Design_Program/{DP2_TRACKER,14_LANGUAGE_RECIPE,16_COUNTRY_PACK_RECIPE,07_PACKAGES_UI_IMPLEMENTATION_SPEC}.md, Development_Program/DESIGN_DRIVEN_SCHEMA_BACKLOG.md, Database_Architecture/, krishi-verse/packages/{tokens,i18n}/, qa_gates.py, BRAND-029…038) — **zero misses, all exist.** Every count independently re-grepped from `Phase-1 all screen design/Krishi_Verse_Design_System/` and matches exactly: 546 mobile / 439 console / 10 lang dirs × 15 = 150 / 1 lang-ar console / 83 templates / 23 documents / 18 collateral / 29 site / 39 brand / 150 icon SVGs / 151 sprite symbols / 42 schema-backlog items (Tier 1=12, Tier 2=18, Tier 3=12) — every figure byte-exact to the doc's own claims. **2. Tokens export — re-run, idempotency, values, provenance, garbage-scan.** Re-ran `hand1_tokens_export.py` twice (dry-run + execute): byte-identical to the already-shipped output (`diff -rq` empty across all 7 files) — confirmed idempotent. Spot-verified 8 token values by hand against CSS sources: mobile `color-primary-600`(#1e6f3f)/`space-4`(1rem), web `web-control-h`(36px)/`web-control-h-lg`(44px), dark `surface-page`(#12171d)/`color-ink-700`(#e8ebee), senior `text-base` (`calc(1rem*1.30)`, resolved 20.8px, computed representation correct) — all exact. Provenance SHA-256s for all 3 CSS sources recomputed independently (`shasum -a 256`) — byte-identical to the export's own `provenance.sources` block. **1 real bug found + fixed (P1, garbage-token class):** `parse_senior_mode()`'s `var(--X)` resolution branch sliced `raw.strip()[4:-1]` (stripping only `"var("`), leaving a redundant leading `"--"` on the lookup key (`"--tap-large"` instead of `"tap-large"`); since `mobile_vars` keys are stored without the `--` prefix, the lookup always missed, silently falling back to `"?"` — producing a garbage `$resolvedPx:"?"` and a malformed double-dash `"----tap-large"` note for **both** `mobile.senior.tap-min` and `tap-rural`. Fixed (`[6:-1]`, stripping `"var(--"`); re-ran, verified both now resolve to `"56px"` (tokens.css's own `--tap-large`), matching the note's own "tap floor generalized to 56px" claim instead of contradicting it. Diff before/after confirmed the fix touched only those 2 note strings in `design-tokens.css`/`.json` (the only 2 files that render senior-mode notes) — nothing else changed. Post-fix self-tests all re-pass: JSON valid, XML valid, `node --check` clean on .js/tailwind.config.js, CSS brace-balanced (11/11), a full-export scan found zero remaining "?"/empty-key/comment-fragment garbage anywhere, `breakpoint` category confirmed absent from both scopes (the builder's own mid-batch comment-parse fix holds), `KVTokens.swift`'s digit-leading-identifier fix confirmed (only the documenting comment matches `public static let [0-9]`, zero real declarations).

Census — independently reproduced, not trusted.** `find` on `web-screens/` confirms **439 root files + 1 `lang-ar/` file = 440**, not the founder's loose "452" (Hard Rule 11 applied correctly — discrepancy recorded, not silently absorbed).
