Interactive-element sizing (32px tree-item/date-field, matching the file's own pre-existing 28–32px dense-console convention) is consistent with the rest of the file — the mobile 44px tap floor is correctly N/A here (web/console realm, confirmed by the build's own grep showing senior-mode/screen-split are mobile-only mechanisms).

**4. Guardian ruling — byte-checked + independently reasoned, not rubber-stamped.** Contract §6's Guardian-path text independently re-grepped against `KRISHI_VERSE_MASTER_WORK_CONTRACT.md` line 62 — **byte-exact** to both the build and prior-QA reports' quotes. **Independent reasoning on whether the 4 promoted ad-hoc classes needed a fresh Guardian approval: no.** Guardian gates a component's *first use*; for 3 of the 4 (`kvw-sidebar-foot`, `kvw-callout`, `kvw-wizard-shell`) first use already happened pre-HAND-2 as ad-hoc inline styles/bare classes in production screens — this batch only formalizes an already-deployed pattern into the shared stylesheet with zero visual change (verified: the new CSS default matches each file's existing inline declaration). `kvw-toolbar` is the one exception disclosed by the build itself (first real CSS, a genuine visual fix, not a mirror) — but it too formalizes an already-shipped, already-in-use bare div, not a new UI pattern being introduced for the first time. Only the 3 founder-named gaps (tree view, date-range, diff upgrade) are genuinely NEW interaction patterns — those are correctly Guardian-satisfied by the founder's own HAND-2 order naming and directing all 3.

**5. W128/W340 — diagnosed, confirmed pre-existing, reconciled against APPLY-7's baseline.** Independent `qa_gates.py --profile console --recursive --expect-files 440` run on `web-screens/` reproduced **3,521 PASS / 6 FAIL exactly**, both files matching the build's claim: `W128-tenant-listings-bulk.html` (`states_coverage`: missing empty/error/flagged_off) and `W340-gov-dbt-batch.html` (5 `law_numbering` FAIL rows on a worked-example table). Confirmed pre-existing two ways: mtime (`W128` 2026-07-22 06:09, hours before this session; `W340` 2026-07-21 12:44, previous day) and the byte log showing HAND-2 touched only 2 files, neither of which is W128/W340. **Reconciled against APPLY-7's "zero new failures" claim** (`apply7_report.md` §6): APPLY-7's sweep ran `--checks tag_balance,hex_audit` only — a narrower check-set that never exercised `law_numbering` or `states_coverage` — so the two "0 fail" and "6 fail" results are not in tension; they audited different check-sets, not the same one at different times. Both files' original build batches were W-D15 (W128) and W-D38 (W340), each independently QA-passed at the time under the check-set then in force. **Left as disclosed known debt, not fixed here**: different screens, unrelated content, out of HAND-2's component-library scope — a mass-fix would need its own dry-run/census per Hard Rule 9, not a scope-creep patch bundled into this batch.

**6. Console regression sweep — own invocation.** Same command as above; own numbers matched the build's exactly (3,521/6, same 2-file signature), both before and after this QA's own library-page edits (which touch only `web-component-library.html`, outside the swept `web-screens/` population).

**INDEPENDENT QA VERDICT (2026-07-22, Review-Agent — HAND-2, RELAUNCH #2 of the QA/Records agent): PASS.** Every claim across all 6 governing checks (census reproduction, library-page orphan/coverage script, new-CSS read-in-full, Guardian byte-check + independent reasoning, W128/W340 diagnosis + APPLY-7 reconciliation, own regression sweep) was independently re-derived — own `find`/`grep -rl` counts, own Python orphan-diff script, own brace recount, own contract byte-check, own `qa_gates.py` invocations, own mtime checks — never trusted from either the builder's or the first (now-dead) QA agent's tooling or prose. **The first QA relaunch's work was verified intact** (sha256 match) and its 8 fixes are real, not fabricated. **1 further small defect found + fixed this pass:** 3 tone-modifier classes created this batch (`kvw-callout-info/-danger/-success`) had zero demo; fixed with a dated annotation, re-verified 0 orphans/0 gaps/11-11 PASS. Zero P0/P1. **W128/W340 are correctly disclosed as pre-existing, out-of-scope debt**, not silently ignored nor wrongly blamed on this batch. **PHASE O remains OPEN.** Next: HAND-3 (qa_gates pipeline adoption) / HAND-4 (redline convention) — or the founder's own open list.

## HAND-3 — qa_gates.py v3 + standing pipeline adoption: favicon/meta_essentials/lang_length checks (additive-only, never auto-join any profile), SYS-1's own "PROPOSED adoption" flipped to ADOPTED, pipeline v6

**Builder:** single-pass build, spec + report same session (`Design_Program/specs_v3/spec_hand3.md` + `hand3_report.md`). Founder order was written against a stale queue doc ("qa_gates.py v2 + pipeline v5 adoption") — reconciled first, per Hard Rule 2/8: `qa_gates.py` was already v2.1 (`states_coverage` shipped at BODY-1) and the pipeline already v6; only the favicon/meta/length checks and the standing-adoption paperwork were genuinely missing, confirmed by pre-batch grep (0 hits for "favicon"/"meta_essentials" in the pre-batch file).

**What shipped:** `qa_gates.py` v2.1→v3 (1165→1672 lines): `favicon` (per-file `<link rel="icon">` presence + a CORRECT relative href to the ratified LOGO-2 asset, realm-gated required for site/brand/console/store, skip-PASS for mobile/print/templates), `meta_essentials` (charset+title+lang always, viewport required only site/mobile, PLUS lang-{code} directory consistency incl. `dir="rtl"` for `ar`), and `lang_length` (one harness, two dispatch modes: templates/sms|ussd recomputes the GSM-7/UCS-2 segment law extended from audit_w51.py's 2-script table to the full 10-script canon table; any lang-{code} directory runs an expansion-ratio/overflow-risk audit ported from `apply1_expansion_audit.py`, generalized to any on-disk lang pair). All 3 new checks are excluded from `DEFAULT_CHECKS` and — stricter than `states_coverage` — never auto-join under ANY `--profile`; they run only when named explicitly in `--checks`. `audit_w51.py` left byte-untouched below a new annotation comment pointing to qa_gates.py as the new single source of truth. `brand/BRAND-028-web-qa-automation.html` gets a v3 section (check table, realm-gate table, backward-compat law, standing-adoption note citing Q19) + canon-wide RUN RESULTS. `15_PIPELINE_PLAYBOOK.md` tool line bumped v2.1→v3 + a new BUILD AGENT protocol line naming the 3 checks as part of the standard regression sweep.

**1. The detector itself — adversarially fixture-tested, not trusted.** Wrote synthetic HTML fixtures at `/tmp` and called all 3 new check functions directly (not via the CLI, to isolate logic from argument parsing). `favicon`: correct-href file PASSes, wrong-href FAILs, missing-link FAILs, non-gated realm (mobile) skip-PASSes even with zero favicon markup — all 4 behave exactly as designed. `meta_essentials`: a `lang="ar"` file WITHOUT `dir="rtl"` correctly FAILs ("lang-ar is an RTL directory but dir=rtl missing"); a file in `lang-bn/` carrying `lang="mr"` correctly FAILs (directory/lang mismatch); correct files of both kinds PASS; realm-gating for `viewport` independently confirmed honest against real `templates/email` behavior (`VIEWPORT_REQUIRED_PROFILES = {site, mobile}` only, matching `PROFILE_DEFS`). `lang_length`: hand-computed the GSM-7 160/153 and UCS-2 70/67 segment-law boundaries at the exact 160/161 and 70/71 character edges — both match the check's own arithmetic exactly; fed it a real SMS bubble (`SMS-001-critical-set.html`, 12 bubbles) and reproduced all 12 recomputed charset/chars/segments values by hand; a synthetic wrong-badge SMS bubble and a synthetic wrong-count USSD block were both correctly caught as DEFECTs. Confirmed `lang_length`'s segment-law constants are byte-identical to `audit_w51.py`'s own `is_ucs2()`/`compute()` (a genuine port, extended from 2 scripts to 10, not a fork with drifted constants) — real GSM-7/UCS-2 arithmetic verified identical in both files.

**2. Backward compatibility — reproduced on 3 realms, matched against the HAND-2 regression baseline.** Ran the exact v2.1-era command lines (`brand/` bare, `site/ --profile site`, `web-screens/ --profile console`) and confirmed byte-identical `checks run:` lines to the builder's claims (10 checks for brand/site, 11 for console incl. `states_coverage`'s pre-existing auto-join — zero new v3 check names leaked anywhere). Ran the full default-checks regression sweep on `web-screens/ --profile console --recursive --expect-files 440`: **3,521 PASS / exactly 6 FAIL, the identical 2-file signature HAND-2's own recorded baseline documented** (`W128-tenant-listings-bulk.html` states_coverage gap, `W340-gov-dbt-batch.html` 5 law_numbering rows) — zero new failures, regression proof holds byte-for-byte against the prior batch's own sweep.

**3. The 3 debt findings — spot-verified, and one genuine detector bug found underneath them (Hard Rule 10).** **Favicon 0/508:** direct grep for literal (unescaped) `<link rel="icon">`/`<link rel="shortcut icon">` tags across `site/`+`brand/`+`web-screens/` (508 files: 29+39+440) returns **zero** real hits — confirmed honest, not a detector artifact (the one loose-grep hit found was HTML-escaped prose inside BRAND-028 itself, describing the check, not a rendered tag). The causal claim ("LOGO-2 wired favicon into the 20 live `krishi-verse_website/` pages only") independently confirmed: those 20 live pages DO carry real favicon links (20/20), while 2 sampled design-canon files (SITE-000, SITE-016) show zero in their `<head>` — SITE-016's own grounding comment even documents LOGO-2 wiring the live site's `assets/` folder, not canon. **USSD-003…006 badge drift:** independently recomputed all 26 blocks across the 4 files by hand — 24 are genuine defects (2 in USSD-005 already clean); real, correctly NOT mass-fixed at this scale in an S batch. **The original "2–4 characters off" characterization was itself imprecise** (true range 1–3 chars concat-method / flat +5 space-method) — a small reporting defect, fixed in both `hand3_report.md` and `BRAND-028` with a dated QA annotation. **lang-{code} expansion overflow — a REAL DETECTOR BUG found underneath, not just content debt:** running the check on 2 lang dirs (mr, bn) reproduced the reported W530 chat-preview overflow pattern — but digging into why `screens/lang-ml/`+`screens/lang-or/` showed only 1/15 FAIL each (suspiciously low vs. their siblings) found that `lang_length`'s expansion-mode helper (`extract_lang_len_elements()`) stripped `<style>`/`<script>` blocks BEFORE stripping HTML comments — since this canon's grounding comments routinely mention "`<style>`" as prose (e.g. "scoped to this file's own `<style>` block only"), the non-greedy strip regex matched from that in-comment mention to the real closing tag, deleting the comment's own closing `-->` and causing Python's `html.parser` to silently treat the entire rest of the file as one unclosed comment — a FALSE zero-element result, not a genuine translation finding. **This is a regression, not a novel bug**: `Design_Program/specs_v3/apply5_report.md` §4 already found and fixed this exact defect for the apply5 expansion-audit scripts ("future batches should inherit the apply5 versions") — the qa_gates v3 port inherited the pre-fix `apply1_expansion_audit.py` order instead. Verified blast radius: **31 files** were silently returning 0/truncated elements (14/15 in `lang-ml`, 14/15 in `lang-or`, 2/15 in `lang-kn`, plus `web-screens/lang-ar/W128` — the exact file originally characterized as "a 0-element-matched structural mismatch," itself a symptom of this bug, not a real finding). **Fixed directly in `qa_gates.py`** (comments now strip first, dated annotation at the function, per Hard Rule 10 — fix the detector) and re-ran the full canon-wide sweep: true numbers are materially higher (51 of 151 lang-dir files flagged, not 26; **all 11 of 11 directories** show real signal, not "9 of 11"; the W530 src=18/translated=14 pattern is confirmed in 9 of 10 mobile languages, not 8 — `ar`'s own translated markup has 19 elements so never fit that specific pattern). Both `hand3_report.md` (QA ADDENDUM section) and `BRAND-028` (corrected RUN RESULTS cards/table + a dated QA-correction doctrine block) now carry the true numbers.

**4. BRAND-028 — v3 section accurate against argparse reality; Q19 cite byte-checked.** Check names/realm-gate tables cross-checked against `CHECK_REGISTRY`/`FAVICON_REQUIRED_PROFILES`/`VIEWPORT_REQUIRED_PROFILES` in code: accurate. Q19's quoted decision-register line ("qa_gates standing adoption (endorsed — pipeline v5)") byte-matched against `12_G0-2_DECISION_REGISTER.md` line 44 — exact, and its ordinal position (5th of 6 clauses in the Q15–Q20 routed batch) confirmed = Q19. **Demos-are-deployments check:** BRAND-028 itself was run through the new checks — it correctly FAILS `favicon` (it's one of the 39 `brand/` files already folded into the honest 0/508 aggregate, no separate disclosure needed) and PASSes `meta_essentials`; no undisclosed exemption found. Tag balance re-verified PASS after this pass's own edits.

**5. Playbook edits — accurate.** `15_PIPELINE_PLAYBOOK.md`'s "never auto-join under any `--profile`" claim for the 3 new checks independently verified against `DEFAULT_CHECKS`'s exclusion list and `PROFILE_DEFS` (neither references the 3 new check names) — matches code exactly.

**INDEPENDENT QA VERDICT (2026-07-22, Review-Agent — HAND-3): PASS, with 1 real detector bug found + fixed (not builder-blocking; a QA-side catch).** Every claim across all 5 governing checks (adversarial detector fixture-testing, backward-compat + regression-baseline reproduction, all 3 debt findings, BRAND-028 accuracy, playbook accuracy) was independently re-derived — own `/tmp` fixtures, own hand-computed segment-law arithmetic, own grep censuses, own recomputed sweeps — never trusted from the builder's own tooling or report prose. **The 3 new check functions are correctly implemented and match every adversarial case tested.** **1 real regression-class detector bug found and fixed in `qa_gates.py` itself** (comment/style strip order in `lang_length`'s expansion mode, re-introducing a bug `apply5_report.md` had already fixed and flagged for future batches to inherit) — 31 files were silently under-reported; fixed with a dated annotation, canon-wide numbers recomputed and corrected in both `hand3_report.md` and `BRAND-028`. **2 small reporting-precision defects found + fixed** (USSD drift range "2–4"→correct "1–3/flat+5"; directory-fail-count "9 of 11"→correct "11 of 11"). Backward compatibility and the HAND-2 regression baseline both reproduced byte-for-byte. Zero P0 in the shipped tool logic itself (the bug found was in a NEW v3 check's own helper, caught before it could poison a future batch's findings — exactly the failure mode Hard Rule 10 exists to prevent). Portal's historical "PROPOSED for adoption" line flipped to ADOPTED, citing Q19 + this batch. **PHASE O remains OPEN.** Next: **HAND-4 (redline convention)** — the last queued Phase O batch — or the founder's own open list. Known debt spawned by this batch (correctly NOT mass-fixed in an S batch): favicon canon-pass (508 files, needs a dedicated FAVICON-CANON-1 scripted follow-up), USSD-003…006 badge-drift correction (4 files, content-only), lang-dir overflow content review (11 directories, human-confirmation-required per the tool's own honest positional-pairing caveat).

## HAND-4 — redline / spec-extraction doctrine: PATH B ratified (standalone-HTML-IS-the-redline), BRAND-039 decision + doctrine + annotation register, first canon page to pass favicon+meta_essentials, pipeline v6 — THE FINAL QUEUED DESIGN-PROGRAM BATCH

**Builder:** single-pass decision/doctrine batch (`Design_Program/specs_v3/spec_hand4.md` + `hand4_report.md`). Founder order: "define the per-screen dev-spec annotation convention or confirm the standalone-HTML-is-the-redline doctrine explicitly." No screens built — a decision batch, not a mass-build.

**What shipped:** `brand/BRAND-039-redline-doctrine.html` — Exhibit E decision record (PATH B, standalone-HTML-IS-the-redline, RATIFIED under the founder's standing LOGO-1 delegation, override open), the cost-math case (1,177-file redline authoring vs. zero-cost devtools inspection; Q42 sprite-drift precedent generalized to redline scale), a live 5-question extraction proof against `web-screens/W001-admin-dashboard.html`, and a formal annotation register (6 marker types + syntax rules). Cross-referenced at `designer_pack/07-developer-handoff.md` new §11 and one `15_PIPELINE_PLAYBOOK.md` line.

**Independent QA (2026-07-22) — verdict: PASS, with 2 real doctrine-honesty gaps found + fixed in place (dated annotations, not builder-blocking).**

1. **The decision, re-weighed independently: sound.** Recounted the 1,177-file population from scratch (`546+439+83+23+18+29+39`, own `find` commands) — exact match. Q42 citation byte-verified against `12_G0-2_DECISION_REGISTER.md` line 17 — verbatim. Cost asymmetry (200+ build hours vs. ~3 edits) and the two-sources-of-truth drift risk are real and not overstated; PATH A was never seriously competitive. Exhibit E is complete (decision / decision-basis / date 2026-07-22 / founder-override-open, matching BRAND-029's own signoff markup).
2. **Doctrine honesty — 1 real overclaim found + fixed.** The draft's "literal values legitimately exist in exactly two places" (content; CSS-gradient/hex-table exceptions) was incomplete. Grep-census this pass: **198 files / 1,003 occurrences** of literal hex in `fill=`/`stroke=`/`stop-color=` SVG presentation attributes (frozen logo/badge/icon artwork, e.g. the LOGO-4.1 "Powered by Krishi Verse" badge) — entirely outside `hex_audit`'s v2 CSS-only scope (`<style>`/`style=""` contexts only), so neither counted nor flagged, and not one of the doctrine's named carve-outs. Confirmed by spot-checking 2 console screens (`web-screens/W117-tenant-dashboard.html`, `web-screens/W123-tenant-listings.html`) — both carry `stop-color="#1e6f3f"`/`stroke="#f9c45a"` with zero token trace available. **Fixed:** BRAND-039 corrected in place to a third named carve-out ("Frozen brand/icon SVG artwork") with the exact grep and count, `[QA-FIX HAND-4 2026-07-22]` annotated — the artwork itself is correctly ratified (LOGO-1…6), the doctrine's completeness claim was not.
3. **Annotation register — 1 real gap found + fixed, 1 near-fork flagged as debt.** The register's own grep (`QA-STATES-EXEMPT|QA-FIX|QA-LAW-EXTERNAL|QA-HEX-TABLE-DECLARED|DARK-READY|SENIOR-READY|TABLET-READY|APPLIED at`) missed `"<asset> retired at <BATCH>"` — an actual, larger, pre-existing HTML-comment marker convention (**457 real comment occurrences**, not table prose, across web-screens/collateral/site — e.g. `<!-- brand glyph: look-alike retired at LOGO-3.1, geometry = ... (BRAND-029 Exhibit E) -->`), bigger by volume than several markers the register did include. **Fixed:** added as a 7th reg-card with real byte-quoted examples, dated `[QA-FIX HAND-4 2026-07-22]`. Also found `brand/BRAND-028-web-qa-automation.html` carries 4 occurrences of `[QA-CORRECTED <date>, was "..."]` — functionally a duplicate of `QA-FIX` under a different tag, introduced at the HAND-3 QA pass, missed by this batch's own vocabulary grep — exactly the vocabulary-fork risk this register exists to prevent, already having happened once undetected. Not folded into BRAND-028 (outside this batch's edit scope, bigger blast radius than a doctrine-page fix) — flagged as known debt in a new register callout instead.
4. **Extraction proof — independently redone on a different tenant-console screen (`web-screens/W117-tenant-dashboard.html`), 5 fresh questions, own timing:** (1) KPI card padding → 20px, `.kvw-card{padding:var(--space-5)}` → `--space-5:1.25rem`, ~10s; (2) "New listing" primary button → `var(--color-primary-600)` → `#1e6f3f`, ~10s; (3) "GMV this month" KPI value font → `var(--font-display)` → Fraunces/Hind/Georgia stack, ~10s; (4) notification bell tap target → `.kvw-topbar-iconbtn` 36×36px via `--web-control-h`, same DELTA-001 dense-console exception as W001, ~40s disclosed 2-file lookup; (5) **"Powered by Krishi Verse" badge color → NOT resolvable via any token trace at all** — literal `stop-color="#1e6f3f"`/`stroke="#f9c45a"` SVG attributes, the exact gap found in finding #2 above. Net: 4/5 clean via the cascade (matching W001's own ratio), 1/5 genuinely outside the doctrine's stated taxonomy — a materially useful independent redo, not a repeat of the builder's own result.
5. **W001's bell-36px claim, independently judged: genuinely DELTA-001's ratified exception, not a defect wearing one's clothes.** Read `13_G0-3_DELTA_REGISTER.md` directly: DELTA-001 is listed among items with status **flipped from de-facto to FORMALLY CLOSED**, and the v1.3 changelog line (verbatim, register line 25) reads "§4/§6 addendum per DELTA-001 residual: console density adaptation requires pointer/hover media-query detection (engineering-owed)" — confirms ratified canon with a real, still-open engineering residual, exactly as `07-developer-handoff.md` §10 and BRAND-039 state. Not a defect.
6. **BRAND-039 mechanics — independently re-run, not trusted from the report.** Own `qa_gates.py` sweep of `brand/` (`--profile brand --checks no_script,currency_glyphs,physical_props,tag_balance,law_numbering,media_queries,hex_audit,pii_patterns,favicon,meta_essentials,lang_length`, 40 files): **41 total FAILs both before and after this pass's own doctrine edits** (39 favicon + 2 physical_props — BRAND-017/BRAND-028, pre-existing, unrelated), **BRAND-039 clean on all 11 named checks including favicon and meta_essentials** — reproduced exactly, including post-fix (tag balance still `balanced`, hex_audit still `0 unrecognized` after the added prose-hex citations, correctly outside v2's CSS-only scope like the page's pre-existing citations). **"First canon page" claim reconciled honest:** `grep -rl 'rel="icon"'` across the whole design-system canon returns only `brand/BRAND-028` (prose mention, not a live tag) and `brand/BRAND-039` (real tag) — zero elsewhere, including `site/` (still 0/29, matching HAND-3's baseline). The live, separately-deployed `krishi-verse_website/` (20 pages, LOGO-2) DOES carry real favicons — but that tree is not canon (`Krishi_Verse_Design_System/site/` is), so the doctrine's own scoping ("first page in ANY realm's canon") is accurate, not overreaching into the live site.
7. **Cross-refs — accurate.** `designer_pack/07-developer-handoff.md` §11 and the one `15_PIPELINE_PLAYBOOK.md` line both byte-checked against BRAND-039's actual content; no drift found.
8. **Regression — reproduced, zero new failures**, matching the report's claimed 41-FAIL baseline exactly (39 pre-existing brand/ files unchanged, BRAND-039 itself is the only new file and it's clean).

**PHASE O IS NOW CLOSED — the design-program queue is empty.** Founder-order backlog: the 8 reserved G0-2 questions, contract v1.3 signature, G0-R3 taste-calls; HAND-3 debt carried forward unresolved (favicon 508-file canon pass, USSD-003…006 badge drift, lang-dir expansion overflow across 11 directories); standing external items (counsel×14 COUNSEL-PENDING sockets, `{{vendor proof}}` items, native-speaker/type reviews for 9 machine-draft languages + hi/gu wordmarks). Full verdict recorded here; `DP2_TRACKER.md` STATE block supersedes HAND-3's NEXT ACTION.

## CLOSE-1 — design-debt closure sweep: favicon canon mass-pass (508/508 inserted, 509/509 PASS), USSD-003…006 badge drift closed (0/26 defects remain), BRAND-026's 2 remaining rows shipped as real opt-in senior-mode CSS, QA-CORRECTED sanctioned as a QA-FIX alias, pipeline v6

**Builder:** single-pass closure batch (`Design_Program/specs_v3/spec_close1.md` + `close1_report.md`). Founder order: close 4 previously-flagged known-debt items named across `hand2_report.md`/`hand3_report.md`/`hand4_report.md`/`apply8_report.md` — favicon (HAND-3), USSD badge drift (HAND-3), BRAND-026's 2 PROPOSED rows (APPLY-8), and the `QA-CORRECTED`/`QA-FIX` near-fork (HAND-4).

**What shipped:** (1) `close1_favicon.py` (farmer root) — census→dry-run→reconcile→execute→byte-log→idempotency per Hard Rule 9, inserting `<link rel="icon" href="{relpath}" type="image/svg+xml">` immediately after each file's `<title>` line across `site/`(29)+`brand/`(40)+`web-screens/`(440, incl. `lang-ar/`) = 508 insertions, BRAND-039 (HAND-4) the sole pre-existing PASS; byte-log `close1_favicon_byte_log.json` carries sha256 before/after for all 508; `screens/`(mobile) and `templates/` correctly left SKIP-gated and untouched, per the check's own realm gates. (2) USSD-003…006 badge recount: all 26 blocks recomputed via `qa_gates.lang_length_ussd_rows()` directly (the real detector), 24 corrected across their 3-4 redundant mentions (badge span, `.meta` caption, segment-class table row, self-test parenthetical), 2 already matching the concat method left byte-unchanged, each fix annotated `[QA-FIX CLOSE-1 2026-07-22: old→new]`. (3) `system/screen.css` gains 2 new opt-in hooks scoped inside `.screen.senior-mode` — `.nav-secondary{display:none}` and `.icon-only-action[aria-label]::after{content:attr(aria-label)}` — additive-only, zero canon consumer yet; `brand/BRAND-026-senior-mode.html`'s "Simplified nav"/"Always-visible labels" rows flip PROPOSED→`APPLIED — CLOSE-1` with conservative-reading interpretation trails, self-test row (a) recounted from final markup. (4) Hygiene: `QA-CORRECTED` formally sanctioned as a `QA-FIX` alias in BRAND-039's own register (per its own syntax rule 4), the "known near-fork" callout flipped from "flagged not fixed" to "CLOSED at CLOSE-1"; `kvw-banner`(4)/`kvw-btn-quiet`(3) correctly left below HAND-2's 5-use threshold; `kvw-sidebar-foot`'s 244-file body-content cleanup correctly NOT piggybacked onto the favicon script's `<head>`-only mechanism.

**Independent QA (2026-07-22) — verdict: PASS, with 1 real hygiene defect found + fixed (dated, not builder-blocking).**

1. **Favicon — re-run independently, not trusted from the builder's own script.** `qa_gates.py --checks favicon` (named explicitly, per the v3 rule that it never auto-joins any `--profile`) re-run fresh on all 3 realms: `site --profile site` 29/29 PASS, `brand --profile brand` 40/40 PASS, `web-screens --profile console --recursive` 440/440 PASS — **509/509 canon-wide**, matching the claim exactly. A duplicate-insertion scan (`grep -c '<link rel="icon"' <file>` across all 509) confirms exactly one real tag per file; a first, naive quoted-string grep flagged BRAND-028/BRAND-039 at 3 hits each, but the extra hits are HTML-escaped prose (`&lt;link rel="icon"&gt;`) inside table cells describing the check itself, not live tags — caught and discarded as a false positive of the *scan*, not a defect of the *batch* (an instructive echo of the same false-positive class the builder's own report already flagged for its ad-hoc tag-balance sampler). `screens/` and `templates/` independently confirmed to carry zero `<link rel="icon">` occurrences canon-wide — correctly untouched, not exceeded. Path correctness sampled at 6 depths (`site/SITE-000`, `site/SITE-028`, `brand/BRAND-000`, `brand/BRAND-039`, `web-screens/W001`, `web-screens/lang-ar/W128`) — every href independently resolved via `test -e` to the real `designer_pack/assets/logo/exports/favicon.svg`. Byte-log spot-checked 3 entries (first/middle/last of 508) — sha256 and byte-count of the logged `sha256_after`/`bytes_after` match the live file exactly, no drift between the log and disk. Tag-balance sampled 5 files (2 favicon-touched + BRAND-026 + BRAND-039 + W128) — all PASS.
2. **Regression — the claimed pre-existing failure sets reproduced exactly, unrelated to the favicon edit.** A full named-check sweep (`favicon,meta_essentials,lang_length,hex_audit,tag_balance,no_script,physical_props[,states_coverage,law_numbering]`) on all 3 realms reproduces: site 29/29 `hex_audit` FAIL (`#0a3d1f`/`#15803d`, canon-wide site-realm debt, zero relation to `<head>`), brand 2/2 `physical_props` FAIL (BRAND-017/BRAND-028, pre-existing per HAND-4's own dogfood note), web-screens 7 FAIL lines (2 on W128 — `lang_length`+`states_coverage` — plus 5 `law_numbering` rows on W340) — all matching the report's claimed baseline byte-for-byte. Mechanically consistent with the byte-diff proof that every touched file's only change is the single-line `<link>` insertion.
3. **USSD — recomputed from the real detector, not hand-approximated.** Ran `qa_gates.lang_length_ussd_rows()` directly against all 4 files: **0/26 defects remain**, and the 2 blocks the report says were already clean (USSD-005's N-2.3=90, N-2.3.3.1=104) independently confirmed to match the concat method exactly (`real(concat)==claimed`), as claimed — not merely asserted. Tag balance re-verified PASS on all 4 files.
4. **1 real defect found and fixed: incomplete `QA-FIX` dating.** BRAND-039's own binding syntax rule for the marker (`<!-- QA-FIX <BATCH> <YYYY-MM-DD>: <what was wrong> — <what was corrected> -->`) requires the date on every occurrence — but 22 of the 30 total `QA-FIX CLOSE-1` annotations across the 4 USSD files were abbreviated to `[QA-FIX CLOSE-1: N→M]`, silently dropping the date (only the first-per-file and self-test-per-file annotations carried it). Dated in place to `2026-07-22` across all 22; tag-balance and the `lang_length_ussd_rows()` detector both re-verified clean post-fix (still 0/26 defects, no regression from the edit).
5. **Senior-mode hooks — read in full, scoping and absence claims both independently reproduced.** Both new rules confirmed, by direct read, scoped only inside `.screen.senior-mode` (never bare). A subject-grep for `class="...nav-secondary..."` / `class="...icon-only-action..."` (not the label) across `screens/`, `web-screens/`, `site/`, `brand/`, `templates/`, `collateral/`, `documents/` returns zero real markup consumers — matching the "ships the mechanism, does not force-retrofit" claim exactly (the one file-level hit for "nav-secondary" is BRAND-026's own prose table cell, not a class attribute). `system/screen.css`'s brace count independently recounted at 44/44. A full `screens/` (546-file, non-recursive — the correct scope for the "344/546" baseline the report itself cites) `--profile mobile` sweep reproduces `physical_props` 344 FAIL / `tag_balance` 0 FAIL / `no_script` 0 FAIL exactly, byte-identical in composition to APPLY-8's own documented baseline — zero new failures from the 2 new senior-mode rule blocks.
6. **BRAND-026's conservative reading, independently judged sound against its own spec text.** Both remaining rows ("max ~3 primary actions/decisions," "every actionable control carries a visible label") are content-authoring guidelines, not literal CSS selectors — a stylesheet has no structural way to detect "which nav item is secondary" or "which control is icon-only" without an author-applied hook, so shipping two opt-in classes rather than inventing an unrequested global rule (e.g. auto-hiding the 4th+ nav item, which would risk hiding a primary destination — a Rule Zero violation) is the correct, non-dodging call, not a builder side-step of something genuinely implementable. Self-test row (a)'s 5/5 recount (2 `APPLIED — APPLY-8`, 2 `APPLIED — CLOSE-1`, 1 still `PROPOSED §17`) independently matches the actual 5 table rows exactly.
7. **Hygiene — both register claims and both count claims independently reproduced.** BRAND-039's new `QA-CORRECTED` reg-card confirmed to follow its own syntax rule 4 (tag + syntax recorded in the very batch that formalizes it, with a real byte-quoted example); the "known near-fork" callout confirmed flipped from "flagged not fixed" to "CLOSED at CLOSE-1"; BRAND-028's 4 existing `QA-CORRECTED` occurrences confirmed byte-unchanged. `kvw-banner`/`kvw-btn-quiet` real-usage counts (4/3, excluding the component-library definition page which is not a production consumer) independently reproduced — correctly below HAND-2's 5-use promotion threshold; `kvw-sidebar-foot`'s ~244-file scope (243 `web-screens/` + 1 `lang-ar/`, excluding library/doc-mention pages) independently confirmed real and correctly left untouched — its body-content per-usage cleanup shares no mechanism with the favicon script's `<head>`-only anchor.

Zero P0/P1 beyond the 1 dated fix above. **THE FAVICON AND USSD DEBT HAND-3 FLAGGED IS NOW CLOSED**; the senior-mode mechanism gains its 2 remaining rows as real, additive, opt-in CSS; the annotation-vocabulary near-fork HAND-4 flagged is resolved rather than left open. **§17/NEXT**: **CLOSE-2 — lang-ur pilot** (the last outstanding design promise) — then the founder's own reserved decisions ("start the decisions": 8 reserved G0-2 questions, contract v1.3 signature, G0-R3 taste-calls; standing external items unchanged). `DP2_TRACKER.md` STATE block supersedes HAND-4's own NEXT ACTION.

## CLOSE-2 — lang-ur Urdu RTL pilot: THE LAST PROMISED LANGUAGE IS BUILT (11 language directories + ar console proof), BRAND-018 flips, pipeline v6

**Builder:** relaunched batch (`Design_Program/specs_v3/spec_close2.md` + `close2_report.md`) — original agent died mid-self-test; the relaunch's Phase 0 forensics found the 15 files themselves genuinely complete and undamaged (a clean death, no partial-edit debris), so it finished only the unfinished verification/reporting layer.

**What shipped:** `screens/lang-ur/` — 15 files, filenames byte-identical to `screens/lang-ar/`'s list, `dir="rtl" lang="ur"` on all 15, `ur-IN` candidate locale (🇮🇳, deliberate inversion from ar's 🇦🇪), `[lang="ur"]{font-family:'Noto Nastaliq Urdu',...;line-height:1.9}` scoped per-file (zero `system/*.css` additions), 108 `.bidi` isolations, 0 Arabic-Indic + 0 Urdu digits, 0 kasheeda, TS-003 Latin glossary held. BRAND-018 flips: font availability VERIFIED — CLOSE-2 (live `fonts.googleapis.com` query), line-height APPLIED (15/15), font-size-floor rows correctly left PROPOSED (out of single-language scope). `{{unit-term review}}` flag completed to 12/13 quintal-content files at relaunch (5 files newly flagged, dated `[BUILD-FIX CLOSE-2 relaunch]`). Expansion/template-length audits at farmer root (relocated from `specs_v3/` per playbook), `qa_gates.py`'s `lang_length` detector gap (misclassifying `.md-chip` watermarks as translatable elements) flagged as known debt, not fixed by the builder (correctly deferred — Hard Rule 10 detector-fixes are QA's job in this pipeline).

**Independent QA (2026-07-22) — verdict: PASS, with 4 real defects found + fixed (dated, not builder-blocking), 1 known-debt item found already resolved and reclassified from debt to CLOSED.**

1. **Two-lives scan — clean.** Every claimed count recounted at the element level, not trusted from the builder's report: exactly 1 real `.md-banner-ur` div per file (15/15; the "3 per file" grep figure is 1 comment mention + 1 CSS rule + 1 real div, not 3 real banners — no double-insertion); `.md-chip` element counts recounted per file (3–7 real spans, matching each file's own header-comment block enumeration with one exception noted below); `{{unit-term review}}` recounted at exactly 1 occurrence in each of the 5 relaunch-touched files (19/521/524/529/530) — no duplicates; `01-welcome.html`/`59-farmer-payout-history.html` independently confirmed to have zero rendered quintal content (correctly unflagged, not missed); tag balance 15/15 PASS both before and after this pass's own fixes.
2. **A real undocumented-but-legitimate finding: 4 files (`12-my-listings`, `52-farmer-mandi-prices`, `56-farmer-orders`, `530-farmer-krishimitra-home`) carry in-file `QA-FIX (CLOSE-2 self-audit/post-audit)` comments restoring APPLY-9's tablet two-pane (`screen-split`/`screen-split-body`/`screen-split-list`/`screen-split-detail`) wrapper, found dropped from the initial ur draft by the build agent's own post-audit — genuinely restored 1:1 against source in structure (verified element-for-element against the English source in all 4), but this restoration work is nowhere mentioned in `close2_report.md`'s findings tables — a real reporting-completeness gap, noted here since the underlying fix itself is sound. **1 defect found in the restoration itself + fixed:** `12-my-listings.html`'s restored `.screen-split-detail` pane left its `aria-label` untranslated (English), inconsistent with the other 3 files' fully-Urdu labels — fixed in place, dated `[QA-FIX CLOSE-2 QA, 2026-07-22]`. **Also newly found:** `lang-ar/`'s own copies of these same 4 files carry **zero** `screen-split` structure at all (confirmed by direct grep — source carries 4 real elements, ar carries 0) — a real, larger, pre-existing gap in the *earlier* ar batch that ur's own self-audit caught and fixed for ur but never cross-checked against ar the way the 440 mismatch was. Flagged here as shared debt for a future ar-touch-up batch (out of this batch's scope to backfill 4 files' worth of translated tablet panes).
3. **Urdu language quality — sampled 4 files deep (`52-farmer-mandi-prices`, `440-farmer-dispute-respond`, `09-farmer-home`, `12-my-listings`), confirmed genuine.** Mechanically proved not-Arabic-reused: grepped Urdu-specific letters ے/ڑ/ٹ/ں (absent from Arabic script entirely) — present across all but one ur file (`01-welcome` has ٹ/ں but 0 ڑ, expected for short content), **zero** occurrences of any of the four in all 15 `lang-ar/` files, confirming genuine script differentiation, not a copy. Direct block diff of the same screen/line against `lang-ar/` (e.g. 440's "Offer a remedy": ar "تقديم تعويض" vs ur "معاوضہ پیش کریں" — different vocabulary, different word order) confirms genuinely different languages, not reused text. Read blocks are fluent, grammatically sound, formal (aap-register) Urdu — natural SOV constructions, correct verb agreement, plausible agri vocabulary (کوئنٹل/quintal compounds, crop names, grade terms) — not Hindi-transliterated. Register/native-review-pending honesty disclosure confirmed present and accurate.
4. **Mechanics — every count independently recomputed, not trusted from the builder's tooling.** Digit scans (own regex, both `٠-٩` and `۰-۹` ranges): 0/0 across all 15, confirmed. Kasheeda (`ـ`): 0/15, confirmed. `.bidi` element count: own count summed to exactly **108**, matching the claim exactly. `lang="ur"`/`dir="rtl"`: 15/15. Font override: present in all 15 (`grep -l`), and confirmed absent from `system/*.css` (0 hits). `qa_gates.py --profile mobile` regression sweep reproduced byte-identically against `lang-ar/`'s own copies of the same 15 source files — same 3 `hex_audit` FAILs (19-wallet-home/52-mandi/521-voice), same 5 `physical_props` FAILs (01/09/19/52/59) — zero new failures.
5. **The 440 structural mismatch — confirmed pre-existing in `lang-ar` too, trivially fixable in both, fixed in both.** Traced to the source's `.fdr-optional-badge` class being dropped in favor of a duplicated inline style in both language translations (byte-identical pattern in `lang-ar/440` and `lang-ur/440`) — this class-drop is what caused `qa_gates.py`'s `lang_length` (and the standalone audit script) to mis-tokenize the row (src=8/translated=7), producing the reported 2.875× "Optional" ratio artifact. Fixed by restoring `class="fdr-optional-badge"` (dropping the redundant inline duplicate) in **both** files, dated `[QA-FIX CLOSE-2 QA, 2026-07-22]`; post-fix, both files show clean 8/8 element-count parity and 0 overflow flags on this row. Recomputed `close2_ur_expansion_audit.py` post-fix: 193 pairs/16 flags (was 192/17 — the fix resolved one bogus structural-mismatch-driven flag and correctly re-paired one more element).
6. **The `lang_length` detector gap — found already CLOSED, not open debt.** `qa_gates.py` already carries an `LL_MD_CHIP_RE` guard (`\bmd-chip\b`, checked before the `-chip\b` clause) dated `QA-FIX CLOSE-2 2026-07-22`, matching exactly the fix `close2_report.md` still describes as "known debt, not fixed this batch" — the report is stale relative to the tool's current state. Verified the fix is correct and complete: re-ran `--checks lang_length` on both `lang-ur/` and `lang-ar/` post-fix — results are now sane and positionally aligned (real overflow flags like "Reject"→"مسترد کریں" ratio=1.67, "Home"→"الرئيسية" ratio=2.00 on ar, genuine structural mismatches on ar's un-restored screen-split files), no more `.md-chip`-induced garbage pairings. Reclassifying this from "known debt" to CLOSED in this record.
7. **Audits — spot-recomputed independently, all match.** SMS-001 OTP: 99 chars, UCS-2, `ceil(99/67)=2` segments — hand-verified against the 70-char single-segment / 67-char per-part UCS-2 limits, matches exactly. EM-002 (37/70 chars) and PU-001 (24/111 chars) both independently recomputed, matching exactly. 6 expansion-ratio pairs spot-checked by hand (Stats 2.40×, Reject 1.67×, Qtl badges 1.5–1.6×, Add Money/Send 1.44–1.5×) all reproduced exactly via direct source-vs-ur text length math.
8. **`lang-ar/` baseline — confirmed untouched except the 1 shared 440 fix disclosed above** (item 5); all other 14 files byte-unchanged (spot-checked via the regression sweep's identical failure-set reproduction).

Zero P0 beyond the 4 dated QA-fixes above (1 aria-label translation, 2×440 class restoration, 0 net-new detector work since the detector fix was already present and verified sound). **THE LAST PROMISED LANGUAGE IS BUILT — 11 `screens/lang-{code}/` directories, independently recounted (`ar/as/bn/kn/ml/mr/or/pa/ta/te/ur`), plus the `web-screens/lang-ar/` console proof — all machine-draft pending native review, Nastaliq face PROPOSED pending type review.** **§17/NEXT: Design promises: ALL KEPT. Next: founder decision sitting ("start the decisions" — 11 items) then Development Program.** `DP2_TRACKER.md` STATE block supersedes CLOSE-1's own NEXT ACTION.

## G0-4 — founder decision sitting (2026-07-22): all 11 open items PERSONALLY DECIDED by the founder (Dev), direct — FOUNDER DECISION DEBT = ZERO

**Sitting, not a build batch.** This agent acted as both builder and records agent for this sitting (G0-2's own precedent). No screens built; this is a decision/records/cascade sitting per `Design_Program/15_PIPELINE_PLAYBOOK.md`'s HARD RULES.

**What the founder decided, verbatim, 2026-07-22 (recorded authority: "Founder, direct, 2026-07-22" on every item):**

2. **Q24/DELTA-059 channel fan-out** — ROUTINE tier = ONE primary channel per farmer preference + auto-fallback to SMS on non-delivery; money/security tiers stay multi-channel.

4. **Q28 JP-KR** — FRAMEWORK-ONLY until entry nears; build packs via `16_COUNTRY_PACK_RECIPE.md` at that time.
5. **Q26 van livery** — UNIFY BILINGUAL, Gujarati + English on ALL van panels.
6. **G0-R3a W273 gold hero CTA** — KEEP; gold ratified as the store realm's commerce accent.
7. **G0-R3b Gov blue pill** — KEEP + DOCUMENT; ratified as intentional realm differentiation.
8. **G0-R3c W207 duplicate primary CTA** — FIX; secondary action demoted to outline style.
9. **Q29 social handles** — `@krishiverse` family primary (fallbacks `@krishiverseapp`, `@krishiverseHQ`); hashtags `#KrishiVerse`/`#FromFarmToFuture`; registration is an external action, queued, availability NOT asserted.
10.–11. **Q30/contract v1.3** — SIGN AS DRAFTED: the §E changelog (`13_G0-3_DELTA_REGISTER.md`) pasted into `KRISHI_VERSE_MASTER_WORK_CONTRACT.md` with date 2026-07-22 filled in, and every correction it names applied surgically into the contract body itself this sitting.
